options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts
options.description: List of calendars.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.khal.addresses
options.description:
Email addresses to be associated with this account. Used to check the
participation status ("PARTSTAT"), refer to khal documentation.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.khal.color
options.description:
Color in which events in this calendar are displayed.
For instance 'light green' or an RGB color '#ff0000'

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.khal.enable
options.description: Whether to enable khal access.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.khal.glob
options.description:
The glob expansion to be searched for events or birthdays when
type is set to discover.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.khal.priority
options.description: Priority of a calendar used for coloring (calendar with highest priority is preferred).

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.khal.readOnly
options.description: Keep khal from making any changes to this account.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.khal.type
options.description: Either a single calendar (calendar which is the default) or a directory with multiple calendars (discover).

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.local
options.description: Local configuration for the calendar.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.local.encoding
options.description:
File encoding for items, both content and file name.
Defaults to UTF-8.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.local.fileExt
options.description: The file extension to use.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.local.path
options.description: The path of the storage.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.local.type
options.description: The type of the storage.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.name
options.description:
Unique identifier of the calendar. This is set to the
attribute name of the calendar configuration.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.pimsync.enable
options.description: Whether to enable synchronization using pimsync.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.pimsync.extraLocalStorageDirectives
options.description: Extra directives that should be added under this accounts local storage directive

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.pimsync.extraPairDirectives
options.description: Extra directives that should be added under this accounts pair directive

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.pimsync.extraRemoteStorageDirectives
options.description: Extra directives that should be added under this accounts remote storage directive

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.primary
options.description:
Whether this is the primary account. Only one account may be
set as primary.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.primaryCollection
options.description:
The primary collection of the account. Required when an
account has multiple collections.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.qcal.enable
options.description: Whether to enable qcal access.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.remote
options.description: Remote configuration for the calendar.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.remote.passwordCommand
options.description: A command that prints the password to standard output.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.remote.type
options.description: The type of the storage.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.remote.url
options.description: The URL of the storage.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.remote.userName
options.description: User name for authentication.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.color
options.description: Display color of the calendar in hex

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the Thunderbird mail client for this account.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.profiles
options.description:
List of Thunderbird profiles for which this account should be
enabled. If this list is empty (the default), this account will
be enabled for all declared profiles.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.readOnly
options.description: Mark calendar as read only

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.auth
options.description: Authentication settings. The default is `basic`.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.authCert
options.type: null or string or list of string
options.description:
Either a path to a certificate with a client certificate and
the key or a list of paths to the files with them.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.clientIdCommand
options.description:
A command that prints the OAuth credentials to standard
output.

See
<https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/config.html#google>
for more information.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.clientSecretCommand
options.description:
A command that prints the OAuth credentials to standard
output.

See
<https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/config.html#google>
for more information.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.collections
options.type: null or (list of (string or list of string))
options.description: The collections to synchronize between the storages.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.conflictResolution
options.type: null or one of "remote wins", "local wins" or list of string
options.description:
What to do in case of a conflict between the storages. Either
`remote wins` or
`local wins` or
a list that contains a command to run. By default, an error
message is printed.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.enable
options.description: Whether to enable synchronization using vdirsyncer.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.itemTypes
options.description:
Kinds of items to show. The default is to show everything.
This depends on particular features of the server, the results
are not validated.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.metadata
options.description:
Metadata keys that should be synchronized when vdirsyncer
metasync is executed.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.partialSync
options.description:
What should happen if synchronization in one direction
is impossible due to one storage being read-only.
Defaults to `revert`.

See
<https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/config.html#pair-section>
for more information.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.postHook
options.description:
Command to call for each item creation and modification.
The command will be called with the path of the new/updated
file.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.timeRange
options.description:
A time range to synchronize. start and end can be any Python
expression that returns a `datetime.datetime`
object.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.timeRange.end
options.description: End of time range to show.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.timeRange.start
options.description: Start of time range to show.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.tokenFile
options.description: A file path where access tokens are stored.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.urlCommand
options.description: A command that prints the URL of the storage.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.useVcard4
options.description:
Specifies whether vdirsyncer should request vCards in version 4.0.
If set to `false` then vdirsyncer will default to version 3.0.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.userAgent
options.description:
The user agent to report to the server. Defaults to
`vdirsyncer`.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.userNameCommand
options.description: A command that prints the user name to standard output.

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.verify
options.description: Null or path to certificate to verify SSL against

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.verifyFingerprint
options.description:
Optional. SHA1 or MD5 fingerprint of the expected server certificate.

See
<https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/ssl-tutorial.html#ssl-tutorial>
for more information.

options.title: accounts.calendar.basePath
options.description:
The base directory in which to save calendars. May be a
relative path, in which case it is relative the home
directory.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts
options.description: List of contacts.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.khal.addresses
options.description:
Email addresses to be associated with this account. Used to check the
participation status ("PARTSTAT"), refer to khal documentation.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.khal.collections
options.description: VCARD collections to be searched for contact birthdays.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.khal.color
options.description:
Color in which events in this calendar are displayed.
For instance 'light green' or an RGB color '#ff0000'

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.khal.enable
options.description: Whether to enable khal access.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.khal.priority
options.description: Priority of a calendar used for coloring (calendar with highest priority is preferred).

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.khal.readOnly
options.description: Keep khal from making any changes to this account.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.khard.addressbooks
options.type: (list of string) or string convertible to it
options.description:
If provided, each item on this list will generate an
entry on khard configuration file as a separate addressbook
(vdir).

This is used for hardcoding sub-directories under the local
storage path
(accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.local.path) for khard. The
default value will set the aforementioned path as a single vdir.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.khard.enable
options.description: Whether to enable khard access.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.khard.glob
options.description:
The glob expansion to be searched for contacts when
type is set to discover.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.khard.type
options.description:
Either a single vdir located in [](#opt-accounts.contact.accounts._name_.local.path)
or multiple automatically discovered vdirs in
[](#opt-accounts.contact.accounts._name_.local.path)/[](#opt-accounts.contact.accounts._name_.khard.glob).

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.local
options.description: Local configuration for the contacts.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.local.encoding
options.description:

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.local.path
options.description: The path of the storage.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.local.type
options.description: The type of the storage.

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.name

options.title: accounts.contact.accounts.<name>.remote.userName

options.title: accounts.contact.basePath
options.description:
The base directory in which to save contacts. May be a
relative path, in which case it is relative the home
directory.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts
options.description: List of email accounts.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.address
options.description: The email address of this account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.enable
options.description: Whether to enable aerc.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.extraAccounts
options.type: attribute set of (values (null, bool, int, string, or float) or a list of values, that will be joined with a comma)
options.description:
Extra config added to the configuration section for this account in
{file}`$HOME/.config/aerc/accounts.conf`.
See {manpage}`aerc-accounts(5)`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.extraBinds
options.type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or attribute set of (values (null, bool, int, string, or float) or a list of values, that will be joined with a comma))
options.description:
Extra bindings specific to this account, added to
{file}`$HOME/.config/aerc/binds.conf`.
See {manpage}`aerc-binds(5)`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.extraConfig
options.type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or attribute set of (values (null, bool, int, string, or float) or a list of values, that will be joined with a comma))
options.description:
Config specific to this account, added to {file}`$HOME/.config/aerc/aerc.conf`.
Aerc only supports per-account UI configuration.
For other sections of {file}`$HOME/.config/aerc/aerc.conf`,
use `programs.aerc.extraConfig`.
See {manpage}`aerc-config(5)`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.imapAuth
options.description:
Sets the authentication mechanism if imap is used as the incoming
method.
See {manpage}`aerc-imap(5)`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.imapOauth2Params
options.description:
Sets the oauth2 params if authentication mechanism oauthbearer or
xoauth2 is used.
See {manpage}`aerc-imap(5)`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.imapOauth2Params.client_id
options.description: The OAuth2 client identifier.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.imapOauth2Params.client_secret
options.description: The OAuth2 client secret.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.imapOauth2Params.scope
options.description: The OAuth2 requested scope.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.imapOauth2Params.token_endpoint
options.description: The OAuth2 token endpoint.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpAuth
options.description:
Sets the authentication mechanism if smtp is used as the outgoing
method.
See {manpage}`aerc-smtp(5)`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params
options.description:
Sets the oauth2 params if authentication mechanism oauthbearer or
xoauth2 is used.
See {manpage}`aerc-imap(5)`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params.client_id
options.description: The OAuth2 client identifier.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params.client_secret
options.description: The OAuth2 client secret.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params.scope
options.description: The OAuth2 requested scope.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params.token_endpoint
options.description: The OAuth2 token endpoint.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aliases
options.type: list of (string matching the pattern .*@.* or (submodule))
options.description: Alternative identities of this account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.alot.contactCompletion
options.description:
Contact completion configuration as expected per alot.
See [alot's wiki](http://alot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/configuration/contacts_completion.html) for
explanation about possible values.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.alot.extraConfig
options.description: Extra settings to add to this Alot account configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.alot.sendMailCommand
options.description:
Command to send a mail. If msmtp is enabled for the account,
then this is set to
{command}`msmtpq --read-envelope-from --read-recipients`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.astroid.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Astroid.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.astroid.extraConfig
options.description: Extra settings to add to this astroid account configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.astroid.sendMailCommand
options.description:
Command to send a mail. If msmtp is enabled for the account,
then this is set to
{command}`msmtpq --read-envelope-from --read-recipients`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.enable
options.description:
Whether this account is enabled.  Potentially useful to allow
setting email configuration globally then enabling or disabling on
specific systems.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.flavor
options.description:
Some email providers have peculiar behavior that require
special treatment. This option is therefore intended to
indicate the nature of the provider.

When this indicates a specific provider then, for example,
the IMAP, SMTP, and JMAP server configuration may be set
automatically.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders
options.description: Standard email folders.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.drafts
options.description: Relative path of the drafts mail folder.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.inbox
options.description: Relative path of the inbox mail.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.sent
options.description: Relative path of the sent mail folder.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.trash
options.description: Relative path of the deleted mail folder.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.delete
options.description:
Enable if you want to delete read messages from the server. Most
users should either enable `delete` or disable
`readAll`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.destinationCommand
options.description: Specify a command delivering the incoming mail to your maildir.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the getmail mail retriever for this account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.mailboxes
options.description:
A non-empty list of mailboxes. To download all mail you can
use the `ALL` mailbox.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.readAll
options.description:
Enable if you want to fetch all, even the read messages from the
server. Most users should either enable `delete` or
disable `readAll`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg
options.description: GPG configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg.encryptByDefault
options.description: Encrypt outgoing messages by default.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg.key
options.description: The key to use as listed in {command}`gpg --list-keys`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg.signByDefault
options.description: Sign messages by default.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.himalaya.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the email client Himalaya CLI for this email account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.himalaya.settings
options.description:
Himalaya CLI configuration for this email account.
See <https://github.com/pimalaya/himalaya/blob/master/config.sample.toml> for supported values.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap
options.description: The IMAP configuration to use for this account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.authentication
options.type: null or string or one of "anonymous", "apop", "clear", "cram_md5", "digest_md5", "gssapi", "login", "ntlm", "plain", "xoauth2"
options.description: The authentication mechanism.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.host
options.description: Hostname of IMAP server.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.port
options.type: null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
options.description:
The port on which the IMAP server listens. If
`null` then the default port is used.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls
options.description: Configuration for secure connections.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls.certificatesFile
options.description:
Path to file containing certificate authorities that should
be used to validate the connection authenticity. If
`null` then the system default is used.
Note, if set then the system default may still be accepted.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls.enable
options.description: Whether to enable TLS/SSL.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls.useStartTls
options.description: Whether to use STARTTLS.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.boxes
options.description: IMAP folders to watch.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.enable
options.description: Whether to enable imapnotify.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.extraArgs
options.description: Extra arguments to pass to goimapnotify.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.extraConfig
options.description: Additional configuration to add for this account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.onNotify
options.type: string or attribute set of string
options.description: Shell commands to run on any event.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.onNotifyPost
options.type: string or attribute set of string
options.description: Shell commands to run after onNotify event.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.jmap
options.description: The JMAP configuration to use for this account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.jmap.host
options.description:
Hostname of JMAP server.

If both this option and [](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.jmap.sessionUrl) are specified,
`host` is preferred by applications when establishing a
session.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.jmap.sessionUrl
options.description:
URL for the JMAP Session resource.

If both this option and [](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.jmap.host) are specified,
`host` is preferred by applications when establishing a
session.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.enable
options.description: Whether to enable lieer Gmail synchronization for notmuch.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.notmuchSetupWarning
options.description:
Warn if Notmuch is not also enabled for this account.

This can safely be disabled if {command}`notmuch init`
has been used to configure this account outside of Home
Manager.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings
options.description:
Settings which are applied to {file}`.gmailieer.json`
for the account.

See the [lieer manual](https://github.com/gauteh/lieer/)
for documentation of settings not explicitly covered by this module.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.drop_non_existing_label
options.description: Allow missing labels on the Gmail side to be dropped.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.file_extension
options.description:
Extension to include in local file names, which can be useful
for indexing with third-party programs.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.ignore_empty_history
options.description:
Work around a Gmail API quirk where an empty change history
is sometimes returned.

See this
[GitHub issue](https://github.com/gauteh/lieer/issues/120)
for more details.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.ignore_remote_labels
options.description:
Set Gmail labels to ignore when syncing from remote labels to
local tags (before translations).

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.ignore_tags
options.description:
Set labels to ignore when syncing from local tags to
remote labels (after translations).

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.local_trash_tag
options.description: Local tag to which the remote Gmail 'TRASH' label is translated.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.remove_local_messages
options.description: Remove local messages that have been deleted on the remote.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.replace_slash_with_dot
options.description: Replace '/' with '.' in Gmail labels.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.timeout
options.description: HTTP timeout in seconds. 0 means forever or system timeout.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.sync.enable
options.description: Whether to enable lieer synchronization service.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.sync.frequency
options.description:
How often to synchronize the account.

This value is passed to the systemd timer configuration as the
onCalendar option. See
{manpage}`systemd.time(7)`
for more information about the format.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.maildir
options.description: Maildir configuration for this account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.maildir.path
options.description:
Path to maildir directory where mail for this account is
stored. This is relative to the base maildir path.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.create
options.description:
Automatically create missing mailboxes within the
given mail store.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.enable
options.description: Whether to enable synchronization using mbsync.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.expunge
options.description:
Permanently remove messages marked for deletion from
the given mail store.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.account
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)
options.description: Account section extra configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.channel
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)
options.description: Per channel extra configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.local
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)
options.description: Local store extra configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.remote
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)
options.description: Remote store extra configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.flatten
options.description:
If set, flattens the hierarchy within the maildir by
substituting the canonical hierarchy delimiter
`/` with this value.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups
options.description:
Some email providers (Gmail) have a different directory hierarchy for
synchronized email messages. Namely, when using mbsync without specifying
a set of channels into a group, all synchronized directories end up beneath
the `[Gmail]/` directory.

This option allows you to specify a group, and subsequently channels that
will allow you to sync your mail into an arbitrary hierarchy.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels
options.description:
List of channels that should be grouped together into this group. When
performing a synchronization, the groups are synchronized, rather than
the individual channels.

Using these channels and then grouping them together allows for you to
define the maildir hierarchy as you see fit.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.extraConfig
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)
options.description:
Extra configuration lines to add to *THIS* channel's
configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.farPattern
options.description:
IMAP4 patterns for which mailboxes on the remote mail server to sync.
If `Patterns` are specified, `farPattern`
is interpreted as a prefix which is not matched against the patterns,
and is not affected by mailbox list overrides.

If this is left as the default, then mbsync will default to the pattern
`INBOX`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.name
options.description:
The unique name for THIS channel in THIS group. The group will refer to
this channel by this name.

In addition, you can manually sync just this channel by specifying this
name to mbsync on the command line.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.nearPattern
options.description:
Name for where mail coming from the remote (far) mail server will end up
locally. The mailbox specified by the far pattern will be placed in
this directory.

If this is left as the default, then mbsync will default to the pattern
`INBOX`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.patterns
options.description:
Instead of synchronizing *just* the mailboxes that
match the `farPattern`, use it as a prefix which is
not matched against the patterns, and is not affected by mailbox list
overrides.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.name
options.description:
The name of this group for this account. These names are different than
some others, because they will hide channel names that are the same.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.patterns
options.description: Pattern of mailboxes to synchronize.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.remove
options.description: Propagate mailbox deletions to the given mail store.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.subFolders
options.description:
The on-disk folder naming style. This option has no
effect when {option}`flatten` is used.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable the meli mail client for this account.
Requires SMTP settings..

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.mailboxAliases
options.description: Folder display name

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.mailboxes
options.description: Mailboxes to show in meli

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.msmtp.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable msmtp.

If enabled then it is possible to use the
`--account` command line
option to send a message for a given account using the
{command}`msmtp` or {command}`msmtpq` tool.
For example, {command}`msmtp --account=private` would
send using the account defined in
{option}`accounts.email.accounts.private`. If the
`--account` option is not
given then the primary account will be used.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.msmtp.extraConfig
options.description:
Extra configuration options to add to {file}`~/.msmtprc`.
See <https://marlam.de/msmtp/msmtprc.txt> for
examples.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.msmtp.tls.fingerprint
options.type: null or string matching the pattern ([[:alnum:]]{2}:)+[[:alnum:]]{2}
options.description:
Fingerprint of a trusted TLS certificate.
The fingerprint can be obtained by executing
{command}`msmtp --serverinfo --tls --tls-certcheck=off`.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mu.enable
options.description: Whether to enable mu indexing.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.enable
options.description: Whether to enable mujmap JMAP synchronization for notmuch.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.notmuchSetupWarning
options.description:
Warn if Notmuch is not also enabled for this account.

This can safely be disabled if {file}`mujmap.toml` is managed
outside of Home Manager.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings
options.description:
Settings which are applied to {file}`mujmap.toml`
for the account.

See the [mujmap project](https://github.com/elizagamedev/mujmap)
for documentation of settings not explicitly covered by this module.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.auto_create_new_mailboxes
options.description:
Whether to create new mailboxes automatically on the server from notmuch
tags.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.cache_dir
options.description:
The cache directory in which to store mail files while they are being
downloaded. The default is operating-system specific.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.fqdn
options.description:
Fully qualified domain name of the JMAP service.

mujmap looks up the JMAP SRV record for this host to determine the JMAP session
URL. Mutually exclusive with
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.mujmap.settings.session_url).

If `null`, defaults to
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.jmap.host).

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.password_command
options.type: null or string or list of string
options.description:
Shell command which will print a password to stdout for basic HTTP
authentication.

If `null`, defaults to
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.passwordCommand).

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.session_url
options.description:
Session URL to connect to.

Mutually exclusive with
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.mujmap.settings.fqdn).

If `null`, defaults to
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.jmap.sessionUrl).

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags
options.description:
Tag configuration.

Beware that there are quirks that require manual consideration if changing the
values of these files; please see
<https://github.com/elizagamedev/mujmap/blob/main/mujmap.toml.example>
for more details.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.deleted
options.description:
Tag for notmuch to use for messages stored in the mailbox labeled with the
`Trash` name attribute.

If set to an empty string, this mailbox *and its child
mailboxes* are not synchronized with a tag.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.directory_separator
options.description: Directory separator for mapping notmuch tags to maildirs.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.important
options.description:
Tag for notmuch to use for messages stored in the mailbox labeled with the
`Important` name attribute and/or with the `$Important`
keyword.

If set to an empty string, this mailbox, *its child
mailboxes*, and these keywords are not synchronized with a tag.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.inbox
options.description:
Tag for notmuch to use for messages stored in the mailbox labeled with the
`Inbox` name attribute.

If set to an empty string, this mailbox *and its child
mailboxes* are not synchronized with a tag.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.lowercase
options.description:
If true, translate all mailboxes to lowercase names when mapping to notmuch
tags.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.phishing
options.description:
Tag for notmuch to use for the IANA `$Phishing` keyword.

If set to an empty string, this keyword is not synchronized with a tag.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.sent
options.description:
Tag for notmuch to use for messages stored in the mailbox labeled with the
`Sent` name attribute.

If set to an empty string, this mailbox *and its child
mailboxes* are not synchronized with a tag.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.spam
options.description:
Tag for notmuch to use for messages stored in the mailbox labeled with the
`Junk` name attribute and/or with the `$Junk` keyword,
*except* for messages with the `$NotJunk` keyword.

If set to an empty string, this mailbox, *its child
mailboxes*, and these keywords are not synchronized with a tag.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.username
options.description:
Username for basic HTTP authentication.

If `null`, defaults to
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.userName).

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.name
options.description:
Unique identifier of the account. This is set to the
attribute name of the account configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.neomutt.enable
options.description: Whether to enable NeoMutt.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.neomutt.extraConfig
options.description: Extra lines to add to the folder hook for this account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.neomutt.extraMailboxes
options.description: List of extra mailboxes

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.neomutt.mailboxName
options.description: Use a different name as mailbox name

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.neomutt.mailboxType
options.description: Whether this account uses maildir folders or IMAP mailboxes

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.neomutt.sendMailCommand
options.description: Command to send a mail. If not set, neomutt will be in charge of sending mails.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.neomutt.showDefaultMailbox
options.description: Show the default mailbox (INBOX)

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.notmuch.enable
options.description: Whether to enable notmuch indexing.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.notmuch.neomutt.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Notmuch support in NeoMutt.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.notmuch.neomutt.virtualMailboxes
options.description: List of virtual mailboxes using Notmuch queries

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.notmuch.neomutt.virtualMailboxes.*.limit
options.description: Restricts number of messages/threads in the result.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.notmuch.neomutt.virtualMailboxes.*.name
options.description: Name to display

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.notmuch.neomutt.virtualMailboxes.*.query
options.description: Notmuch query

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.notmuch.neomutt.virtualMailboxes.*.type
options.description: Reads all matching messages or whole-threads. The default is 'messages' or nm_query_type.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.offlineimap.enable
options.description: Whether to enable OfflineIMAP.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.offlineimap.extraConfig.account
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean)
options.description: Extra configuration options to add to the account section.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.offlineimap.extraConfig.local
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean)
options.description:
Extra configuration options to add to the local account
section.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.offlineimap.extraConfig.remote
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean)
options.description:
Extra configuration options to add to the remote account
section.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.offlineimap.postSyncHookCommand
options.description: Command to run after fetching new mails.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.passwordCommand
options.type: null or string or list of string
options.description:
A command, which when run writes the account password on
standard output.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.primary
options.description:
Whether this is the primary account. Only one account may be
set as primary.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.realName
options.description: Name displayed when sending mails.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.signature
options.description: Signature configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.signature.command
options.description: A command that generates a signature.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.signature.delimiter
options.description: The delimiter used between the document and the signature.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.signature.showSignature
options.description: Method to communicate the signature.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.signature.text
options.description: Signature content.
options.example:
''
  --
  Luke Skywalker
  May the force be with you.
''

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.smtp
options.description: The SMTP configuration to use for this account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.smtp.authentication
options.type: null or string or one of "anonymous", "apop", "clear", "cram_md5", "digest_md5", "gssapi", "login", "ntlm", "plain", "xoauth2"
options.description: The authentication mechanism.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.smtp.host
options.description: Hostname of SMTP server.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.smtp.port
options.type: null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
options.description:
The port on which the SMTP server listens. If
`null` then the default port is used.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.smtp.tls
options.description: Configuration for secure connections.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.smtp.tls.certificatesFile
options.description:
Path to file containing certificate authorities that should
be used to validate the connection authenticity. If
`null` then the system default is used.
Note, if set then the system default may still be accepted.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.smtp.tls.enable
options.description: Whether to enable TLS/SSL.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.smtp.tls.useStartTls
options.description: Whether to use STARTTLS.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the Thunderbird mail client for this account.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.messageFilters
options.description:
List of message filters to add to this Thunderbird account
configuration.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.messageFilters.*.action
options.description: Action to perform on matched messages.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.messageFilters.*.actionValue
options.description: Argument passed to the filter action, e.g. a folder path.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.messageFilters.*.condition
options.description: Condition to match messages against.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.messageFilters.*.enabled
options.description: Whether this filter is currently active.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.messageFilters.*.extraConfig
options.description: Extra settings to apply to the filter

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.messageFilters.*.name
options.description: Name for the filter.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.messageFilters.*.text
options.description:
The raw text of the filter.
Note that this will override all other options.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.messageFilters.*.type
options.description: Type for this filter.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.perIdentitySettings
options.type: function that evaluates to a(n) attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or string)
options.description:
Extra settings to add to each identity of this Thunderbird
account configuration. The {var}`id` given as
argument is an automatically generated identifier.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.profiles
options.description:
List of Thunderbird profiles for which this account should be
enabled. If this list is empty (the default), this account will
be enabled for all declared profiles.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.thunderbird.settings
options.type: function that evaluates to a(n) attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or string)
options.description:
Extra settings to add to this Thunderbird account configuration.
The {var}`id` given as argument is an automatically
generated account identifier.

options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.userName
options.description:
The server username of this account. This will be used as
the SMTP, IMAP, and JMAP user name.

options.title: accounts.email.certificatesFile
options.description:
Path to default file containing certificate authorities that
should be used to validate the connection authenticity. This
path may be overridden on a per-account basis.

options.title: accounts.email.maildirBasePath
options.description:
The base directory for account maildir directories. May be a
relative path (e.g. the user setting this value as "MyMaildir"),
in which case it is relative the home directory (e.g. resulting
in "~/MyMaildir").

options.title: dbus.packages
options.description:
Packages whose D-Bus configuration files should be included in
the configuration of the D-Bus session-wide message bus. Specifically,
files in «pkg»/share/dbus-1/services will be included in the user's
$XDG_DATA_HOME/dbus-1/services directory.

options.title: dconf.settings
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (GVariant value)
options.description:
Settings to write to the dconf configuration system.

Note that the database is strongly-typed so you need to use the same types
as described in the GSettings schema. For example, if an option is of type
`uint32` (`u`), you need to wrap the number
using the `lib.hm.gvariant.mkUint32` constructor.
Otherwise, since Nix integers are implicitly coerced to `int32`
(`i`), it would get stored in the database as such, and GSettings
might be confused when loading the setting.

You might want to use [dconf2nix](https://github.com/gvolpe/dconf2nix)
to convert dconf database dumps into compatible Nix expression.

options.title: editorconfig.enable
options.description: Whether to enable EditorConfig home configuration file.

options.title: editorconfig.settings
options.type: attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
options.description:
Configuration written to {file}`$HOME/.editorconfig`.
`root = true` is automatically added to the file,
it must not be added here.
See <https://editorconfig.org> for documentation.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.antialiasing
options.description: Whether to enable font antialiasing.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.configFile
options.description:
Extra font config files that will be added to `~/.config/fontconfig/conf.d/`.
Files are named like `fontconfig/conf.d/{priority}-{label}.conf`.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Whether this font config file should be generated..

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.label
options.description: Label to use for the name of the config file.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.priority
options.description:
Determines the order in which configs are loaded.
Must be a value within the range of 0-99, where priority 0 is the highest priority and 99 is the lowest.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.source
options.description: Config file to source. Alternatively, use the 'text' option instead.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.configFile.<name>.text
options.description: Verbatim contents of the config file. If this option is null then the 'source' option must be set.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.defaultFonts.emoji
options.description:
Per-user default emoji font(s). Multiple fonts may be listed in
case a font does not support all emoji.

Note that fontconfig matches color emoji fonts preferentially,
so if you want to use a black and white font while having
a color font installed (eg. Noto Color Emoji installed alongside
Noto Emoji), fontconfig will still choose the color font even
when it is later in the list.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.defaultFonts.monospace
options.description:
Per-user default monospace font(s). Multiple fonts may be listed in
case multiple languages must be supported.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.defaultFonts.sansSerif
options.description:
Per-user default sans serif font(s). Multiple fonts may be listed
in case multiple languages must be supported.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.defaultFonts.serif
options.description:
Per-user default serif font(s). Multiple fonts may be listed in
case multiple languages must be supported.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable fontconfig configuration. This will, for
example, allow fontconfig to discover fonts and
configurations installed through
{var}`home.packages` and
{command}`nix-env`.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.hinting
options.description: The font hinting mode.

options.title: fonts.fontconfig.subpixelRendering
options.description: The sub-pixel rendering mode.

options.title: gtk.colorScheme
options.description: Default color scheme for all GTK versions

options.title: gtk.cursorTheme
options.description: Default cursor theme for all GTK versions.

options.title: gtk.cursorTheme.name
options.description: The name of the cursor theme within the package.

options.title: gtk.cursorTheme.package
options.description:
Package providing the cursor theme. This package will be installed
to your profile. If `null` then the cursor theme
is assumed to already be available in your profile.

options.title: gtk.cursorTheme.size
options.description: The size of the cursor.

options.title: gtk.enable
options.description: Whether to enable GTK theming and configuration.

options.title: gtk.font
options.description: Default font for all GTK versions.

options.title: gtk.font.name
options.description: The family name of the font within the package.

options.title: gtk.font.package
options.description:
Package providing the font. This package will be installed
to your profile. If `null` then the font
is assumed to already be available in your profile.

options.title: gtk.font.size
options.type: null or signed integer or floating point number
options.description: The size of the font.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.configLocation
options.description: The location of the GTK 2 configuration file.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.cursorTheme
options.description: Cursor theme for GTK 2 applications.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.cursorTheme.name
options.description: The name of the cursor theme within the package.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.cursorTheme.package
options.description:
Package providing the cursor theme. This package will be installed
to your profile. If `null` then the cursor theme
is assumed to already be available in your profile.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.cursorTheme.size
options.description: The size of the cursor.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.enable
options.description: Whether to enable GTK 2 configuration.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.extraConfig
options.description: Extra lines to add to {file}`~/.gtkrc-2.0`.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.font
options.description: Font for GTK 2 applications.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.font.name
options.description: The family name of the font within the package.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.font.package
options.description:
Package providing the font. This package will be installed
to your profile. If `null` then the font
is assumed to already be available in your profile.

options.title: gtk.gtk2.font.size
options.type: null or signed integer or floating point number
options.description: The size of the font.

options.title: home.activation
options.description:
The activation scripts blocks to run when activating a Home
Manager generation. Any entry here should be idempotent,
meaning running twice or more times produces the same result
as running it once.

If the script block produces any observable side effect, such
as writing or deleting files, then it
*must* be placed after the special
`writeBoundary` script block. Prior to the
write boundary one can place script blocks that verifies, but
does not modify, the state of the system and exits if an
unexpected state is found. For example, the
`checkLinkTargets` script block checks for
collisions between non-managed files and files defined in
[](#opt-home.file).

A script block should respect the {var}`DRY_RUN` variable. If it is set
then the actions taken by the script should be logged to standard out
and not actually performed. A convenient shell function {command}`run`
is provided for activation script blocks. It is used as follows:

{command}`run {command}`
: Runs the given command on live run, otherwise prints the command to
standard output.

{command}`run --quiet {command}`
: Runs the given command on live run and sends its standard output to
{file}`/dev/null`, otherwise prints the command to standard output.

{command}`run --silence {command}`
: Runs the given command on live run and sends its standard and error
output to {file}`/dev/null`, otherwise prints the command to standard
output.

The `--quiet` and `--silence` flags are mutually exclusive.

A script block should also respect the {var}`VERBOSE` variable, and if
set print information on standard out that may be useful for debugging
any issue that may arise. The variable {var}`VERBOSE_ARG` is set to
{option}`--verbose` if verbose output is enabled. You can also use the
provided shell function {command}`verboseEcho`, which acts as
{command}`echo` when verbose output is enabled.

options.title: home.checks
options.description:
Packages that are added as dependencies of the home's build, usually
for the purpose of validating some part of the configuration.

Unlike `home.extraDependencies`, these store paths do not
become part of the built home configuration.

options.title: home.enableDebugInfo
options.description:
Some Nix packages provide debug symbols for
{command}`gdb` in the `debug` output.
This option ensures that those are automatically fetched from
the binary cache if available and {command}`gdb` is
configured to find those symbols.

options.title: home.enableNixpkgsReleaseCheck
options.description:
Determines whether to check for release version mismatch between Home
Manager and Nixpkgs. Using mismatched versions is likely to cause errors
and unexpected behavior. It is therefore highly recommended to use a
release of Home Manager that corresponds with your chosen release of
Nixpkgs.

When this option is enabled and a mismatch is detected then a warning
will be printed when the user configuration is being built.

options.title: home.extraDependencies
options.type: list of path in the Nix store
options.description:
A list of paths that should be included in the home
closure but generally not visible.

For built-time checks the `home.checks` option is more appropriate
for that purpose as checks should not leave a trace in the built
home configuration.

options.title: home.extraOutputsToInstall
options.description:
List of additional package outputs of the packages
{var}`home.packages` that should be installed into
the user environment.

options.title: home.file
options.description: Attribute set of files to link into the user home.

options.title: home.file.<name>.enable
options.description:
Whether this file should be generated. This option allows specific
files to be disabled.

options.title: home.file.<name>.executable
options.description:
Set the execute bit. If `null`, defaults to the mode
of the {var}`source` file or to `false`
for files created through the {var}`text` option.

options.title: home.file.<name>.force
options.description:
Whether the target path should be unconditionally replaced
by the managed file source. Warning, this will silently
delete the target regardless of whether it is a file or
link.

options.title: home.file.<name>.ignorelinks
options.description:
When `recursive` is enabled, adds `-ignorelinks` flag to lndir

It causes lndir to not treat symbolic links in the source directory specially.
The link created in the target directory will point back to the corresponding
(symbolic link) file in the source directory. If the link is to a directory

options.title: home.file.<name>.onChange
options.description:
Shell commands to run when file has changed between
generations. The script will be run
*after* the new files have been linked
into place.

Note, this code is always run when `recursive` is
enabled.

options.title: home.file.<name>.recursive
options.description:
If the file source is a directory, then this option
determines whether the directory should be recursively
linked to the target location. This option has no effect
if the source is a file.

If `false` (the default) then the target
will be a symbolic link to the source directory. If
`true` then the target will be a
directory structure matching the source's but whose leafs
are symbolic links to the files of the source directory.

options.title: home.file.<name>.source
options.description:
Path of the source file or directory. If
[](#opt-home.file._name_.text)
is non-null then this option will automatically point to a file
containing that text.

options.title: home.file.<name>.target
options.description: Path to target file relative to {env}`HOME`.

options.title: home.file.<name>.text
options.description:
Text of the file. If this option is null then
[](#opt-home.file._name_.source)
must be set.

options.title: home.homeDirectory
options.description: The user's home directory. Must be an absolute path.
options.default:
"$HOME"   for state version < 20.09,
undefined for state version ≥ 20.09

options.title: home.keyboard
options.description:
Keyboard configuration. Set to `null` to
disable Home Manager keyboard management.
options.default:
"{ }"  for state version < 21.11,
"null" for state version ≥ 21.11

options.title: home.keyboard.layout
options.description:
Keyboard layout. If `null`, then the system
configuration will be used.

This defaults to `null` for state
version ≥ 19.09 and `"us"` otherwise.

options.title: home.keyboard.model
options.description: Keyboard model.

options.title: home.keyboard.options
options.description: X keyboard options; layout switching goes here.

options.title: home.keyboard.variant
options.description:
X keyboard variant. If `null`, then the
system configuration will be used.

This defaults to `null` for state
version ≥ 19.09 and `""` otherwise.

options.title: home.language.base

options.title: home.language.collate
options.description: The language to use for collation (alphabetical ordering).

options.title: home.language.ctype
options.description: Character classification category.

options.title: home.language.measurement

options.title: home.language.messages

options.title: home.language.monetary

options.title: home.language.name

options.title: home.language.numeric

options.title: home.language.paper

options.title: home.language.telephone

options.title: home.language.time
options.description: The language to use for formatting times.

options.title: home.packages
options.description: The set of packages to appear in the user environment.

options.title: home.pointerCursor
options.description:
Cursor configuration.

Top-level options declared under this submodule are backend independent
options. Options declared under namespaces such as `x11`
are backend specific options. By default, only backend independent cursor
configurations are generated. If you need configurations for specific
backends, you can toggle them via the enable option. For example,
[](#opt-home.pointerCursor.x11.enable)
will enable x11 cursor configurations.

Note that this will merely generate the cursor configurations.
To apply the configurations, the relevant subsytems must also be configured.
For example, [](#opt-home.pointerCursor.gtk.enable) will generate
the gtk cursor configuration, but [](#opt-gtk.enable) needs
to be set for it to be applied.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.dotIcons.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable `.icons` config generation for {option}`home.pointerCursor`
.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.enable
options.description: Whether to enable cursor config generation.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.gtk.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable gtk config generation for {option}`home.pointerCursor`
.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.enable
options.description: Whether to enable hyprcursor config generation.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.hyprcursor.size
options.description: The cursor size for hyprcursor.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.name
options.description: The cursor name within the package.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.package
options.description: Package providing the cursor theme.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.size
options.description: The cursor size.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.sway.enable
options.description: Whether to enable sway config generation for {option}`home.pointerCursor`.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.x11.defaultCursor
options.description: The default cursor file to use within the package.

options.title: home.pointerCursor.x11.enable

Whether to enable x11 config generation for {option}`home.pointerCursor`
.

options.title: home.preferXdgDirectories
options.description: Whether to make programs use XDG directories whenever supported.

options.title: home.profileDirectory
options.description: The profile directory where Home Manager generations are installed.

options.title: home.sessionPath
options.description:
Extra directories to prepend to {env}`PATH`.

These directories are added to the {env}`PATH` variable in a
double-quoted context, so expressions like `$HOME` are
expanded by the shell. However, since expressions like `~` or
`*` are escaped, they will end up in the {env}`PATH`
verbatim.

options.title: home.sessionSearchVariables
options.type: attribute set of list of string
options.description:
Extra directories to prepend to arbitrary PATH-like
environment variables (e.g.: {env}`MANPATH`). The values
will be concatenated by `:`.
These directories are added to the environment variable in a
double-quoted context, so expressions like `$HOME` are
expanded by the shell. However, since expressions like `~` or
`*` are escaped, they will end up in the environment
verbatim.

options.title: home.sessionVariables
options.type: lazy attribute set of (string or absolute path or signed integer or floating point number)

options.title: home.shell.enableBashIntegration
options.description: Whether to enable Bash integration.

options.title: home.shell.enableFishIntegration
options.description: Whether to enable Fish integration.

options.title: home.shell.enableIonIntegration
options.description: Whether to enable Ion integration.

options.title: home.shell.enableNushellIntegration
options.description: Whether to enable Nushell integration.

options.title: home.shell.enableShellIntegration
options.description:
Whether to globally enable shell integration for all supported shells.

Individual shell integrations can be overridden with their respective
`shell.enable<SHELL>Integration` option. For example, the following
declaration globally disables shell integration for Bash:

```nix
home.shell.enableBashIntegration = false;
```

options.title: home.shell.enableZshIntegration
options.description: Whether to enable Zsh integration.

options.title: home.shellAliases
options.description:
An attribute set that maps aliases (the top level attribute names
in this option) to command strings or directly to build outputs.

This option should only be used to manage simple aliases that are
compatible across all shells. If you need to use a shell specific
feature then make sure to use a shell specific option, for example
[](#opt-programs.bash.shellAliases) for Bash.

options.title: home.stateVersion
options.description:
It is occasionally necessary for Home Manager to change
configuration defaults in a way that is incompatible with
stateful data. This could, for example, include switching the
default data format or location of a file.

The *state version* indicates which default
settings are in effect and will therefore help avoid breaking
program configurations. Switching to a higher state version
typically requires performing some manual steps, such as data
conversion or moving files.

options.title: home.username
options.description: The user's username.
options.default:
"$USER"   for state version < 20.09,
undefined for state version ≥ 20.09

options.title: i18n.glibcLocales

Customized `glibcLocales` package providing
the `LOCALE_ARCHIVE_*` environment variable.

This option only applies to the Linux platform.

When Home Manager is configured with NixOS, the default value
will be set to {var}`i18n.glibcLocales` from the
system configuration.

options.default: `true` if the deprecated option `enabled` is set, false otherwise

options.description:
Ignore the user configures. **Warning**: When this is enabled, the

options.description: Quick phrases.

The addon configures in `conf` folder in ini format with global sections.
Each item is written to the corresponding file.

options.type: section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
