options.type: attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
options.description: attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))

options.title: i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.globalOptions
options.type: open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
options.description: The global options in `config` file in ini format.

options.title: i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.inputMethod
options.type: open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
options.description: The input method configure in `profile` file in ini format.

options.title: i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.themes
options.description: Themes to be written to {file}`$XDG_DATA_HOME/fcitx5/themes/${name}`

options.title: i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.themes.<name>.highlightImage
options.description: Path to the SVG of the highlight.

options.title: i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.themes.<name>.panelImage
options.description: Path to the SVG of the panel.

options.title: i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.themes.<name>.theme
options.type: null or (attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))) or strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path
options.description:
The `theme.conf` file of the theme.

See https://fcitx-im.org/wiki/Fcitx_5_Theme#Background_images
for more information.

options.title: i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.waylandFrontend
options.description:
Use the Wayland input method frontend.
See [Using Fcitx 5 on Wayland](https://fcitx-im.org/wiki/Using_Fcitx_5_on_Wayland).

options.title: i18n.inputMethod.kime.extraConfig
options.description:
kime configuration. Refer to
<https://github.com/Riey/kime/blob/develop/docs/CONFIGURATION.md>
for details on supported values.
options.example:
daemon:
  modules: [Xim,Indicator]
indicator:
  icon_color: White
engine:
  hangul:
    layout: dubeolsik

options.title: i18n.inputMethod.type
options.description:
Select the enabled input method. Input methods are software to input
symbols that are not available on standard input devices.

Input methods are especially used to input Chinese, Japanese and
Korean characters.

Currently the following input methods are available in Home Manager:

`fcitx5`
: A customizable lightweight input method.
  The next generation of fcitx.
  Addons (including engines, dictionaries, skins) can be added using
  [](#opt-i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.addons).

`nabi`
: A Korean input method based on XIM. Nabi doesn't support Qt 5.

`uim`
: The "universal input method" is a library with an XIM bridge.
  uim mainly supports Chinese, Japanese and Korean.

`hime`
: An extremely easy-to-use input method framework.

`kime`
: A Korean IME.
options.default: The value of the deprecated option `enabled`, defaulting to null

options.title: i18n.inputMethod.uim.toolbar
options.description: Selected UIM toolbar.

options.title: launchd.agents
options.description: Define LaunchAgents.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config
options.type: open submodule of attribute set of anything
options.description: Define a launchd job. See {manpage}`launchd.plist(5)` for details.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.AbandonProcessGroup
options.description:
When a job dies, launchd kills any remaining processes with the same process group ID as the job. Setting
this key to true disables that behavior.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Debug
options.description:
This optional key specifies that launchd should adjust its log mask temporarily to LOG_DEBUG while
dealing with this job.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Disabled
options.description:
This optional key is used as a hint to `launchctl(1)` that it should not submit this job to launchd when
loading a job or jobs. The value of this key does NOT reflect the current state of the job on the running
system. If you wish to know whether a job is loaded in launchd, reading this key from a configuration
file yourself is not a sufficient test. You should query launchd for the presence of the job using
the `launchctl(1)` list subcommand or use the ServiceManagement framework's
`SMJobCopyDictionary()` method.

Note that as of Mac OS X v10.6, this key's value in a configuration file conveys a default value, which
is changed with the [-w] option of the `launchctl(1)` load and unload subcommands. These subcommands no
longer modify the configuration file, so the value displayed in the configuration file is not necessarily
the value that `launchctl(1)` will apply. See `launchctl(1)` for more information.

Please also be mindful that you should only use this key if the provided on-demand and KeepAlive criteria
are insufficient to describe the conditions under which your job needs to run. The cost to have a
job loaded in launchd is negligible, so there is no harm in loading a job which only runs once or very
rarely.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.EnableGlobbing
options.description: This flag causes launchd to use the `glob(3)` mechanism to update the program arguments before invocation.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.EnableTransactions
options.description:
This flag instructs launchd that the job promises to use `vproc_transaction_begin(3)` and
`vproc_transaction_end(3)` to track outstanding transactions that need to be reconciled before the
process can safely terminate. If no outstanding transactions are in progress, then launchd is free to
send the SIGKILL signal.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.EnvironmentVariables
options.description:
This optional key is used to specify additional environment variables to be set before running the
job.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.ExitTimeOut
options.description:
The amount of time launchd waits before sending a SIGKILL signal. The default value is 20 seconds. The
value zero is interpreted as infinity.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.GroupName
options.description:
This optional key specifies the group to run the job as. This key is only applicable when launchd is
running as root. If UserName is set and GroupName is not, the the group will be set to the default
group of the user.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.HardResourceLimits
options.description:
Resource limits to be imposed on the job. These adjust variables set with `setrlimit(2)`.  The following
keys apply:

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.HardResourceLimits.CPU
options.description: The maximum amount of cpu time (in seconds) to be used by each process.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.HardResourceLimits.Core
options.description: The largest size (in bytes) core file that may be created.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.HardResourceLimits.Data
options.description:
The maximum size (in bytes) of the data segment for a process; this defines how far a program may
extend its break with the `sbrk(2)` system call.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.HardResourceLimits.FileSize
options.description: The largest size (in bytes) file that may be created.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.HardResourceLimits.MemoryLock
options.description: The maximum size (in bytes) which a process may lock into memory using the `mlock(2)` function.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.HardResourceLimits.NumberOfFiles
options.description:
The maximum number of open files for this process.  Setting this value in a system wide daemon
will set the `sysctl(3)` kern.maxfiles (SoftResourceLimits) or kern.maxfilesperproc (HardResourceLimits)
value in addition to the `setrlimit(2)` values.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.HardResourceLimits.NumberOfProcesses
options.description:
The maximum number of simultaneous processes for this user id.  Setting this value in a system
wide daemon will set the `sysctl(3)` kern.maxproc (SoftResourceLimits) or kern.maxprocperuid
(HardResourceLimits) value in addition to the `setrlimit(2)` values.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.HardResourceLimits.ResidentSetSize
options.description:
The maximum size (in bytes) to which a process's resident set size may grow.  This imposes a
limit on the amount of physical memory to be given to a process; if memory is tight, the system
will prefer to take memory from processes that are exceeding their declared resident set size.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.HardResourceLimits.Stack
options.description:
The maximum size (in bytes) of the stack segment for a process; this defines how far a program's
stack segment may be extended.  Stack extension is performed automatically by the system.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.InitGroups
options.description:
This optional key specifies whether `initgroups(3)` should be called before running the job.  The default
is true in 10.5 and false in 10.4. This key will be ignored if the UserName key is not set.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.KeepAlive
options.description:
This optional key is used to control whether your job is to be kept continuously running or to let
demand and conditions control the invocation. The default is false and therefore only demand will start
the job. The value may be set to true to unconditionally keep the job alive. Alternatively, a dictionary
of conditions may be specified to selectively control whether launchd keeps a job alive or not. If
multiple keys are provided, launchd ORs them, thus providing maximum flexibility to the job to refine
the logic and stall if necessary. If launchd finds no reason to restart the job, it falls back on
demand based invocation.  Jobs that exit quickly and frequently when configured to be kept alive will
be throttled to converve system resources.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Label
options.description: This required key uniquely identifies the job to launchd.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.LaunchEvents
options.description:
Specifies higher-level event types to be used as launch-on-demand event
sources.  Each sub-dictionary defines events for a particular event
subsystem, such as "com.apple.iokit.matching", which can be used to
launch jobs based on the appearance of nodes in the IORegistry. Each
dictionary within the sub-dictionary specifies an event descriptor that
is specified to each event subsystem. With this key, the job promises to
use the xpc_set_event_stream_handler(3) API to consume events. See
xpc_events(3) for more details on event sources.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.LaunchOnlyOnce
options.description:
This optional key specifies whether the job can only be run once and only once.  In other words, if the
job cannot be safely respawned without a full machine reboot, then set this key to be true.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.LimitLoadFromHosts
options.description:
This configuration file only applies to hosts NOT listed with this key. Note: One should set kern.hostname
in `sysctl.conf(5)` for this feature to work reliably.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.LimitLoadToHosts
options.description:
This configuration file only applies to the hosts listed with this key. Note: One should set kern.hostname
in `sysctl.conf(5)` for this feature to work reliably.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.LimitLoadToSessionType
options.type: null or string or list of string
options.description:
This configuration file only applies to sessions of the type specified. This key is used in concert
with the -S flag to {command}`launchctl`.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.LowPriorityBackgroundIO
options.description:
This optional key specifies whether the kernel should consider this daemon to be low priority when
doing file system I/O when the process is throttled with the Darwin-background classification.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.LowPriorityIO
options.description:
This optional key specifies whether the kernel should consider this daemon to be low priority when
doing file system I/O.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.MachServices
options.description:
This optional key is used to specify Mach services to be registered with the Mach bootstrap sub-system.
Each key in this dictionary should be the name of service to be advertised. The value of the key must
be a boolean and set to true.  Alternatively, a dictionary can be used instead of a simple true value.

Finally, for the job itself, the values will be replaced with Mach ports at the time of check-in with
launchd.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Nice
options.description: This optional key specifies what nice(3) value should be applied to the daemon.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.OnDemand
options.description:
This key was used in Mac OS X 10.4 to control whether a job was kept alive or not. The default was
true.  This key has been deprecated and replaced in Mac OS X 10.5 and later with the more powerful
KeepAlive option.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.ProcessType
options.description:
This optional key describes, at a high level, the intended purpose of the job.  The system will apply
resource limits based on what kind of job it is. If left unspecified, the system will apply light
resource limits to the job, throttling its CPU usage and I/O bandwidth. The following are valid values:

Background
: Background jobs are generally processes that do work that was not directly requested by the user.
  The resource limits applied to Background jobs are intended to prevent them from disrupting the
  user experience.

Standard
: Standard jobs are equivalent to no ProcessType being set.

Adaptive
: Adaptive jobs move between the Background and Interactive classifications based on activity over
  XPC connections. See `xpc_transaction_begin(3)` for details.

Interactive
: Interactive jobs run with the same resource limitations as apps, that is to say, none. Interactive
  jobs are critical to maintaining a responsive user experience, and this key should only be
  used if an app's ability to be responsive depends on it, and cannot be made Adaptive.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Program
options.description:
This key maps to the first argument of `execvp(3)`.  If this key is missing, then the first element of
the array of strings provided to the ProgramArguments will be used instead.  This key is required in
the absence of the ProgramArguments key.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.ProgramArguments
options.description:
This key maps to the second argument of `execvp(3)`.  This key is required in the absence of the Program
key. Please note: many people are confused by this key. Please read `execvp(3)` very carefully!

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.QueueDirectories
options.description:
Much like the WatchPaths option, this key will watch the paths for modifications. The difference being
that the job will only be started if the path is a directory and the directory is not empty.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.RootDirectory
options.description: This optional key is used to specify a directory to `chroot(2)` to before running the job.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.RunAtLoad
options.description:
This optional key is used to control whether your job is launched once at the time the job is loaded.
The default is false.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.ServiceIPC
options.description:
This optional key specifies whether the job participates in advanced
communication with launchd. The default is false. This flag is
incompatible with the inetdCompatibility key.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SessionCreate
options.description:
This key specifies that the job should be spawned into a new security
audit session rather than the default session for the context is belongs
to. See auditon(2) for details.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets
options.description:
This optional key is used to specify launch on demand sockets that can be used to let launchd know when
to run the job. The job must check-in to get a copy of the file descriptors using APIs outlined in
launch(3).  The keys of the top level Sockets dictionary can be anything. They are meant for the application
developer to use to differentiate which descriptors correspond to which application level protocols
(e.g. http vs. ftp vs. DNS...).  At check-in time, the value of each Sockets dictionary key will
be an array of descriptors. Daemon/Agent writers should consider all descriptors of a given key to be
to be effectively equivalent, even though each file descriptor likely represents a different networking
protocol which conforms to the criteria specified in the job configuration file.

The parameters below are used as inputs to call `getaddrinfo(3)`.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.Bonjour
options.type: null or boolean or list of string
options.description:
This optional key can be used to request that the service be registered with the
`mDNSResponder(8)`.  If the value is boolean, the service name is inferred from the SockServiceName.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.MulticastGroup
options.description:
This optional key can be used to request that the datagram socket join a multicast group.  If the
value is a hostname, then `getaddrinfo(3)` will be used to join the correct multicast address for a
given socket family.  If an explicit IPv4 or IPv6 address is given, it is required that the SockFamily
family also be set, otherwise the results are undefined.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.SecureSocketWithKey
options.description:
This optional key is a variant of SockPathName. Instead of binding to a known path, a securely
generated socket is created and the path is assigned to the environment variable that is inherited
by all jobs spawned by launchd.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.SockFamily
options.description: This optional key can be used to specifically request that "IPv4" or "IPv6" socket(s) be created.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.SockNodeName
options.description: This optional key specifies the node to `connect(2)` or `bind(2)` to.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.SockPassive
options.description:
This optional key specifies whether `listen(2)` or `connect(2)` should be called on the created file
descriptor. The default is true ("to listen").

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.SockPathMode
options.description:
This optional key specifies the mode of the socket. Known bug: Property lists don't support
octal, so please convert the value to decimal.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.SockPathName
options.description:
This optional key implies SockFamily is set to "Unix". It specifies the path to `connect(2)` or
`bind(2)` to.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.SockProtocol
options.description:
This optional key specifies the protocol to be passed to `socket(2)`.  The only value understood by
this key at the moment is "TCP".

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.SockServiceName
options.description: This optional key specifies the service on the node to `connect(2)` or `bind(2)` to.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Sockets.<name>.SockType
options.description:
This optional key tells launchctl what type of socket to create. The default is "stream" and
other valid values for this key are "dgram" and "seqpacket" respectively.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SoftResourceLimits
options.description:
Resource limits to be imposed on the job. These adjust variables set with `setrlimit(2)`.  The following
keys apply:

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SoftResourceLimits.CPU
options.description: The maximum amount of cpu time (in seconds) to be used by each process.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SoftResourceLimits.Core
options.description: The largest size (in bytes) core file that may be created.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SoftResourceLimits.Data
options.description:
The maximum size (in bytes) of the data segment for a process; this defines how far a program may
extend its break with the `sbrk(2)` system call.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SoftResourceLimits.FileSize
options.description: The largest size (in bytes) file that may be created.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SoftResourceLimits.MemoryLock
options.description: The maximum size (in bytes) which a process may lock into memory using the mlock(2) function.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SoftResourceLimits.NumberOfFiles
options.description:
The maximum number of open files for this process.  Setting this value in a system wide daemon
will set the `sysctl(3)` kern.maxfiles (SoftResourceLimits) or kern.maxfilesperproc (HardResourceLimits)
value in addition to the `setrlimit(2)` values.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SoftResourceLimits.NumberOfProcesses
options.description:
The maximum number of simultaneous processes for this user id.  Setting this value in a system
wide daemon will set the `sysctl(3)` kern.maxproc (SoftResourceLimits) or kern.maxprocperuid
(HardResourceLimits) value in addition to the `setrlimit(2)` values.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SoftResourceLimits.ResidentSetSize
options.description:
The maximum size (in bytes) to which a process's resident set size may grow.  This imposes a
limit on the amount of physical memory to be given to a process; if memory is tight, the system
will prefer to take memory from processes that are exceeding their declared resident set size.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.SoftResourceLimits.Stack
options.description:
The maximum size (in bytes) of the stack segment for a process; this defines how far a program's
stack segment may be extended.  Stack extension is performed automatically by the system.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.StandardErrorPath
options.description: This optional key specifies what file should be used for data being sent to stderr when using `stdio(3)`.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.StandardInPath
options.description:
This optional key specifies what file should be used for data being supplied to stdin when using
`stdio(3)`.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.StandardOutPath
options.description: This optional key specifies what file should be used for data being sent to stdout when using `stdio(3)`.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.StartCalendarInterval
options.description:
This optional key causes the job to be started every calendar interval as specified. The semantics are
much like {manpage}`crontab(5)`: Missing attributes are considered to be wildcard. Unlike cron which skips
job invocations when the computer is asleep, launchd will start the job the next time the computer wakes
up.  If multiple intervals transpire before the computer is woken, those events will be coalesced into
one event upon waking from sleep.

::: {.important}
The list must not be empty and must not contain duplicate entries (attrsets which compare equally).
:::

::: {.caution}
Since missing attrs become wildcards, an empty attrset effectively means "every minute".
:::

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.StartInterval
options.description:
This optional key causes the job to be started every N seconds.  If the system is asleep, the job will
be started the next time the computer wakes up.  If multiple intervals transpire before the computer is
woken, those events will be coalesced into one event upon wake from sleep.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.StartOnMount
options.description: This optional key causes the job to be started every time a filesystem is mounted.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.ThrottleInterval
options.description:
This key lets one override the default throttling policy imposed on jobs by launchd.  The value is in
seconds, and by default, jobs will not be spawned more than once every 10 seconds.  The principle
behind this is that jobs should linger around just in case they are needed again in the near future.
This not only reduces the latency of responses, but it encourages developers to amortize the cost of
program invocation.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.TimeOut
options.description:
The recommended idle time out (in seconds) to pass to the job. If no value is specified, a default time
out will be supplied by launchd for use by the job at check in time.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.Umask
options.description:
This optional key specifies what value should be passed to `umask(2)` before running the job. Known bug:
Property lists don't support octal, so please convert the value to decimal.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.UserName
options.description:
This optional key specifies the user to run the job as. This key is only applicable when launchd is
running as root.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.WaitForDebugger
options.description:
This optional key specifies that launchd should instruct the kernel to have the job wait for a debugger
to attach before any code in the job is executed.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.WatchPaths
options.description: This optional key causes the job to be started if any one of the listed paths are modified.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.WorkingDirectory
options.description: This optional key is used to specify a directory to `chdir(2)` to before running the job.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.inetdCompatibility
options.description: The presence of this key specifies that the daemon expects to be run as if it were launched from inetd.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.config.inetdCompatibility.Wait
options.description:
This flag corresponds to the "wait" or "nowait" option of inetd. If true, then the listening
socket is passed via the standard in/out/error file descriptors. If false, then `accept(2)` is
called on behalf of the job, and the result is passed via the standard in/out/error descriptors.

options.title: launchd.agents.<name>.enable
options.description: Whether to enable ‹name›.

options.title: launchd.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable Home Manager to define per-user daemons by making use
of launchd's LaunchAgents.

options.title: lib
options.description:
This option allows modules to define helper functions,
constants, etc.

options.title: manual.html.enable
options.description:
Whether to install the HTML manual. This also installs the
{command}`home-manager-help` tool, which opens a local
copy of the Home Manager manual in the system web browser.

options.title: manual.json.enable
options.description:
Whether to install a JSON formatted list of all Home Manager
options. This can be located at
{file}`<profile directory>/share/doc/home-manager/options.json`,
and may be used for navigating definitions, auto-completing,
and other miscellaneous tasks.

options.title: manual.manpages.enable
options.description:
Whether to install the configuration manual page. The manual can
be reached by {command}`man home-configuration.nix`.

When looking at the manual page pretend that all references to
NixOS stuff are actually references to Home Manager stuff.
Thanks!

options.title: meta.maintainers
options.description:
List of maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

The option value is not a list of maintainers, but an attribute set that maps module file names to lists of maintainers.

options.title: meta.teams
options.description:
List of team maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

options.title: news.display
options.description:
How unread and relevant news should be presented when
running {command}`home-manager build` and
{command}`home-manager switch`.

The options are

`silent`
: Do not print anything during build or switch. The
  {command}`home-manager news` command still
  works for viewing the entries.

`notify`
: The number of unread and relevant news entries will be
  printed to standard output. The {command}`home-manager
  news` command can later be used to view the entries.

`show`
: A pager showing unread news entries is opened.

options.title: nix.buildMachines
options.description:
This option lists the machines to be used if distributed builds are
enabled (see {option}`nix.distributedBuilds`).
Nix will perform derivations on those machines via SSH by copying the
inputs to the Nix store on the remote machine, starting the build,
then copying the output back to the local Nix store.

options.title: nix.buildMachines.*.hostName
options.description: The hostname of the build machine.

options.title: nix.buildMachines.*.mandatoryFeatures
options.description:
A list of features mandatory for this builder. The builder will
be ignored for derivations that don't require all features in
this list. All mandatory features are automatically included in
{var}`supportedFeatures`.

options.title: nix.buildMachines.*.maxJobs
options.description:
The number of concurrent jobs the build machine supports. The
build machine will enforce its own limits, but this allows hydra
to schedule better since there is no work-stealing between build
machines.

options.title: nix.buildMachines.*.protocol
options.description:
The protocol used for communicating with the build machine.
Use `ssh-ng` if your remote builder and your
local Nix version support that improved protocol.

Use `null` when trying to change the special localhost builder
without a protocol which is for example used by hydra.

options.title: nix.buildMachines.*.publicHostKey
options.description:
The (base64-encoded) public host key of this builder. The field
is calculated via {command}`base64 -w0 /etc/ssh/ssh_host_type_key.pub`.
If null, SSH will use its regular known-hosts file when connecting.

options.title: nix.buildMachines.*.speedFactor
options.description:
The relative speed of this builder. This is an arbitrary integer
that indicates the speed of this builder, relative to other
builders. Higher is faster.

options.title: nix.buildMachines.*.sshKey
options.description:
The path to the SSH private key with which to authenticate on
the build machine. The private key must not have a passphrase.
If null, the building user (root on NixOS machines) must have an
appropriate ssh configuration to log in non-interactively.

Note that for security reasons, this path must point to a file
in the local filesystem, *not* to the nix store.

options.title: nix.buildMachines.*.sshUser
options.description:
The username to log in as on the remote host. This user must be
able to log in and run nix commands non-interactively. It must
also be privileged to build derivations, so must be included in
{option}`nix.settings.trusted-users`.

options.title: nix.buildMachines.*.supportedFeatures
options.description:
A list of features supported by this builder. The builder will
be ignored for derivations that require features not in this
list.

options.title: nix.buildMachines.*.systems
options.description: The system types the build machine can execute derivations on.

options.title: nix.channels
options.description:
A declarative alternative to Nix channels. Whereas with stock channels,
you would register URLs and fetch them into the Nix store with
{manpage}`nix-channel(1)`, this option allows you to register the store
path directly. One particularly useful example is registering flake
inputs as channels.

This option can coexist with stock Nix channels. If the same channel is
defined in both, this option takes precedence.

options.title: nix.checkConfig
options.description:
If enabled (the default), checks for data type mismatches and that Nix
can parse the generated nix.conf.

options.title: nix.distributedBuilds
options.description:
Whether to distribute builds to the machines listed in
{option}`nix.buildMachines`.

options.title: nix.extraOptions
options.description: Additional text appended to {file}`nix.conf`.

options.title: nix.gc.automatic
options.description:
Automatically run the garbage collector at a specific time.

Note: This will only garbage collect the current user's profiles.

options.title: nix.gc.dates
options.type: (optionally newline-terminated) single-line string or list of string
options.description:
When to run the Nix garbage collector.

On Linux this is a string as defined by {manpage}`systemd.time(7)`.

On Darwin it must be one of: hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, semiannually, annually, which are implemented as defined in {manpage}`systemd.time(7)`.

options.title: nix.gc.options
options.description:
Options given to {file}`nix-collect-garbage` when the
garbage collector is run automatically.

options.title: nix.gc.persistent
options.description:
If true, the time when the service unit was last triggered is
stored on disk. When the timer is activated, the service unit is
triggered immediately if it would have been triggered at least once
during the time when the timer was inactive.

options.title: nix.gc.randomizedDelaySec
options.description:
Add a randomized delay before each garbage collection.
The delay will be chosen between zero and this value.
This value must be a time span in the format specified by
{manpage}`systemd.time(7)`

options.title: nix.keepOldNixPath
options.description:
Whether {option}`nix.nixPath` should keep the previously set values in
{env}`NIX_PATH`.

options.title: nix.nixPath
options.description:
Adds new directories to the Nix expression search path.

Used by Nix when looking up paths in angular brackets
(e.g. `<nixpkgs>`).

options.title: nix.package
options.description: The nix package to use.

options.title: nix.registry
options.description: User level flake registry.

options.title: nix.registry.<name>.exact
options.description:
Whether the {option}`from` reference needs to match exactly. If set,
a {option}`from` reference like `nixpkgs` does not
match with a reference like `nixpkgs/nixos-20.03`.

options.title: nix.registry.<name>.flake
options.description: The flake input to which {option}`from>` is to be rewritten.

options.title: nix.registry.<name>.from
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or package)
options.description: The flake reference to be rewritten.

options.title: nix.registry.<name>.to
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or package)
options.description: The flake reference to which {option}`from>` is to be rewritten.

options.title: nix.settings
options.type: open submodule of attribute set of (Nix config atom (null, bool, int, float, str, path or package) or list of (Nix config atom (null, bool, int, float, str, path or package)))
options.description:
Configuration for Nix; see {manpage}`nix.conf(5)` for available options.
The value declared here will be translated directly to the key-value pairs Nix expects.

Configuration specified in [](#opt-nix.extraOptions) will be appended
verbatim to the resulting config file.

options.title: nixpkgs.config
options.description:
The configuration of the Nix Packages collection. (For
details, see the Nixpkgs documentation.) It allows you to set
package configuration options.

If `null`, then configuration is taken from
the fallback location, for example,
{file}`~/.config/nixpkgs/config.nix`.

Note, this option will not apply outside your Home Manager
configuration like when installing manually through
{command}`nix-env`. If you want to apply it both
inside and outside Home Manager you can put it in a separate
file and include something like

```nix
  nixpkgs.config = import ./nixpkgs-config.nix;
  xdg.configFile."nixpkgs/config.nix".source = ./nixpkgs-config.nix;
```

in your Home Manager configuration.

options.title: nixpkgs.overlays
options.description:
List of overlays to use with the Nix Packages collection. (For
details, see the Nixpkgs documentation.) It allows you to
override packages globally. This is a function that takes as
an argument the *original* Nixpkgs. The
first argument should be used for finding dependencies, and
the second should be used for overriding recipes.

If `null`, then the overlays are taken from
the fallback location, for example,
{file}`~/.config/nixpkgs/overlays`.

Like {var}`nixpkgs.config` this option only
applies within the Home Manager configuration. See
{var}`nixpkgs.config` for a suggested setup that
works both internally and externally.

options.title: pam.sessionVariables
options.description:
Environment variables that will be set for the PAM session.
The variable values must be as described in
{manpage}`pam_env.conf(5)`.

Note, this option will become deprecated in the future and its use is
therefore discouraged.

options.title: pam.yubico.authorizedYubiKeys.ids
options.description:
List of authorized YubiKey token IDs. Refer to
<https://developers.yubico.com/yubico-pam>

options.title: programs.aerospace.launchd.enable
options.description:
Configure the launchd agent to manage the AeroSpace process.

The first time this is enabled, macOS will prompt you to allow this background
item in System Settings.

You can verify the service is running correctly from your terminal.
Run: `launchctl list | grep aerospace`

- A running process will show a Process ID (PID) and a status of 0, for example:
  `12345	0	org.nix-community.home.aerospace`

- If the service has crashed or failed to start, the PID will be a dash and the
  status will be a non-zero number, for example:
  `-	1	org.nix-community.home.aerospace`

In case of failure, check the logs with `cat /tmp/aerospace.err.log`.

For more detailed service status, run `launchctl print gui/$(id -u)/org.nix-community.home.aerospace`.

NOTE: Enabling this option will configure AeroSpace to **not** manage its own
launchd agent. Specifically, it will set `start-at-login = false` and
`after-login-command = []` in the configuration file, as those are now handled
by Home Manager and launchd instead.

options.description: Whether the launchd service should be kept alive.

options.description:
Path to a file containing the sync account sync key. This is different from
the account password.

To get the sync key, follow these steps:

- Enable this Home Manager module: `programs.anki.enable = true`
- Open Anki.
- Navigate to the sync settings page. (Tools > Preferences > Syncing)
- Log in to your AnkiWeb account.
- Select "Yes" to the prompt about saving preferences and syncing.
- A Home Manager warning prompt will show. Select "Show details...".
- Get your sync key from the message: "syncKey changed from \`None\` to \`<YOUR SYNC KEY WILL BE HERE>\`"

options.description:

options.description:
Whether to enable Fish integration.

If enabled, this will bind the up-arrow key to open the Atuin history.

options.title: programs.atuin.forceOverwriteSettings
options.description:
When enabled, force overwriting of the Atuin configuration file
({file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/atuin/config.toml`).
Any existing Atuin configuration will be lost.

Enabling this is useful when adding settings for the first time
because Atuin writes its default config file after every single
shell command, which can make it difficult to manually remove.

options.description: The atuin package to use.

where the name of each attribute is the theme-name

See <https://docs.atuin.sh/guide/theming/> for the full list
of options.

options.title: programs.autorandr.profiles.<name>.config.<name>.scale
options.description:

Either configure by pixels or a scaling factor. When using pixel method the
{manpage}`xrandr(1)`
option
`--scale-from`
will be used; when using factor method the option
`--scale`
will be used.

This option is a shortcut version of the transform option and they are mutually
exclusive.

options.description: Output scaling method.

options.title: programs.autorandr.profiles.<name>.config.<name>.scale.x
options.type: floating point number or (positive integer, meaning >0)
options.description: Horizontal scaling factor/pixels.

options.title: programs.autorandr.profiles.<name>.config.<name>.scale.y
options.type: floating point number or (positive integer, meaning >0)
options.description: Vertical scaling factor/pixels.

options.title: programs.autorandr.profiles.<name>.config.<name>.transform
options.type: null or (3×3 matrix of floating point numbers)
options.description:
Refer to
{manpage}`xrandr(1)`
for the documentation of the transform matrix.

options.title: programs.awscli.credentials
options.type: open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
options.description:
Configuration written to {file}`$HOME/.aws/credentials`.

For security reasons, never store cleartext passwords here.
We recommend that you use `credential_process` option to retrieve
the IAM credentials from your favorite password manager during runtime,
or use AWS IAM Identity Center to get short-term credentials.

See <https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-chap-authentication.html>.

options.description:

Note, if you use NixOS or nix-darwin and do not have Bash completion
enabled in the system configuration, then make sure to add

```nix

```

to your system configuration to get completion for system packages.
Note, the legacy {file}`/etc/bash_completion.d` path is
not supported by Home Manager.

See the output of `borg help patterns` for the syntax. Pattern paths

options.title: programs.broot.settings.skin
options.description:
Color configuration.

Complete list of keys (expected to change before the v1 of broot):

* `char_match`
* `code`
* `directory`
* `exe`
* `file`
* `file_error`
* `flag_label`
* `flag_value`
* `input`
* `link`
* `permissions`
* `selected_line`
* `size_bar_full`
* `size_bar_void`
* `size_text`
* `spinner`
* `status_error`
* `status_normal`
* `table_border`
* `tree`
* `unlisted`

Add `_fg` for a foreground color and
`_bg` for a background color.

options.title: programs.broot.settings.verbs
options.type: list of attribute set of (boolean or string or list of string)
options.description:
Define new verbs. For more information, see
[Verb Definition Attributes](https://dystroy.org/broot/documentation/configuration/#verb-definition-attributes)
in the broot documentation.

The possible attributes are:

`invocation` (optional)
: how the verb is called by the user, with placeholders for arguments

`execution` (mandatory)
: how the verb is executed

`key` (optional)
: a keyboard key triggering execution

`keys` (optional)
: multiple keyboard keys each triggering execution

`shortcut` (optional)
: an alternate way to call the verb (without
  the arguments part)

`leave_broot` (optional)
: whether to quit broot on execution
  (default: `true`)

`from_shell` (optional)
: whether the verb must be executed from the
  parent shell (default: `false`)

    theme[process_end]="#6272a4"
  '';
}

options.title: programs.claude-code.agents
options.type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path)
options.description:
Custom agents for Claude Code.
The attribute name becomes the agent filename, and the value is either:
- Inline content as a string with frontmatter
- A path to a file containing the agent content with frontmatter
Agents are stored in .claude/agents/ directory.

options.title: programs.claude-code.agentsDir
options.description:
Path to a directory containing agent files for Claude Code.
Agent files from this directory will be symlinked to .claude/agents/.

options.title: programs.claude-code.commands
options.type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path)
options.description:
Custom commands for Claude Code.
The attribute name becomes the command filename, and the value is either:
- Inline content as a string
- A path to a file containing the command content
Commands are stored in .claude/commands/ directory.
options.example:
{
  changelog = ''
    ---
    allowed-tools: Bash(git log:*), Bash(git diff:*)
    argument-hint: [version] [change-type] [message]
    description: Update CHANGELOG.md with new entry
    ---
    Parse the version, change type, and message from the input
    and update the CHANGELOG.md file accordingly.
  '';
  fix-issue = ./commands/fix-issue.md;
  commit = ''
    ---
    allowed-tools: Bash(git add:*), Bash(git status:*), Bash(git commit:*)
    description: Create a git commit with proper message
    ---
    ## Context

    - Current git status: !`git status`
    - Current git diff: !`git diff HEAD`
    - Recent commits: !`git log --oneline -5`

    ## Task

    Based on the changes above, create a single atomic git commit with a descriptive message.
  '';
}

options.example:

''

options.description: JSON configuration for Claude Code settings.json

options.title: programs.claude-code.skills
options.type: attribute set of (strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path)
options.description:
Custom skills for Claude Code.
The attribute name becomes the skill filename or directory name, and the value is either:
- Inline content as a string (creates .claude/skills/<name>.md)
- A path to a file (creates .claude/skills/<name>.md)
- A path to a directory (creates .claude/skills/<name>/ with all files)
options.example:
{
  xlsx = ./skills/xlsx.md;
  data-analysis = ./skills/data-analysis;
  pdf-processing = ''
    ---
    name: pdf-processing
    description: Extract text and tables from PDF files, fill forms, merge documents. Use when working with PDF files or when the user mentions PDFs, forms, or document extraction.
    ---

    # PDF Processing

    ## Quick start

    Use pdfplumber to extract text from PDFs:

    ```python
    import pdfplumber

    with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
        text = pdf.pages[0].extract_text()
    ```
  '';
}

options.description: List each file's Git status if tracked or ignored ({option}`--git` argument).

options.title: programs.eza.icons
options.description:
Display icons next to file names ({option}`--icons` argument).

Note, the support for Boolean values is deprecated.
Setting this option to `true` corresponds to `--icons=auto`.

options.description: The eza package to use.

options.description:

options.description: Whether to enable Fabric's `yt` alias.

options.title: programs.fabric-ai.enableZshIntegration
options.description: Whether to enable Zsh integration.

options.description: The fabric-ai package to use.

options.title: programs.fastfetch.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Fastfetch.

options.description: Whether to enable fd, a simple, fast and user-friendly alternative to {command}`find`.

options.description: Extra command line options passed to fd.

options.description: Search hidden files and directories ({option}`--hidden` argument).

options.title: programs.fd.ignores
options.description: List of paths that should be globally ignored.

options.title: programs.fd.package
options.description: The fd package to use.

options.title: programs.feh.buttons
options.type: attribute set of (null or string or signed integer or list of (string or signed integer))
options.description:
Override feh's default mouse button mapping. If you want to disable an
action, set its value to null. If you want to bind multiple buttons to
an action, set its value to a list.
See <https://man.finalrewind.org/1/feh/#BUTTONS_CONFIG_SYNTAX> for
default bindings and available commands.

options.title: programs.feh.enable
options.description: Whether to enable feh - a fast and light image viewer.

options.title: programs.feh.keybindings
options.type: attribute set of (null or string or list of string)
options.description:
Override feh's default keybindings. If you want to disable a keybinding
set its value to null. If you want to bind multiple keys to an action,
set its value to a list.
See <https://man.finalrewind.org/1/feh/#KEYS_CONFIG_SYNTAX> for
default bindings and available commands.

options.title: programs.feh.package
options.description: The feh package to use.

options.title: programs.feh.themes
options.type: attribute set of list of string
options.description:
Define themes for feh.
See <https://man.finalrewind.org/1/feh/#THEMES_CONFIG_SYNTAX> for
important guidelines and limitations related to theme configuration.

options.title: programs.firefox.darwinDefaultsId
options.description: The id for the darwin defaults in order to set policies

options.title: programs.firefox.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Firefox.

options.title: programs.firefox.enableGnomeExtensions
options.description:
Whether to enable the GNOME Shell native host connector. Note, you
also need to set the NixOS option
`services.gnome.gnome-browser-connector.enable` to
`true`.

options.title: programs.firefox.finalPackage
options.description: Resulting Firefox package.

options.title: programs.firefox.languagePacks
options.description:
The language packs to install. Available language codes can be found
on the releases page:
`https://releases.mozilla.org/pub/firefox/releases/${version}/linux-x86_64/xpi/`,
replacing `${version}` with the version of Firefox you have. If
the version string of your Firefox derivative diverts from the upstream
version, try setting the `release` option.

options.title: programs.firefox.nativeMessagingHosts
options.description:
Additional packages containing native messaging hosts that should be
made available to Firefox extensions.

options.title: programs.firefox.package
options.description:
The Firefox package to use. If state version ≥ 19.09 then
this should be a wrapped Firefox package. For earlier state
versions it should be an unwrapped Firefox package.
Set to `null` to disable installing Firefox.

options.title: programs.firefox.pkcs11Modules
options.description: Additional packages to be loaded as PKCS #11 modules in Firefox.

options.title: programs.firefox.policies
options.description: [See list of policies](https://mozilla.github.io/policy-templates/).

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles
options.description: Attribute set of Firefox profiles.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.bookmarks
options.type: (submodule) or ((list of ((bookmark submodule) or (directory submodule) or value "separator" (singular enum))) or (attribute set of ((bookmark submodule) or (directory submodule) or value "separator" (singular enum))) convertible to it) convertible to it
options.description: Declarative bookmarks.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.bookmarks.configFile
options.description: Configuration file to define custom bookmarks.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.bookmarks.force
options.description: Whether to force override existing custom bookmarks.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.bookmarks.meta.maintainers
options.description:
List of maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

The option value is not a list of maintainers, but an attribute set that maps module file names to lists of maintainers.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.bookmarks.meta.teams
options.description:
List of team maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.bookmarks.settings
options.type: (list of ((bookmark submodule) or (directory submodule) or value "separator" (singular enum))) or (attribute set of ((bookmark submodule) or (directory submodule) or value "separator" (singular enum))) convertible to it
options.description: Custom bookmarks.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.containers
options.description:
Attribute set of container configurations. See
[Multi-Account
Containers](https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/containers)
for more information.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.containers.<name>.color
options.description: Container color.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.containers.<name>.icon
options.description: Container icon.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.containers.<name>.id
options.description: Container ID. This should be set to a unique number per container in this profile.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.containers.<name>.name
options.description: Container name, e.g., shopping.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.containersForce
options.description:
Whether to force replace the existing containers configuration.
This is recommended since Firefox will replace the symlink on
every launch, but note that you'll lose any existing configuration
by enabling this.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.extensions
options.type: (submodule) or (list of package) convertible to it
options.description: Submodule for installing and configuring extensions.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.extensions.exactPermissions
options.description:
When enabled,
{option}`programs.firefox.profiles.<profile>.extensions.settings.<extensionID>.permissions`
must specify the exact set of permissions that the
extension will request.

This means that if the authorized permissions are
broader than what the extension requests, the
assertion will fail.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.extensions.exhaustivePermissions
options.description:
When enabled, the user must authorize requested
permissions for all extensions from
{option}`programs.firefox.profiles.<profile>.extensions.packages`
in
{option}`programs.firefox.profiles.<profile>.extensions.settings.<extensionID>.permissions`

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.extensions.force
options.description:
Whether to override all previous firefox settings.

This is required when using `settings`.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.extensions.packages
options.description:
List of ‹name› add-on packages to install for this profile.
Some pre-packaged add-ons are accessible from the Nix User Repository.
Once you have NUR installed run

```console
$ nix-env -f '<nixpkgs>' -qaP -A nur.repos.rycee.firefox-addons
```

to list the available ‹name› add-ons.

Note that it is necessary to manually enable these extensions
inside ‹name› after the first installation.

To automatically enable extensions add
`"extensions.autoDisableScopes" = 0;`
to
[{option}`programs.firefox.profiles.<profile>.settings`](#opt-programs.firefox.profiles._name_.settings)

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.extensions.settings
options.description:
Attribute set of options for each extension.
The keys of the attribute set consist of the ID of the extension
or its UUID wrapped in curly braces.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.extensions.settings.<name>.force
options.description:
Forcibly override any existing configuration for
this extension.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.extensions.settings.<name>.permissions
options.description:
Allowed permissions for this extension. See
<https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/manifest.json/permissions>
for a list of relevant permissions.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.extensions.settings.<name>.settings
options.description: Json formatted options for this extension.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.extraConfig
options.description: Extra preferences to add to {file}`user.js`.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.id
options.description: Profile ID. This should be set to a unique number per profile.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.isDefault
options.description: Whether this is a default profile.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.name
options.description: Profile name.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.path
options.description: Profile path.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.preConfig
options.description:
Extra preferences to add to {file}`user.js`, before
[](#opt-programs.firefox.profiles._name_.settings).

Use [](#opt-programs.firefox.profiles._name_.extraConfig), unless
you want to overwrite in
[](#opt-programs.firefox.profiles._name_.settings), then use this
option.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.search
options.description: Declarative search engine configuration.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.search.default
options.description:
The default search engine used in the address bar and search
bar.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.search.engines
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (JSON value)
options.description:
Attribute set of search engine configurations. Engines that
only have {var}`metaData` specified will be treated as builtin
to Firefox.

See [SearchEngine.jsm](https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/rev/e3f42ec9320748b2aab3d474d1e47075def9000c/toolkit/components/search/SearchEngine.sys.mjs#890-923)
in Firefox's source for available options. We maintain a
mapping to let you specify all options in the referenced link
without underscores, but it may fall out of date with future
options.

Note, {var}`icon` is also a special option added by Home
Manager to make it convenient to specify absolute icon paths.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.search.force
options.description:
Whether to force replace the existing search
configuration. This is recommended since Firefox will
replace the symlink for the search configuration on every
launch, but note that you'll lose any existing configuration
by enabling this.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.search.meta.maintainers
options.description:
List of maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

The option value is not a list of maintainers, but an attribute set that maps module file names to lists of maintainers.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.search.meta.teams
options.description:
List of team maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.search.order
options.description:
The order the search engines are listed in. Any engines that
aren't included in this list will be listed after these in an
unspecified order.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.search.privateDefault
options.description: The default search engine used in the Private Browsing.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.settings
options.type: attribute set of (Firefox preference (int, bool, string, and also attrs, list, float as a JSON string))
options.description:
Attribute set of Firefox preferences.

Firefox only supports int, bool, and string types for
preferences, but home-manager will automatically
convert all other JSON-compatible values into strings.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.userChrome
options.type: strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path
options.description: Custom Firefox user chrome CSS.

options.title: programs.firefox.profiles.<name>.userContent
options.type: strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path
options.description: Custom Firefox user content CSS.

options.description: Whether to enable Progressive Web Apps for Firefox.

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.package
options.description: The firefoxpwa package to use.

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles
options.description:
Attribute set of profile options. The keys of that attribute set consist of
ULIDs. A ULID is made of 26 characters, each of which is one of
'0123456789ABCDEFGHJKMNPQRSTVWXYZ' (Excluding I, L, O and U). See
<https://github.com/ulid/spec?tab=readme-ov-file#canonical-string-representation>.

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles.<name>.name
options.description: Name of the profile.

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles.<name>.settings
options.description:
Settings for this profile. See
<https://github.com/filips123/PWAsForFirefox/blob/cb4fc76873cc51129d9290754768e6a340c521b2/native/src/components/profile.rs#L13-L34>
for a list of available options.

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles.<name>.sites
options.description:
Attribute set of site options for this profile. The keys of that attribute set
consist of ULIDs. A ULID is made of 26 characters, each of which is one of
'0123456789ABCDEFGHJKMNPQRSTVWXYZ' (Excluding I, L, O and U). See
<https://github.com/ulid/spec?tab=readme-ov-file#canonical-string-representation>.
Site ULIDs must be unique across profiles.

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles.<name>.sites.<name>.desktopEntry.categories

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles.<name>.sites.<name>.desktopEntry.enable

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles.<name>.sites.<name>.desktopEntry.icon

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles.<name>.sites.<name>.manifestUrl

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles.<name>.sites.<name>.name
options.description: Name of the site.

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles.<name>.sites.<name>.settings
options.description:
Settings for this site. See
<https://github.com/filips123/PWAsForFirefox/blob/cb4fc76873cc51129d9290754768e6a340c521b2/native/src/components/site.rs#L98-L115>

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.profiles.<name>.sites.<name>.url
options.description: Start URL of the site.

options.title: programs.firefoxpwa.settings
options.description:
Settings to be written to the configuration file. See

If enabled, abbreviations will be preferred over aliases when

Shell script code called during fish shell

options.title: programs.fish.shellInitLast
options.description:
Shell script code called during interactive fish shell

bar.

options.title: programs.floorp.profiles.<name>.search.engines
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (JSON value)
options.description:
Attribute set of search engine configurations. Engines that
only have {var}`metaData` specified will be treated as builtin
to Floorp.

See [SearchEngine.jsm](https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/rev/e3f42ec9320748b2aab3d474d1e47075def9000c/toolkit/components/search/SearchEngine.sys.mjs#890-923)
in Floorp's source for available options. We maintain a
mapping to let you specify all options in the referenced link
without underscores, but it may fall out of date with future
options.

Note, {var}`icon` is also a special option added by Home
Manager to make it convenient to specify absolute icon paths.

options.title: programs.floorp.profiles.<name>.search.force
options.description:
Whether to force replace the existing search
configuration. This is recommended since Floorp will
replace the symlink for the search configuration on every
launch, but note that you'll lose any existing configuration
by enabling this.

options.title: programs.floorp.profiles.<name>.search.meta.maintainers
options.description:
List of maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

The option value is not a list of maintainers, but an attribute set that maps module file names to lists of maintainers.

options.title: programs.floorp.profiles.<name>.search.meta.teams
options.description:
List of team maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

options.title: programs.floorp.profiles.<name>.search.order
options.description:
The order the search engines are listed in. Any engines that
aren't included in this list will be listed after these in an
unspecified order.

options.title: programs.floorp.profiles.<name>.search.privateDefault
options.description: The default search engine used in the Private Browsing.

options.title: programs.floorp.profiles.<name>.settings
options.type: attribute set of (Floorp preference (int, bool, string, and also attrs, list, float as a JSON string))
options.description:
Attribute set of Floorp preferences.

Floorp only supports int, bool, and string types for
preferences, but home-manager will automatically
convert all other JSON-compatible values into strings.

options.title: programs.floorp.profiles.<name>.userChrome
options.type: strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path
options.description: Custom Floorp user chrome CSS.

options.title: programs.floorp.profiles.<name>.userContent
options.type: strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path
options.description: Custom Floorp user content CSS.

<https://ghostty.org/docs/features/shell-integration#manual-shell-integration-setup>

every time a new commit is checked out on your repository.

options.description:
Enable the automatic {command}`git maintenance`.

If you have SSH remotes, set {option}`programs.git.package` to a
git version with SSH support (eg: `pkgs.gitFull`).

See <https://git-scm.com/docs/git-maintenance>.

options.title: programs.git.maintenance.repositories
options.description:
Repositories on which {command}`git maintenance` should run.

Should be a list of absolute paths.

options.title: programs.git.maintenance.timers
options.description:
Systemd timers to create for scheduled {command}`git maintenance`.

Key is passed to `--schedule` argument in {command}`git maintenance run`
and value is passed to `Timer.OnCalendar` in `systemd.user.timers`.

options.title: programs.git.package
options.description:
The git package to use. Use {var}`pkgs.gitFull`
to gain access to {command}`git send-email` for instance.

options.title: programs.git.settings
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (string or boolean or signed integer or list of (string or boolean or signed integer) or attribute set of (string or boolean or signed integer or list of (string or boolean or signed integer)))
options.description:
Configuration written to {file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/git/config`.
See {manpage}`git-config(1)` for details.

options.title: programs.git.signing.format
options.description:
The signing method to use when signing commits and tags.
Valid values are `openpgp` (OpenPGP/GnuPG), `ssh` (SSH), and `x509` (X.509 certificates).
options.default:
"openpgp" for state version < 25.05,
undefined for state version ≥ 25.05

options.title: programs.git.signing.key
options.description:
The default signing key fingerprint.

Set to `null` to let the signer decide what signing key
to use depending on commit’s author.

options.title: programs.git.signing.signByDefault
options.description: Whether commits and tags should be signed by default.

options.title: programs.git.signing.signer

options.description: Whether to enable gitui, blazing fast terminal-ui for git written in rust.

options.title: programs.gitui.keyConfig
options.type: absolute path or strings concatenated with "\n"
options.description:
Key config in Ron file format. This is written to
{file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/gitui/key_config.ron`.
options.example:
''
  exit: Some(( code: Char('c'), modifiers: ( bits: 2,),)),
  quit: Some(( code: Char('q'), modifiers: ( bits: 0,),)),
  exit_popup: Some(( code: Esc, modifiers: ( bits: 0,),)),
''

options.title: programs.gitui.package
options.description: The gitui package to use.

options.title: programs.gitui.theme
options.type: absolute path or strings concatenated with "\n"
options.description:
Theme in Ron file format. This is written to
{file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/gitui/theme.ron`.
options.default:
''
  (
    selected_tab: Reset,
    command_fg: White,
    selection_bg: Blue,
    selection_fg: White,
    cmdbar_bg: Blue,
    cmdbar_extra_lines_bg: Blue,
    disabled_fg: DarkGray,
    diff_line_add: Green,
    diff_line_delete: Red,
    diff_file_added: LightGreen,
    diff_file_removed: LightRed,
    diff_file_moved: LightMagenta,
    diff_file_modified: Yellow,
    commit_hash: Magenta,
    commit_time: LightCyan,
    commit_author: Green,
    danger_fg: Red,
    push_gauge_bg: Blue,
    push_gauge_fg: Reset,
    tag_fg: LightMagenta,
    branch_fg: LightYellow,
  )
''

A set of Gnome Terminal profiles. Note, the name of a profile must be

(from `util-linux`).

If `true`, allow applications in the
terminal to make text boldface.

options.title: programs.gnome-terminal.profile.<name>.backspaceBinding
options.description:
Which string the terminal should send to an application when the user
presses the *Backspace* key.

`auto`
: Attempt to determine the right value from the terminal's IO settings.

`ascii-backspace`
: Send an ASCII backspace character (`0x08`).

`ascii-delete`
: Send an ASCII delete character (`0x7F`).

`delete-sequence`
: Send the `@7` control sequence.

`tty`
: Send terminal's "erase" setting.

options.title: programs.gnome-terminal.profile.<name>.colors.boldColor

options.title: programs.gnome-terminal.profile.<name>.colors.cursor.foreground
options.description: The foreground color.

options.title: programs.gnome-terminal.profile.<name>.colors.foregroundColor
options.description: The foreground color.

options.description: The foreground color.

options.description: The command to use to start the shell, or null for default shell.

options.title: programs.gnome-terminal.profile.<name>.default
options.description: Whether this should be the default profile.

`auto`
: Send the `@7` control sequence.

`ascii-backspace`
: Send an ASCII backspace character (`0x08`).

`ascii-delete`

`delete-sequence`
: Send the `@7` control sequence.

`tty`
: Send terminal's "erase" setting.

options.title: programs.gpg.enable
options.description: Whether to enable GnuPG.

options.title: programs.gpg.homedir
options.description: Directory to store keychains and configuration.

options.title: programs.gpg.mutableKeys
options.description:
If set to `true`, you may manage your keyring as a user
using the `gpg` command. Upon activation, the keyring
will have managed keys added without overwriting unmanaged keys.

If set to `false`, the path
{file}`$GNUPGHOME/pubring.kbx` will become an immutable
link to the Nix store, denying modifications.

options.title: programs.gpg.mutableTrust
options.description:
If set to `true`, you may manage trust as a user using
the {command}`gpg` command. Upon activation, trusted keys have
their trust set without overwriting unmanaged keys.

If set to `false`, the path
{file}`$GNUPGHOME/trustdb.gpg` will be
*overwritten* on each activation, removing trust for
any unmanaged keys. Be careful to make a backup of your old
{file}`trustdb.gpg` before switching to immutable trust!

options.title: programs.gpg.package
options.description: The gnupg package to use. Also used by the gpg-agent service.

options.title: programs.gpg.publicKeys
options.description:
A list of public keys to be imported into GnuPG. Note, these key files
will be copied into the world-readable Nix store.

options.title: programs.gpg.publicKeys.*.source
options.description: Path of an OpenPGP public key file.

options.title: programs.gpg.publicKeys.*.text
options.description: Text of an OpenPGP public key.

options.title: programs.gpg.publicKeys.*.trust
options.description:
The amount of trust you have in the key ownership and the care the
owner puts into signing other keys. The available levels are

`unknown` or `1`
: I don't know or won't say.

`never` or `2`
: I do **not** trust.

`marginal` or `3`
: I trust marginally.

`full` or `4`
: I trust fully.

`ultimate` or `5`
: I trust ultimately.

See the [Key Management chapter](https://www.gnupg.org/gph/en/manual/x334.html)
of the GNU Privacy Handbook for more.

options.title: programs.hexchat.channels.<name>.loginMethod
options.description:
The login method. The allowed options are:

`null`
:  Default

`"nickServMsg"`
:  NickServ (`/MSG NickServ` + password)

`"nickServ"`
:  NickServ (`/NICKSERV` + password)

`"challengeAuth"`
:  Challenge Auth (username + password)

`"sasl"`
:  SASL (username + password)

`"serverPassword"`
:  Server password (`/PASS` password)

`"saslExternal"`
:  SASL EXTERNAL (cert)

`"customCommands"`
: Use "commands" field for auth. For example
  ```nix
  commands = [ "/msg NickServ IDENTIFY my_password" ]
  ```

options.title: programs.hexchat.enable
options.description: Whether to enable HexChat, a graphical IRC client.

options.title: programs.hexchat.overwriteConfigFiles
options.description:
Enables overwriting HexChat configuration files
({file}`hexchat.conf`, {file}`servlist.conf`).
Any existing HexChat configuration will be lost. Make sure to back up
any previous configuration before enabling this.

Enabling this setting is recommended, because everytime HexChat
application is closed it overwrites Nix/Home Manager provided
configuration files, causing:

1. Nix/Home Manager provided configuration to be out of sync with
   actual active HexChat configuration.
2. Nix/Home Manager updates to be blocked until configuration files are
   manually removed.

options.title: programs.hyprlock.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable Hyprlock, Hyprland's GPU-accelerated lock screen
utility.

Note that PAM must be configured to enable hyprlock to perform
authentication. The package installed through home-manager will *not* be
able to unlock the session without this configuration.

On NixOS, it can be enabled using:

```nix
security.pam.services.hyprlock = {};
```

<https://wiki.hypr.land/Hypr-Ecosystem/hyprlock/> for more examples.

options.title: programs.keepassxc.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable KeePassXC.

::: {.note}
When this flag is set, KeePassXC' builtin native messaging manifest for
communication with its browser extension is automatically installed.
This conflicts with KeePassXC' builtin installation mechanism. To
prevent error messages, either set
{option}`programs.keepassxc.settings.Browser.UpdateBinaryPath` to
`false`, or untick the checkbox

  Application Settings/
    Browser Integration/
      Advanced/
        Update native messaging manifest files at startup

in the GUI.
:::

{manpage}`strftime(3)`.

options.title: programs.khal.locale.datetimeformat
options.description:
khal will display and understand all datetimes in this format.

{manpage}`strftime(3)`.

options.description:

If `null`, the timezone of your computer will be used.

options.description: The first day of the week, where Monday is 0 and Sunday is 6.

options.description:

If `null`, the timezone of your computer will be used.

options.description:
khal will display and understand all dates in this format.
It should contain a year (e.g. `%Y`).

Format strings are for Python `strftime`, similarly to
{manpage}`strftime(3)`.

options.title: programs.khal.locale.longdatetimeformat
options.description:
khal will display and understand all datetimes in this format.
It should contain a year (e.g. `%Y`).

Format strings are for Python `strftime`, similarly to
{manpage}`strftime(3)`.

options.title: programs.khal.locale.timeformat
options.description:
khal will display and understand all times in this format.

Format strings are for Python `strftime`, similarly to
{manpage}`strftime(3)`.

options.title: programs.khal.locale.unicode_symbols
options.description:
By default khal uses some Unicode symbols (as in "non-ASCII") as
indicators for things like repeating events.
If your font, encoding etc. does not support those symbols, set this
to false (this will enable ASCII-based replacements).

options.title: programs.khal.locale.weeknumbers
options.description:
Enable week numbers in calendar and interactive (ikhal) mode.
As those are ISO week numbers, they only work properly if
{option}`firstweekday` is set to 0.

settings as lower level key/value pairs.

Kodi will still show the settings of plugins configured via this
mechanism in the GUI and they appear to be mutable. This however is
not the case and the settings will stay as specified via Home Manager.

options.type: null or attribute sets or lists of strings
options.description:

file in kodis userdata directory. Settings specified here will be
immutable from inside kodi and be hidden from the GUI settings dialog.

See <https://kodi.wiki/view/Advancedsettings.xml> as
reference for how settings need to be specified.

The innermost attributes must be of type str.

options.title: programs.kodi.sources
options.type: null or attribute sets or lists of strings
options.description:
Contents to populate the file `sources.xml` in kodis
userdata directory.

See <https://kodi.wiki/view/Sources.xml> as
reference for how sources need to be specified.

Kodi will still show the dialogs to modify sources in the GUI and they
appear to be mutable. This however is not the case and the sources will
stay as specified via Home Manager.

The innermost attributes must be of type str.

Keys to bind to command line commands which can only be one of the

options.description:
Commands to declare. Commands set to null or an empty string are

options.description: Custom lfrc lines.

options.description: The lf package to use.
