options.description:
Key to bind to the script at {var}`previewer.source` and
pipe through less. Setting to null will not bind any key.

options.title: programs.lf.previewer.source
options.description:
Script or executable to use to preview files. Sets lf's
{var}`previewer` option.

options.title: programs.lf.settings
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or list of (string or signed integer) or boolean)
options.description:
An attribute set of lf settings. See the lf documentation for
detailed descriptions of these options. Prefer
{option}`programs.lf.previewer.*` for setting lf's {var}`previewer`
option. All string options are quoted with double quotes.

options.title: programs.librewolf.darwinDefaultsId
options.description: The id for the darwin defaults in order to set policies

options.title: programs.librewolf.enable
options.description: Whether to enable LibreWolf. LibreWolf is a privacy enhanced Firefox fork.

options.title: programs.librewolf.enableGnomeExtensions
options.description:
Whether to enable the GNOME Shell native host connector. Note, you
also need to set the NixOS option
`services.gnome.gnome-browser-connector.enable` to
`true`.

options.title: programs.librewolf.finalPackage
options.description: Resulting LibreWolf package.

options.title: programs.librewolf.languagePacks
options.description:
The language packs to install. Available language codes can be found
on the releases page:
`https://releases.mozilla.org/pub/firefox/releases/${version}/linux-x86_64/xpi/`,
replacing `${version}` with the version of LibreWolf you have. If
the version string of your Firefox derivative diverts from the upstream
version, try setting the `release` option.

options.title: programs.librewolf.nativeMessagingHosts
options.description:
Additional packages containing native messaging hosts that should be
made available to LibreWolf extensions.

options.title: programs.librewolf.package
options.description:
The LibreWolf package to use. If state version ≥ 19.09 then
this should be a wrapped LibreWolf package. For earlier state
versions it should be an unwrapped LibreWolf package.
Set to `null` to disable installing LibreWolf.

options.title: programs.librewolf.pkcs11Modules
options.description: Additional packages to be loaded as PKCS #11 modules in Firefox.

options.title: programs.librewolf.policies
options.description: [See list of policies](https://mozilla.github.io/policy-templates/).

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles
options.description: Attribute set of LibreWolf profiles.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.bookmarks
options.type: (submodule) or ((list of ((bookmark submodule) or (directory submodule) or value "separator" (singular enum))) or (attribute set of ((bookmark submodule) or (directory submodule) or value "separator" (singular enum))) convertible to it) convertible to it
options.description: Declarative bookmarks.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.bookmarks.configFile
options.description: Configuration file to define custom bookmarks.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.bookmarks.force
options.description: Whether to force override existing custom bookmarks.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.bookmarks.meta.maintainers
options.description:
List of maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

The option value is not a list of maintainers, but an attribute set that maps module file names to lists of maintainers.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.bookmarks.meta.teams
options.description:
List of team maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.bookmarks.settings
options.type: (list of ((bookmark submodule) or (directory submodule) or value "separator" (singular enum))) or (attribute set of ((bookmark submodule) or (directory submodule) or value "separator" (singular enum))) convertible to it
options.description: Custom bookmarks.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.containers
options.description:
Attribute set of container configurations. See
[Multi-Account
Containers](https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/containers)
for more information.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.containers.<name>.color
options.description: Container color.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.containers.<name>.icon
options.description: Container icon.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.containers.<name>.id
options.description: Container ID. This should be set to a unique number per container in this profile.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.containers.<name>.name
options.description: Container name, e.g., shopping.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.containersForce
options.description:
Whether to force replace the existing containers configuration.
This is recommended since LibreWolf will replace the symlink on
every launch, but note that you'll lose any existing configuration
by enabling this.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.extensions
options.type: (submodule) or (list of package) convertible to it
options.description: Submodule for installing and configuring extensions.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.extensions.exactPermissions
options.description:
When enabled,
{option}`programs.librewolf.profiles.<profile>.extensions.settings.<extensionID>.permissions`
must specify the exact set of permissions that the
extension will request.

This means that if the authorized permissions are
broader than what the extension requests, the
assertion will fail.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.extensions.exhaustivePermissions
options.description:
When enabled, the user must authorize requested
permissions for all extensions from
{option}`programs.librewolf.profiles.<profile>.extensions.packages`
in
{option}`programs.librewolf.profiles.<profile>.extensions.settings.<extensionID>.permissions`

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.extensions.force
options.description:
Whether to override all previous firefox settings.

This is required when using `settings`.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.extensions.packages
options.description:
List of ‹name› add-on packages to install for this profile.
Some pre-packaged add-ons are accessible from the Nix User Repository.
Once you have NUR installed run

```console
$ nix-env -f '<nixpkgs>' -qaP -A nur.repos.rycee.firefox-addons
```

to list the available ‹name› add-ons.

Note that it is necessary to manually enable these extensions
inside ‹name› after the first installation.

To automatically enable extensions add
`"extensions.autoDisableScopes" = 0;`
to
[{option}`programs.librewolf.profiles.<profile>.settings`](#opt-programs.librewolf.profiles._name_.settings)

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.extensions.settings
options.description:
Attribute set of options for each extension.
The keys of the attribute set consist of the ID of the extension
or its UUID wrapped in curly braces.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.extensions.settings.<name>.force
options.description:
Forcibly override any existing configuration for
this extension.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.extensions.settings.<name>.permissions
options.description:
Allowed permissions for this extension. See
<https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Mozilla/Add-ons/WebExtensions/manifest.json/permissions>
for a list of relevant permissions.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.extensions.settings.<name>.settings
options.description: Json formatted options for this extension.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.extraConfig
options.description: Extra preferences to add to {file}`user.js`.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.id
options.description: Profile ID. This should be set to a unique number per profile.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.isDefault
options.description: Whether this is a default profile.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.name
options.description: Profile name.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.path
options.description: Profile path.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.preConfig
options.description:
Extra preferences to add to {file}`user.js`, before
[](#opt-programs.firefox.profiles._name_.settings).

Use [](#opt-programs.firefox.profiles._name_.extraConfig), unless
you want to overwrite in
[](#opt-programs.firefox.profiles._name_.settings), then use this
option.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.search
options.description: Declarative search engine configuration.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.search.default
options.description:
The default search engine used in the address bar and search
bar.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.search.engines
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (JSON value)
options.description:
Attribute set of search engine configurations. Engines that
only have {var}`metaData` specified will be treated as builtin
to LibreWolf.

See [SearchEngine.jsm](https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/rev/e3f42ec9320748b2aab3d474d1e47075def9000c/toolkit/components/search/SearchEngine.sys.mjs#890-923)
in LibreWolf's source for available options. We maintain a
mapping to let you specify all options in the referenced link
without underscores, but it may fall out of date with future
options.

Note, {var}`icon` is also a special option added by Home
Manager to make it convenient to specify absolute icon paths.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.search.force
options.description:
Whether to force replace the existing search
configuration. This is recommended since LibreWolf will
replace the symlink for the search configuration on every
launch, but note that you'll lose any existing configuration
by enabling this.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.search.meta.maintainers
options.description:
List of maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

The option value is not a list of maintainers, but an attribute set that maps module file names to lists of maintainers.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.search.meta.teams
options.description:
List of team maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.search.order
options.description:
The order the search engines are listed in. Any engines that
aren't included in this list will be listed after these in an
unspecified order.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.search.privateDefault
options.description: The default search engine used in the Private Browsing.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.settings
options.type: attribute set of (LibreWolf preference (int, bool, string, and also attrs, list, float as a JSON string))
options.description:
Attribute set of LibreWolf preferences.

LibreWolf only supports int, bool, and string types for
preferences, but home-manager will automatically
convert all other JSON-compatible values into strings.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.userChrome
options.type: strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path
options.description: Custom LibreWolf user chrome CSS.

options.title: programs.librewolf.profiles.<name>.userContent
options.type: strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path
options.description: Custom LibreWolf user content CSS.

options.title: programs.librewolf.settings
options.type: attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or string)
options.description:
Attribute set of global LibreWolf settings and overrides. Refer to
<https://librewolf.net/docs/settings/>
for details on supported values.

options.description: looking-glass-client settings.

If this option is non-empty then the `color.theme` option is

options.description:
Configuration written to
{file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/lsd/config.yaml`. See
<https://github.com/Peltoche/lsd#config-file-content>

options.title: programs.lutris.defaultWinePackage
options.description:
The wine/proton package to set as the default for lutris.
It must still be set under proton/winePackages.

options.title: programs.lutris.enable
options.description: Whether to enable lutris..

options.title: programs.lutris.extraPackages
options.description:
List of packages to pass as extraPkgs to lutris.
Please note runners are not detected properly this way, use a proper option for those.

options.title: programs.lutris.package
options.description: The lutris package to use.

options.title: programs.lutris.protonPackages
options.description: List of proton packages to be added for lutris to use with umu-launcher.

options.title: programs.lutris.runners
options.description:
Attribute set of Lutris runners along with their configurations.
Each runner must be named exactly as lutris expects on `lutris --list-runners`.
Note that runners added here won't be configurable through Lutris using the GUI.

options.title: programs.lutris.runners.<name>.package
options.description:
The package to use for this runner, nix will try to find the executable for this package.
A more specific path can be set by using settings.runner.runner_executable instead.
Uncompatible with certain runners, such as wine.

options.title: programs.lutris.runners.<name>.settings
options.description: Settings passed directly to lutris for this runner's config at XDG_CONFIG/lutris/runners.

options.title: programs.lutris.runners.<name>.settings.runner
options.description:
Runner specific options.
For references, you must look for the file of said runner on lutris' source code.

options.title: programs.lutris.runners.<name>.settings.runner.runner_executable
options.description:
Specific option to point to a runner executable directly, don't set runner.package if you set this.
Uncompatible with certain runners such as wine.

options.title: programs.lutris.runners.<name>.settings.system
options.description:
Lutris system options for this runner.
Reference for system options:
https://github.com/lutris/lutris/blob/master/lutris/sysoptions.py#L78

options.title: programs.lutris.steamPackage
options.description:
This must be the same you use for your system, or two instances will conflict,
for example, if you configure steam through the nixos module, a good value is "osConfig.programs.steam.package"

options.title: programs.lutris.winePackages
options.description: List of wine packages to be added for lutris to use.

options.title: programs.man.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable manual pages and the {command}`man`
command. This also includes "man" outputs of all
`home.packages`.

options.title: programs.man.extraConfig
options.description: Additional fields to be added to the end of the user manpath config file.

options.title: programs.man.generateCaches
options.description:
Whether to generate the manual page index caches using
{manpage}`mandb(8)`. This allows searching for a page or
keyword using utilities like {manpage}`apropos(1)`.

This feature is disabled by default because it slows down
building. If you don't mind waiting a few more seconds when
Home Manager builds a new generation, you may safely enable
this option.

options.title: programs.man.package
options.description: The man package to use.

options.title: programs.mangohud.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Mangohud.

options.title: programs.mangohud.enableSessionWide
options.description:
Sets environment variables so that
MangoHud is started on any application that supports it.

options.title: programs.mangohud.package
options.description: The mangohud package to use.

options.description:

options.title: programs.mangohud.settingsPerApplication

options.title: programs.mc.extensionSettings
options.type: attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
options.description:
Settings for `mc/mc.ext.ini` file. This setting completely replaces the default `/etc/mc/mc.ext.ini`.

Midnight Commander does not merge this file with the system default,
so you should copy the original if you want to preserve default behavior and add your changes there.

options.title: programs.mc.fileHighlightSettings
options.type: attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
options.description:
Settings for `mc/filehighlight.ini` file. This setting completely replaces the default `/etc/mc/filehighlight.ini`.

Midnight Commander does not merge this file with the system default, so you should copy the original if you want to preserve default behavior
and add your changes there.

options.title: programs.mc.keymapSettings
options.type: attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
options.description:
Settings for `mc/mc.keymap` file.

Any missing settings will fall back to the system default.

options.title: programs.mc.package
options.description: The mc package to use.

options.title: programs.mc.panelsSettings

options.description:

options.title: programs.mc.settings
options.type: attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
options.description:
Settings for `mc/ini` file.

Any missing settings will fall back to the system default.

options.title: programs.mcfly.enable
options.description: Whether to enable mcfly.

options.title: programs.mcfly.enableBashIntegration

options.title: programs.mcfly.enableFishIntegration

options.title: programs.mcfly.enableLightTheme

options.title: programs.mcfly.enableZshIntegration
options.description: Whether to enable Zsh integration.

options.title: programs.mcfly.fuzzySearchFactor
options.description:
Whether to enable fuzzy searching.
0 is off; higher numbers weight toward shorter matches.
Values in the 2-5 range get good results so far.

options.title: programs.mcfly.fzf.enable
options.description: Whether to enable McFly fzf integration.

options.title: programs.mcfly.settings
options.description:
Settings written to {file}`~/.config/mcfly/config.toml`.

Note, if your McFly database is currently in {file}`~/.mcfly`,
then this option has no effect.
Move the database to {file}`$XDG_DATA_DIR/mcfly/history.db` and
remove {file}`~/.mcfly` to make the settings take effect. See
<https://github.com/cantino/mcfly#database-location>.

options.title: programs.nh.clean.dates
options.description:
How often cleanup is performed.

On linux the format is described in {manpage}`systemd.time(7)`.

On Darwin it must be one of: hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, semiannually, annually, which are implemented as defined in {manpage}`systemd.time(7)`.

options.description:

See `nh clean all --help` for more information.

options.title: programs.nh.darwinFlake
options.type: null or (optionally newline-terminated) single-line string or absolute path
options.description:
The string that will be used for the {env}`NH_DARWIN_FLAKE` environment variable.

{env}`NH_DARWIN_FLAKE` is used by nh as the default flake for performing
{command}`nh darwin` actions, such as {command}`nh darwin switch`.
Setting this will take priority over the `flake` option.

options.title: programs.nh.enable

options.title: programs.nh.flake
options.type: null or (optionally newline-terminated) single-line string or absolute path
options.description:
The path that will be used for the {env}`FLAKE` environment variable.

{env}`FLAKE` is used by nh as the default flake for performing actions,
like {command}`nh os switch`.

options.title: programs.nh.homeFlake
options.type: null or (optionally newline-terminated) single-line string or absolute path
options.description:
The string that will be used for the {env}`NH_HOME_FLAKE` environment variable.

{env}`NH_HOME_FLAKE` is used by nh as the default flake for performing {command}`nh home`
actions, such as {command}`nh home switch`.
Setting this will take priority over the `flake` option.

options.title: programs.nh.osFlake
options.type: null or (optionally newline-terminated) single-line string or absolute path
options.description:
The string that will be used for the {env}`NH_OS_FLAKE` environment variable.

{env}`NH_OS_FLAKE` is used by nh as the default flake for performing {command}`nh os`
actions, such as {command}`nh os switch`.
Setting this will take priority over the `flake` option.

options.title: programs.nheko.settings
options.type: attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))
options.description:
Attribute set of Nheko preferences (converted to an INI file).

For now, it is recommended to run nheko and sign-in before filling in
the configuration settings in this module, as nheko writes the access
token to {file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/nheko/nheko.conf` the
first time we sign in, and we need that data into these settings for the
correct functionality of the application.

This a temporary inconvenience, however, as nheko has plans to move the
authentication stuff into the local database they currently use. Once
this happens, this will no longer be an issue.

options.title: programs.nyxt.config
options.type: strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path
options.description:
Configuration file for Nyxt, written in the Common Lisp
programming language.
options.example:
''
  (in-package #:nyxt-user)
  
  (defvar *my-search-engines*
    (list
     (make-instance 'search-engine
                    :name "Google"
                    :shortcut "goo"
                    #+nyxt-4 :control-url #+nyxt-3 :search-url
                    "https://duckduckgo.com/?q=~a")
     (make-instance 'search-engine
                    :name "MDN"
                    :shortcut "mdn"
                    #+nyxt-4 :control-url #+nyxt-3 :search-url
                    "https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/search?q=~a")))
  
  (define-configuration browser
    ((restore-session-on-startup-p nil)
     (default-new-buffer-url (quri:uri "https://github.com/atlas-engineer/nyxt"))
     (external-editor-program (if (member :flatpak *features*)
                                  "flatpak-spawn --host emacsclient -r"
                                  "emacsclient -r"))
     #+nyxt-4
     (search-engine-suggestions-p nil)
     #+nyxt-4
     (search-engines (append %slot-default% *my-search-engines*))
     ;; Sets the font for the Nyxt UI (not for webpages).
     (theme (make-instance 'theme:theme
                           :font-family "Iosevka"
                           :monospace-font-family "Iosevka"))
     ;; Whether code sent to the socket gets executed.  You must understand the
     ;; risks before enabling this: a privileged user with access to your system
     ;; can then take control of the browser and execute arbitrary code under your
     ;; user profile.
     ;; (remote-execution-p t)
     ))
''

options.title: programs.opencode.rules
options.type: strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path
options.description:
You can provide global custom instructions to opencode.
 The value is either:
 - Inline content as a string
 - A path to a file containing the content
This value is written to {file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/opencode/AGENTS.md`.
options.example:
''
  # TypeScript Project Rules

  ## External File Loading

  CRITICAL: When you encounter a file reference (e.g., @rules/general.md), use your Read tool to load it on a need-to-know basis. They're relevant to the SPECIFIC task at hand.

  Instructions:

  - Do NOT preemptively load all references - use lazy loading based on actual need
  - When loaded, treat content as mandatory instructions that override defaults
  - Follow references recursively when needed

  ## Development Guidelines

  For TypeScript code style and best practices: @docs/typescript-guidelines.md
  For React component architecture and hooks patterns: @docs/react-patterns.md
  For REST API design and error handling: @docs/api-standards.md
  For testing strategies and coverage requirements: @test/testing-guidelines.md

  ## General Guidelines

  Read the following file immediately as it's relevant to all workflows: @rules/general-guidelines.md.
''

options.title: programs.qutebrowser.keyBindings
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (null or strings concatenated with " ;; ")
options.description:
Key bindings mapping keys to commands in different modes. This setting
is a dictionary containing mode names and dictionaries mapping keys to
commands: `{mode: {key: command}}` If you want to map
a key to another key, check the `keyMappings` setting
instead. For modifiers, you can use either `-` or
`+` as delimiters, and these names:

Control
: `Control`, `Ctrl`

Meta
: `Meta`, `Windows`, `Mod4`

Alt
: `Alt`, `Mod1`

Shift
: `Shift`

For simple keys (no `<>`-signs), a capital
letter means the key is pressed with Shift. For special keys (with
`<>`-signs), you need to explicitly add
`Shift-` to match a key pressed with shift. If you
want a binding to do nothing, bind it to the `nop`
command. If you want a default binding to be passed through to the
website, bind it to null. Note that some commands which are only useful
for bindings (but not used interactively) are hidden from the command
completion. See `:help` for a full list of available
commands. The following modes are available:

`normal`
: Default mode, where most commands are invoked.

`insert`
: Entered when an input field is focused on a website, or by
  pressing `i` in normal mode. Passes through almost all keypresses
  to the website, but has some bindings like
  `<Ctrl-e>` to open an external editor.
  Note that single keys can't be bound in this mode.

`hint`
: Entered when `f` is pressed to select links with the keyboard. Note
  that single keys can't be bound in this mode.

`passthrough`
: Similar to insert mode, but passes through all keypresses except
  `<Escape>` to leave the mode. It might be
  useful to bind `<Escape>` to some other
  key in this mode if you want to be able to send an Escape key to
  the website as well. Note that single keys can't be bound in this
  mode.

`command`
: Entered when pressing the `:` key in order to enter a command. Note
  that single keys can't be bound in this mode.

`prompt`
: Entered when there's a prompt to display, like for download
  locations or when invoked from JavaScript.

`yesno`
: Entered when there's a yes/no prompt displayed.

`caret`
: Entered when pressing the `v` mode, used to select text using the
  keyboard.

`register`
: Entered when qutebrowser is waiting for a register name/key for
  commands like `:set-mark`.

options.title: programs.qutebrowser.keyMappings
options.description:
This setting can be used to map keys to other keys. When the key used
as dictionary-key is pressed, the binding for the key used as
dictionary-value is invoked instead. This is useful for global
remappings of keys, for example to map Ctrl-[ to Escape. Note that when
a key is bound (via `bindings.default` or
`bindings.commands`), the mapping is ignored.

options.title: programs.qutebrowser.searchEngines
options.description:
Search engines that can be used via the address bar. Maps a search
engine name (such as `DEFAULT`, or
`ddg`) to a URL with a `{}`
placeholder. The placeholder will be replaced by the search term, use
`{{` and `}}` for literal
`{/}` signs. The search engine named
`DEFAULT` is used when
`url.auto_search` is turned on and something else than
a URL was entered to be opened. Other search engines can be used by
prepending the search engine name to the search term, for example
`:open google qutebrowser`.

options.title: programs.rclone.enable
options.description: Whether to enable rclone.

options.title: programs.rclone.package
options.description: The rclone package to use.

options.title: programs.rclone.remotes
options.description:
An attribute set of remote configurations. Each remote consists of regular
configuration options and optional secrets.

See <https://rclone.org/docs/> for more information on configuring specific
remotes.

options.title: programs.rclone.remotes.<name>.config
options.type: An attribute set containing a remote type and options.
options.description:
Regular configuration options as described in rclone's documentation
<https://rclone.org/docs/>. When specifying options follow the formatting
process outlined here <https://rclone.org/docs/#config-config-file>, namely:
 - Remove the leading double-dash (--) from the rclone option name
 - Replace hyphens (-) with underscores (_)
 - Convert to lowercase
 - Use the resulting string as your configuration key

For example, the rclone option "--mega-hard-delete" would use "hard_delete"
as the config key.

Security Note: Always use the {option}`secrets` option for sensitive data
instead of the {option}`config` option to prevent exposing credentials to
the world-readable Nix store.

options.title: programs.rclone.remotes.<name>.mounts
options.description:
An attribute set mapping remote file paths to their corresponding mount
point configurations.

For each entry, to perform the equivalent of
`rclone mount remote:path/to/files /path/to/local/mount` — as described in the
rclone documentation <https://rclone.org/commands/rclone_mount/> — we create
a key-value pair like this:
`"path/to/files/on/remote" = { ... }`.

options.title: programs.rclone.remotes.<name>.mounts.<name>.enable
options.description: Whether to enable this mount.

options.title: programs.rclone.remotes.<name>.mounts.<name>.logLevel
options.description:
Set the log-level.
See: https://rclone.org/docs/#logging

options.title: programs.rclone.remotes.<name>.mounts.<name>.mountPoint
options.description: A local file path specifying the location of the mount point.

options.title: programs.rclone.remotes.<name>.mounts.<name>.options
options.type: attribute set of (null or boolean or signed integer or floating point number or string)
options.description:
An attribute set of option values passed to `rclone mount`. To set
a boolean option, assign it `true` or `false`. See
<https://nixos.org/manual/nixpkgs/stable/#function-library-lib.cli.toGNUCommandLineShell>
for more details on the format.

Some caching options are set by default, namely `vfs-cache-mode = "full"`
and `cache-dir`. These can be overridden if desired.

options.title: programs.rclone.remotes.<name>.secrets
options.description:
Sensitive configuration values such as passwords, API keys, and tokens. These
must be provided as file paths to the secrets, which will be read at activation
time.

These values are expanded in a shell context within a systemd service, so
you can use bash features like command substitution or variable expansion
(e.g. "${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}" as used by agenix).

options.title: programs.rclone.requiresUnit
options.description:
The name of a systemd user service that must complete before the rclone
configuration file is written.

This is typically used when secrets are managed by an external provisioner
whose service must run before the secrets are accessible.

When using sops-nix or agenix, this value is set automatically to
sops-nix.service or agenix.service, respectively. Set this manually if you
use a different secret provisioner.

options.description:
Configuration settings for rmpc in the Rusty Object Notation
format. All available options can be found in the official
documentation at <https://mierak.github.io/rmpc/next/configuration/>.
options.example:
''
  (
      address: "127.0.0.1:6600",
      password: None,
      theme: None,
      cache_dir: None,
      on_song_change: None,
      volume_step: 5,
      max_fps: 30,
      scrolloff: 0,
      wrap_navigation: false,
      enable_mouse: true,
      enable_config_hot_reload: true,
      status_update_interval_ms: 1000,
      select_current_song_on_change: false,
      browser_song_sort: [Disc, Track, Artist, Title],
  )
''

`maven-local`, `maven-central`.

Custom repositories are defined as

See
<https://www.scala-sbt.org/1.x/docs/Launcher-Configuration.html#3.+Repositories+Section>
about this configuration section and
<https://www.scala-sbt.org/1.x/docs/Proxy-Repositories.html>

options.title: programs.senpai.config.address
options.description:
The address (`host[:port]`) of the IRC server. senpai uses TLS
connections by default unless you specify tls option to be false.
TLS connections default to port 6697, plain-text use port 6667.

UR`ircs://`, `irc://`, and `irc+insecure://` URLs are supported,
in which case only the hostname and port parts will be used. If
the scheme is `ircs/irc+insecure`, tls will be overriden and set
to true/false accordingly.

options.title: programs.senpai.config.nickname
options.description:
Your nickname, sent with a NICK IRC message. It mustn't contain
spaces or colons (:).

options.title: programs.senpai.config.password
options.description:
Your password, used for SASL authentication. Note that it will
reside world-readable in the Nix store.

options.title: programs.senpai.config.password-cmd
options.description:
Alternatively to providing your SASL authentication password
directly in plaintext, you can specify a command to be run to
fetch the password at runtime. This is useful if you store your
passwords in a separate (probably encrypted) file using `gpg` or a
command line password manager such as `pass` or `gopass`. If a
password-cmd is provided, the value of password will be ignored
and the first line of the output of `password-cmd` will be used
for login.

options.title: programs.sketchybar.config
options.type: null or strings concatenated with "\n" or (submodule)
options.description:
The sketchybar configuration. Can be specified as:

1. A string containing the configuration content directly
2. An attribute set with 'source' pointing to a directory containing
   the full configuration, and optionally 'recursive = true' to
   recursively copy all files
3. An attribute set with 'text' containing inline configuration

When using a string or 'text', the appropriate shebang will be
automatically added based on configType (bash or lua).

When using a directory source, it should contain a file named
"sketchybarrc" which serves as the main entry point.
options.example:
# String example - inline configuration
''
# Define colors
export COLOR_BLACK="0xff181926"
export COLOR_WHITE="0xffcad3f5"

# Configure bar
sketchybar --bar height=32 \
                position=top \
                padding_left=10 \
                padding_right=10 \
                color=$COLOR_BLACK

# Configure default values
sketchybar --default icon.font="SF Pro:Bold:14.0" \
                    icon.color=$COLOR_WHITE \
                    label.font="SF Pro:Bold:14.0" \
                    label.color=$COLOR_WHITE

# Add items to the bar
sketchybar --add item clock right \
          --set clock script="date '+%H:%M'" \
                      update_freq=10

# Update the bar
sketchybar --update
''

# Or directory example - for complex configurations
# {
#   source = ./path/to/sketchybar-config;
#   recursive = true;
# }

options.title: programs.sketchybar.configType
options.description:
The type of configuration to generate.

Set to "bash" to use the standard bash configuration.
Set to "lua" to use the Lua configuration via SbarLua.

and selects the required Lua packages from this package set.

options.title: programs.swaylock.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable swaylock.

Note that PAM must be configured to enable swaylock to perform
authentication. The package installed through home-manager
will *not* be able to unlock the session without this
configuration.

On NixOS, this is by default enabled with the sway module, but
for other compositors it can currently be enabled using:

```nix
security.pam.services.swaylock = {};
```

On non-NixOS, it is easiest to avoid incompatibility involving PAM by

1. stopping Home Manager from installing swaylock by setting
   {option}`programs.swaylock.package` to `null`; and

2. instead installing swaylock from your distribution's repository.
options.default:
true  if state version < 23.05 and settings ≠ { },
false otherwise

options.description: The swaylock package to use.

options.title: programs.swaylock.settings
options.type: attribute set of (boolean or floating point number or signed integer or absolute path or string)
options.description:

disables configuration generation.

options.description: Attribute set of Thunderbird profiles.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.accountsOrder
options.description:
Custom ordering of accounts and local folders in
Thunderbird's folder pane. The accounts are specified
by their name. For declarative accounts, it must be the name
of their attribute in `config.accounts.email.accounts` (or
`config.programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.feedAccounts`
for feed accounts). The local folders name can be found in
the `mail.accountmanager.accounts` Thunderbird preference,
for example with Settings > Config Editor ("account1" by
default). Enabled accounts and local folders that aren't
listed here appear in an arbitrary order after the ordered
accounts.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.calendarAccountsOrder
options.description:
Custom ordering of calendar accounts. The accounts are specified
by their name. For declarative accounts, it must be the name
of their attribute in `config.accounts.calendar.accounts`.
Enabled accounts that aren't listed here appear in an arbitrary
order after the ordered accounts.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.extensions
options.description:
List of ‹name› add-on packages to install for this profile.

Note that it is necessary to manually enable extensions
inside ‹name› after the first installation.

To automatically enable extensions add
`"extensions.autoDisableScopes" = 0;`
to
[{option}`programs.thunderbird.profiles.<profile>.settings`](#opt-programs.thunderbird.profiles._name_.settings)

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.extraConfig
options.description: Extra preferences to add to {file}`user.js`.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.feedAccounts
options.description:
Attribute set of feed accounts. Feeds themselves have to be
managed through Thunderbird's settings. This option allows
feeds to coexist with declaratively managed email accounts.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.feedAccounts.<name>.name
options.description: This feed account's name.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.isDefault
options.description:
Whether this is a default profile. There must be exactly one
default profile.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.name
options.description: This profile's name.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.search
options.description: Declarative search engine configuration.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.search.default
options.description:
The default search engine used in the address bar and search
bar.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.search.engines
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (JSON value)
options.description:
Attribute set of search engine configurations. Engines that
only have {var}`metaData` specified will be treated as builtin
to Thunderbird.

See [SearchEngine.jsm](https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/rev/e3f42ec9320748b2aab3d474d1e47075def9000c/toolkit/components/search/SearchEngine.sys.mjs#890-923)
in Thunderbird's source for available options. We maintain a
mapping to let you specify all options in the referenced link
without underscores, but it may fall out of date with future
options.

Note, {var}`icon` is also a special option added by Home
Manager to make it convenient to specify absolute icon paths.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.search.force
options.description:
Whether to force replace the existing search
configuration. This is recommended since Thunderbird will
replace the symlink for the search configuration on every
launch, but note that you'll lose any existing configuration
by enabling this.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.search.meta.maintainers
options.description:
List of maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

The option value is not a list of maintainers, but an attribute set that maps module file names to lists of maintainers.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.search.meta.teams
options.description:
List of team maintainers of each module.
This option should be defined at most once per module.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.search.order
options.description:
The order the search engines are listed in. Any engines that
aren't included in this list will be listed after these in an
unspecified order.

options.title: programs.thunderbird.profiles.<name>.search.privateDefault
options.description: The default search engine used in the Private Browsing.

options.title: programs.vim.settings
options.description:
At attribute set of Vim settings. The attribute names and
corresponding values must be among the following supported
options.

{var}`background`
: one of "dark", "light"

{var}`backupdir`
: list of string

{var}`copyindent`
: boolean

{var}`directory`
: list of string

{var}`expandtab`
: boolean

{var}`hidden`
: boolean

{var}`history`
: signed integer

{var}`ignorecase`
: boolean

{var}`modeline`
: boolean

{var}`mouse`
: one of "n", "v", "i", "c", "h", "a", "r"

{var}`mousefocus`
: boolean

{var}`mousehide`
: boolean

{var}`mousemodel`
: one of "extend", "popup", "popup_setpos"

{var}`number`
: boolean

{var}`relativenumber`
: boolean

{var}`shiftwidth`
: signed integer

{var}`smartcase`
: boolean

{var}`tabstop`
: signed integer

{var}`undodir`
: list of string

{var}`undofile`
: boolean


See the Vim documentation for detailed descriptions of these
options. Use [](#opt-programs.vim.extraConfig) to manually
set any options not listed above.

options.title: programs.visidata.visidatarc
options.description:
Configuration settings and Python function declarations
to be written to ~/.visidatarc. All available options
can be found here: <https://www.visidata.org/docs/>.
options.example:
''
  options.min_memory_mb=100  # stop processing without 100MB free
  
  bindkey('0', 'go-leftmost')   # alias '0' to go to first column, like vim
  
  def median(values):
      L = sorted(values)
      return L[len(L)//2]
  
  vd.aggregator('median', median)
''

options.title: programs.vscode.mutableExtensionsDir
options.description:
Whether extensions can be installed or updated manually
or by Visual Studio Code. Mutually exclusive to
programs.vscode.profiles.

options.title: programs.vscode.nameShort
options.description:
Override for package "short name", used for generating configuration.

This should match the `shortName` field in the package's product.json. If `null`, then searches common locations for a product.json and uses the value from there.

options.title: programs.vscode.package
options.description: The vscode package to use. Version of Visual Studio Code to install.

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles
options.description:
A list of all VSCode profiles. Mutually exclusive
to programs.vscode.mutableExtensionsDir

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.enableExtensionUpdateCheck
options.description:
Whether to enable update notifications for extensions.
Can only be set for the default profile, but
it applies to all profiles.

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.enableMcpIntegration
options.description:
Whether to integrate the MCP servers config from
{option}`programs.mcp.servers` into
{option}`programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.userMcp`.

Note: Settings defined in {option}`programs.mcp.servers` are merged
with {option}`programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.userMcp`, with VSCode
settings taking precedence.

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.enableUpdateCheck
options.description:
Whether to enable update checks/notifications.
Can only be set for the default profile, but
it applies to all profiles.

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.extensions
options.description: The extensions Visual Studio Code should be started with.

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.globalSnippets
options.description: Defines global user snippets.

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.keybindings
options.description:
Keybindings written to Visual Studio Code's
{file}`keybindings.json`.
This can be a JSON object or a path to a custom JSON file.

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.languageSnippets
options.description: Defines user snippets for different languages.

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.userMcp
options.description:
Configuration written to Visual Studio Code's
{file}`mcp.json`.
This can be a JSON object or a path to a custom JSON file.

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.userSettings
options.description:
Configuration written to Visual Studio Code's
{file}`settings.json`.
This can be a JSON object or a path to a custom JSON file.

options.title: programs.vscode.profiles.<name>.userTasks
options.description:
Configuration written to Visual Studio Code's
{file}`tasks.json`.
This can be a JSON object or a path to a custom JSON file.

options.description: The waybar package to use.

options.type: null or absolute path or strings concatenated with "\n"
options.description:

If the value is set to a path literal, then the path will be used as the css file.

options.title: programs.waybar.systemd.enableDebug

options.title: programs.waybar.systemd.enableInspect

See <https://developer.gnome.org/documentation/tools/inspector.html>

options.title: programs.waybar.systemd.target
options.description:
The systemd target that will automatically start the Waybar service.

When setting this value to `"sway-session.target"`,
make sure to also enable {option}`wayland.windowManager.sway.systemd.enable`,
otherwise the service may never be started.

options.title: programs.waylogout.enable

options.title: programs.wayprompt.package

options.description:

{file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/wayprompt/config.ini`.
See {manpage}`wayprompt(5)` for a list of available options.
Note that colours can be either 6-hex-digit RGB or 8-hex-digit RGBA values.

options.title: programs.xmobar.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Xmobar, a minimalistic status bar.

options.title: programs.xmobar.extraConfig
options.description:
Extra configuration lines to add to
{file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/xmobar/.xmobarrc`.
See
<https://xmobar.org/#configuration>
for options.
options.example:
Config
  { font        = "Fira Code"
  , borderColor = "#d0d0d0"
  , border      = FullB
  , borderWidth = 3
  , bgColor     = "#222"
  , fgColor     = "grey"
  , position    = TopSize C 99 30
  , commands    =
      [ Run Cpu ["-t", "cpu: <fc=#4eb4fa><bar> <total>%</fc>"] 10
      , Run Network "enp3s0" ["-S", "True", "-t", "eth: <fc=#4eb4fa><rx></fc>/<fc=#4eb4fa><tx></fc>"] 10
      , Run Memory ["-t","mem: <fc=#4eb4fa><usedbar> <usedratio>%</fc>"] 10
      , Run Date "date: <fc=#4eb4fa>%a %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S </fc>" "date" 10
      , Run StdinReader
      ]
  , sepChar     = "%"
  , alignSep    = "}{"
  , template    = "  %StdinReader% | %cpu% | %memory% | %enp3s0%  }{%date%  "
  }

options.title: programs.zsh.autosuggestion.strategy
options.description:
`ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_STRATEGY` is an array that specifies how suggestions should be generated.
The strategies in the array are tried successively until a suggestion is found.
There are currently three built-in strategies to choose from:

- `history`: Chooses the most recent match from history.
- `completion`: Chooses a suggestion based on what tab-completion would suggest. (requires `zpty` module)
- `match_prev_cmd`: Like `history`, but chooses the most recent match whose preceding history item matches
    the most recently executed command. Note that this strategy won't work as expected with ZSH options that
    don't preserve the history order such as `HIST_IGNORE_ALL_DUPS` or `HIST_EXPIRE_DUPS_FIRST`.

Setting the option to an empty list `[]` will make ZSH_AUTOSUGGESTION_STRATEGY not be set automatically,
allowing the variable to be declared in {option}`programs.zsh.localVariables` or {option}`programs.zsh.sessionVariables`

This file will still be periodically re-written to trim it when the
number of lines grows 20% beyond the value specified by
`programs.zsh.history.save`.

options.title: programs.zsh.initContent
options.description:
Content to be added to {file}`.zshrc`.

To specify the order, use `lib.mkOrder`.

Common order values:
- 500 (mkBefore): Early initialization (replaces initExtraFirst)
- 550: Before completion initialization (replaces initExtraBeforeCompInit)
- 1000 (default): General configuration (replaces initExtra)
- 1500 (mkAfter): Last to run configuration

To specify both content in Early initialization and General configuration, use `lib.mkMerge`.

e.g.

initContent = let
    zshConfigEarlyInit = lib.mkOrder 500 "do something";
    zshConfig = lib.mkOrder 1000 "do something";
in
    lib.mkMerge [ zshConfigEarlyInit zshConfig ];

: Use the Adwaita style from

`breeze`
: Use the Breeze style from
  [`breeze`](https://github.com/KDE/breeze)

`bb10bright`, `bb10dark`, `cde`, `cleanlooks`, `gtk2`, `motif`, `plastique`
: Use styles from

`kvantum`

  [`kvantum`](https://github.com/tsujan/Kvantum)

options.title: services.activitywatch.extraOptions
options.description: Additional arguments to be passed on to the ActivityWatch server.

options.title: services.activitywatch.package
options.description:
The activitywatch package to use. Specifically, this should be a package containing [the Rust implementation

options.title: services.activitywatch.settings
options.description:
Configuration for `aw-server-rust` to be generated at

options.title: services.activitywatch.watchers
options.description:
Watchers to be included with the service alongside with their
configuration.

Check with `systemctl --user status "*aw*"`

If a configuration is set, a file will be generated in
{file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/activitywatch/$WATCHER_NAME/$WATCHER_SETTINGS_FILENAME`.

::: {.note}
The watchers are run with the service manager and the settings format
of the configuration is only assumed to be in TOML. Furthermore, it
assumes the watcher program is using the official client libraries
which has functions to store it in the appropriate location.
:::

options.title: services.activitywatch.watchers.<name>.executable
options.description:
The name of the executable of the watcher. This is useful in case the
watcher name is different from the executable. By default, this
option uses the watcher name.

options.description: Extra arguments to be passed to the watcher executable.

options.title: services.activitywatch.watchers.<name>.name

{file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/activitywatch/$NAME` when
{option}`services.activitywatch.watchers.<name>.settings` is set.

options.title: services.activitywatch.watchers.<name>.package
options.description: The activitywatch package to use. The derivation containing the watcher executable.

options.title: services.activitywatch.watchers.<name>.settings
options.description:
The settings for the individual watcher in TOML format. If set, a
file will be generated at
{file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/activitywatch/$NAME/$FILENAME`.

To set the basename of the settings file, see
[](#opt-services.activitywatch.watchers._name_.settingsFilename).

options.title: services.activitywatch.watchers.<name>.settingsFilename
options.description:
The filename of the generated settings file. By default, this uses
the watcher name to be generated at
{file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/activitywatch/$NAME/$NAME.toml`.

This is useful in case the watcher requires a different name for the
configuration file.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.defaultCacheTtlSsh
options.description:
Set the time a cache entry used for SSH keys is valid to the
given number of seconds.

options.description: Whether to enable GnuPG private key agent.

options.description: Whether to enable Bash integration.

options.description:
Whether to enable extra socket of the GnuPG key agent (useful for GPG
Agent forwarding).

options.title: services.gpg-agent.enableFishIntegration
options.description: Whether to enable Fish integration.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.enableNushellIntegration
options.description: Whether to enable Nushell integration.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.enableScDaemon
options.description:
Make use of the scdaemon tool. This option has the effect of
enabling the ability to do smartcard operations. When
disabled, this option passes
{option}`disable-scdaemon` setting to gpg-agent.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.enableSshSupport
options.description: Whether to use the GnuPG key agent for SSH keys.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.enableZshIntegration
options.description: Whether to enable Zsh integration.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.extraConfig
options.description:
Extra configuration lines to append to the gpg-agent
configuration file.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.grabKeyboardAndMouse
options.description:
Tell the pinentry to grab the keyboard and mouse. This
option should in general be used to avoid X-sniffing
attacks. When disabled, this option passes
{option}`no-grab` setting to gpg-agent.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.maxCacheTtl
options.description:
Set the maximum time a cache entry is valid to n seconds. After this
time a cache entry will be expired even if it has been accessed
recently or has been set using gpg-preset-passphrase. The default is
2 hours (7200 seconds).

options.title: services.gpg-agent.maxCacheTtlSsh
options.description:
Set the maximum time a cache entry used for SSH keys is valid to n
seconds. After this time a cache entry will be expired even if it has
been accessed recently or has been set using gpg-preset-passphrase.
The default is 2 hours (7200 seconds).

options.title: services.gpg-agent.noAllowExternalCache
options.description:
Tell Pinentry not to enable features which use an external cache for
passphrases.

Some desktop environments prefer to unlock all credentials with one
master password and may have installed a Pinentry which employs an
additional external cache to implement such a policy. By using this
option the Pinentry is advised not to make use of such a cache and
instead always ask the user for the requested passphrase.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.pinentry.package
options.description:
The pinentry-gnome3 package to use. Which pinentry interface to use. If not `null`, it sets
{option}`pinentry-program` in {file}`gpg-agent.conf`. Beware that
`pinentry-gnome3` may not work on non-GNOME systems. You can fix it by
adding the following to your configuration:
```nix
home.packages = [ pkgs.gcr ];
```

options.title: services.gpg-agent.pinentry.program
options.description: Which program to search for in the configured `pinentry.package`.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.sshKeys
options.description: Which GPG keys (by keygrip) to expose as SSH keys.

options.title: services.gpg-agent.verbose
options.description: Whether to produce verbose output.

options.title: services.grobi.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the grobi display setup daemon.

options.title: services.grobi.executeAfter
options.description:
Commands to be run after an output configuration was
changed. The Nix value declared here will be translated to
JSON and written to the {option}`execute_after` key
in {file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/grobi.conf`.

options.title: services.grobi.package
options.description: The grobi package to use.

options.title: services.grobi.rules
options.type: list of attribute set of (string or boolean or signed integer or list of string)
options.description:
These are the rules grobi tries to match to the current
output configuration. The rules are evaluated top to bottom,
the first matching rule is applied and processing stops. See
<https://github.com/fd0/grobi/blob/master/doc/grobi.conf>
for more information. The Nix value declared here will be
translated to JSON and written to the {option}`rules`
key in {file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/grobi.conf`.

options.description:
A keysym or raw keycode that toggles the activation state of
gromit-mpx.  Set to `null` to disable the
hotkey in which case you'll have to activate gromit-mpx
manually using the command line.

options.title: services.gromit-mpx.opacity
options.description: Opacity of the drawing overlay.

options.title: services.gromit-mpx.package
options.description: The gromit-mpx package to use.

options.description: Tool definitions for gromit-mpx to use.

options.title: services.gromit-mpx.tools.*.arrowSize
options.description:
If not `null`, automatically draw an arrow
at the end of a stroke with the given size.

options.title: services.gromit-mpx.tools.*.color
options.description: The stroke (or recolor) color of the tool.

options.title: services.gromit-mpx.tools.*.device
options.description:
Use this tool with the given xinput device.  The device with
the name default works with any input.

options.title: services.gromit-mpx.tools.*.modifiers
options.description: Only activate this tool if the given modifiers are also active.

options.title: services.gromit-mpx.tools.*.size
options.description: The tool size.

options.title: services.gromit-mpx.tools.*.type
options.description: Which type of tool this is.

options.title: services.gromit-mpx.undoKey
options.type: null or string or (positive integer, meaning >0)
options.description:
A keysym or raw keycode that causes gromit-mpx to undo the
last stroke.  Use this key along with the shift key to redo an
undone stoke.  Set to `null` to disable the
undo hotkey.

options.title: services.home-manager.autoExpire.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable the Home Manager expire service that periodically expire your
old Home Manager generations.

options.title: services.home-manager.autoExpire.frequency
options.description:
The interval at which the Home Manager auto expire is run.

This value is passed to the systemd timer configuration
as the `OnCalendar` option.

The format is described in {manpage}`systemd.time(7)`.

On Darwin it must be one of: hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, semiannually, annually, which are implemented as defined in {manpage}`systemd.time(7)`.

options.title: services.home-manager.autoExpire.store.cleanup
options.description:
Whether to enable to cleanup Nix store when the Home Manager expire service runs.

It will use `nix-collect-garbage` to cleanup the store,
removing all unreachable store objects from the current user
(i.e.: not only the expired Home Manager generations).

This may not be what you want, this is why this option is disabled
by default.

options.title: services.home-manager.autoExpire.store.options
options.description: Options given to `nix-collect-garbage` when the service runs.

options.title: services.home-manager.autoExpire.timestamp
options.description:
Remove generations older than `TIMESTAMP` where `TIMESTAMP` is
interpreted as in the -d argument of the date tool.

options.title: services.home-manager.autoUpgrade.frequency
options.description:

This value is passed to the systemd timer configuration
as the `OnCalendar` option.
The format is described in
{manpage}`systemd.time(7)`.

options.title: services.home-manager.autoUpgrade.useFlake
options.description: Whether to use 'nix flake update' instead of 'nix-channel --update'.

options.title: services.muchsync.remotes.<name>.frequency
options.description:
How often to run {command}`muchsync`. This
value is passed to the systemd timer configuration as the
`OnCalendar` option. See
{manpage}`systemd.time(7)`
for more information about the format.

options.title: services.muchsync.remotes.<name>.local.checkForModifiedFiles
options.description:
Check for locally modified files.
Without this option, muchsync assumes that files in a maildir are
never edited.

{option}`checkForModifiedFiles` disables certain
optimizations so as to make muchsync at least check the timestamp on
every file, which will detect modified files at the cost of a longer
startup time.

This option is useful if your software regularly modifies the
contents of mail files (e.g., because you are running offlineimap
with "synclabels = yes").

options.title: services.muchsync.remotes.<name>.local.importNew
options.description:
Whether to begin the synchronisation by running
{command}`notmuch new` locally.

options.title: services.muchsync.remotes.<name>.remote.checkForModifiedFiles
options.description:
Check for modified files on the remote side.
Without this option, muchsync assumes that files in a maildir are
never edited.

{option}`checkForModifiedFiles` disables certain
optimizations so as to make muchsync at least check the timestamp on
every file, which will detect modified files at the cost of a longer
startup time.

This option is useful if your software regularly modifies the
contents of mail files (e.g., because you are running offlineimap
with "synclabels = yes").

options.title: services.muchsync.remotes.<name>.remote.host
options.description: Remote SSH host to synchronize with.

options.title: services.muchsync.remotes.<name>.remote.importNew
options.description:
Whether to begin the synchronisation by running
{command}`notmuch new` on the remote side.

options.title: services.muchsync.remotes.<name>.remote.muchsyncPath
options.description:
Specifies the path to muchsync on the server.
Ordinarily, muchsync should be in the default PATH on the server
so this option is not required.
However, this option is useful if you have to install muchsync in
a non-standard place or wish to test development versions of the
code.

options.title: services.muchsync.remotes.<name>.sshCommand
options.description:
Specifies a command line to pass to {command}`/bin/sh`
to execute a command on another machine.

Note that because this string is passed to the shell,
special characters including spaces may need to be escaped.

options.description: Whether to propagate local changes to the remote.

options.title: services.ollama.acceleration
options.description:
What interface to use for hardware acceleration.

- `null`: default behavior
  - if `nixpkgs.config.rocmSupport` is enabled, uses `"rocm"`
  - if `nixpkgs.config.cudaSupport` is enabled, uses `"cuda"`
  - otherwise defaults to `false`
- `false`: disable GPU, only use CPU
- `"rocm"`: supported by most modern AMD GPUs
  - may require overriding gpu type with `services.ollama.rocmOverrideGfx`
    if rocm doesn't detect your AMD gpu
- `"cuda"`: supported by most modern NVIDIA GPUs

options.description: Whether to enable Polybar status bar.

options.title: services.polybar.extraConfig
options.description: Additional configuration to add.
options.example:
''
  [module/date]
  type = internal/date
  interval = 5
  date = "%d.%m.%y"
  time = %H:%M
  format-prefix-foreground = \''${colors.foreground-alt}
  label = %time%  %date%
''

options.title: services.polybar.package
options.description: The polybar package to use.

options.title: services.polybar.script
options.description:
This script will be used to start the polybars.
Set all necessary environment variables here and start all bars.
It can be assumed that {command}`polybar` executable is in the {env}`PATH`.

Note, this script must start all bars in the background and then terminate.

options.title: services.polybar.settings
options.description:
Polybar configuration. This takes a nix attrset and converts it to the
strange data format that polybar uses.
Each entry will be converted to a section in the output file.
Several things are treated specially: nested keys are converted
to dash-separated keys; the special `text` key is ignored as a nested key,
to allow mixing different levels of nesting; and lists are converted to
polybar's `foo-0, foo-1, ...` format.

For example:
```nix
"module/volume" = {
  type = "internal/pulseaudio";
  format.volume = "<ramp-volume> <label-volume>";
  label.muted.text = "🔇";
  label.muted.foreground = "#666";
  ramp.volume = ["🔈" "🔉" "🔊"];
  click.right = "pavucontrol &";
}
```
becomes:
```ini
[module/volume]
type=internal/pulseaudio
format-volume=<ramp-volume> <label-volume>
label-muted=🔇
label-muted-foreground=#666
ramp-volume-0=🔈
ramp-volume-1=🔉
ramp-volume-2=🔊
click-right=pavucontrol &
```

options.title: services.restic.backups
options.description:
Backup configurations for Restic.

On Linux systems, a corresponding systemd user service
(and optionally a systemd timer for automatic scheduling)
will be created, along with a helper wrapper script.

On non-Linux platforms, only the helper wrapper script
will be created.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.backupCleanupCommand
options.description:
A script that must run after finishing the backup process.

This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.backupPrepareCommand
options.description:
A script that must run before starting the backup process.

This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.checkOpts
options.description:
A list of options for 'restic check'.

This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.createWrapper
options.description:
Whether to generate and add a script to the system path, that has the
same environment variables set as the systemd service. This can be used
to e.g. mount snapshots or perform other opterations, without having to
manually specify most options.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.dynamicFilesFrom
options.description:
A script that produces a list of files to back up. The results of
this command, along with the paths specified via {option}`paths`,
are given to the '--files-from' option.

This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.environmentFile
options.description:
A file containing the credentials to access the repository, in the
format of an EnvironmentFile as described by {manpage}`systemd.exec(5)`.
See <https://restic.readthedocs.io/en/stable/030_preparing_a_new_repo.html>
for the specific credentials you will need for your backend.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.exclude
options.description:
Patterns to exclude when backing up. See
<https://restic.readthedocs.io/en/stable/040_backup.html#excluding-files> for
details on syntax.

This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.extraBackupArgs
options.description:
Extra arguments passed to restic backup.

This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.extraOptions
options.description:
Extra extended options to be passed to the restic `-o` flag. See the restic
documentation for more details.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.inhibitsSleep
options.description:
Prevents the system from sleeping while backing up. This uses systemd-inhibit
to block system idling so you may need to enable polkitd with
{option}`security.polkit.enable`.

This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.initialize
options.description:
Create the repository if it does not already exist.

This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.package
options.description: The restic package to use.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.passwordFile
options.description: A file containing the repository password.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.paths
options.description:
Paths to back up, alongside those defined by the {option}`dynamicFilesFrom`
option. If left empty and {option}`dynamicFilesFrom` is also not specified, no
backup command will be run. This can be used to create a prune-only job.

This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.progressFps
options.type: null or (nonnegative integer or floating point number, meaning >=0)
options.description: Controls the frequency of progress reporting.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.pruneOpts
options.description:
A list of policy options for 'restic forget --prune', to automatically
prune old snapshots. See
<https://restic.readthedocs.io/en/latest/060_forget.html#removing-snapshots-according-to-a-policy>
for a full list of options.

Note: The 'forget' command is run *after* the 'backup' command, so keep
that in mind when constructing the --keep-\* options.

This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.rcloneOptions
options.description:
Options to pass to rclone to control its behavior. See
<https://rclone.org/docs/#options> for available options. When specifying
option names, strip the leading `--`. To set a flag such as
`--drive-use-trash`, which does not take a value, set the value to the
Boolean `true`.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.repository
options.description:
Repository to backup to. This should be in the form of a backend specification as
detailed here
<https://restic.readthedocs.io/en/stable/030_preparing_a_new_repo.html>.

If your using the rclone backend, you can configure your remotes with
{option}`programs.rclone.remotes` then use them in your backend specification.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.repositoryFile
options.description:
Path to a file containing the repository location to backup to. This should be
in the same form as the {option}`repository` option.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.runCheck
options.description:
Whether to run 'restic check' with the provided `checkOpts` options.
This option is only supported on linux.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.ssh-package
options.description: The openssh package to use.

options.title: services.restic.backups.<name>.timerConfig
options.type: null or (attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or string or absolute path or list of (boolean or signed integer or string or absolute path)))
options.description:
When to run the backup. See {manpage}`systemd.timer(5)` for details. If null
no timer is created and the backup will only run when explicitly started.

This option is only supported on linux.

config directory.

options.title: services.syncthing.overrideDevices

Whether to delete the devices which are not configured via the

options.title: services.syncthing.overrideFolders

Whether to delete the folders which are not configured via the

options.type: attribute set of (open submodule of (JSON value))
options.description:
Peers/devices which Syncthing should communicate with.

Note that you can still add devices manually, but those changes
will be reverted on restart if [overrideDevices](#opt-services.syncthing.overrideDevices)
is enabled.

options.title: services.syncthing.settings.devices.<name>.autoAcceptFolders
options.description:
Automatically create or share folders that this device advertises at the default path.
See <https://docs.syncthing.net/users/config.html?highlight=autoaccept#config-file-format>.

options.title: services.syncthing.settings.devices.<name>.id

options.type: attribute set of (open submodule of (JSON value))
options.description:
Folders which should be shared by Syncthing.

Note that you can still add folders manually, but those changes
will be reverted on restart if [overrideFolders](#opt-services.syncthing.overrideFolders)
is enabled.

options.title: services.syncthing.settings.folders.<name>.copyOwnershipFromParent
options.description:
On Unix systems, tries to copy file/folder ownership from
the parent directory (the directory it’s located in).
Requires running Syncthing as a privileged user, or
granting it additional capabilities (e.g. CAP_CHOWN on
Linux).

options.title: services.syncthing.settings.folders.<name>.devices
options.type: list of (string or (open submodule of (JSON value)))
options.description:
The devices this folder should be shared with. Each device must

A list of either strings or attribute sets, where values
are device names or device configurations.

Whether to share this folder.
This option is useful when you want to define all folders

Only absolute paths (starting with `/`) and paths relative to
the user's home directory (starting with `~/`) are allowed.

options.title: services.syncthing.settings.folders.<name>.type

options.title: services.syncthing.settings.folders.<name>.versioning
options.description:

See <https://docs.syncthing.net/users/versioning.html>.

options.title: services.syncthing.settings.folders.<name>.versioning.type

Whether the user has accepted to submit anonymous usage data.
The default, 0, mean the user has not made a choice, and Syncthing will ask at some point in the future.
"-1" means no, a number above zero means that that version of usage reporting has been accepted.

options.title: services.trayer.settings
options.type: attribute set of (null or string or boolean or signed integer)
options.description:
Trayer configuration as a set of attributes. Further details can be
found in [trayer's README](https://github.com/sargon/trayer-srg/blob/master/README).

{var}`SetDockType`
: boolean (default: `true`)

{var}`SetPartialStrut`
: boolean (default: `true`)

{var}`align`
: one of "left", "right", "center" (default: `"center"`)

{var}`alpha`
: signed integer (default: `127`)

{var}`distance`
: signed integer (default: `0`)

{var}`distancefrom`
: one of "left", "right", "top", "bottom" (default: `"top"`)

{var}`edge`
: one of "left", "right", "top", "bottom", "none" (default: `"bottom"`)

{var}`expand`
: boolean (default: `true`)

{var}`height`
: signed integer (default: `26`)

{var}`heighttype`
: one of "request", "pixel" (default: `"pixel"`)

{var}`iconspacing`
: signed integer (default: `0`)

{var}`margin`
: signed integer (default: `0`)

{var}`monitor`
: unsigned integer, meaning >=0, or value "primary" (singular enum) (default: `0`)

{var}`padding`
: signed integer (default: `0`)

{var}`tint`
: string (default: `"0xFFFFFFFF"`)

{var}`transparent`
: boolean (default: `false`)

{var}`width`
: signed integer (default: `100`)

{var}`widthtype`
: one of "request", "pixel", "percent" (default: `"percent"`)

options.title: services.wl-clip-persist.clipboardType
options.description:
The clipboard type to persist.

- `regular`: Persist the regular clipboard only (recommended).
- `primary`: Persist the primary selection only.
- `both`: Persist both regular and primary clipboards.

Note: Operating on the primary clipboard may have unintended side effects
for some applications.

options.title: services.wl-clip-persist.enable
options.description: Whether to enable wl-clip-persist, a Wayland clipboard persistence tool.

options.title: services.wl-clip-persist.extraOptions
options.description:
Extra command-line arguments to pass to wl-clip-persist.

Available options include:
- `--write-timeout <ms>`: Timeout for writing clipboard data (default: 3000).
- `--ignore-event-on-error`: Only handle events without errors.
- `--all-mime-type-regex <regex>`: Filter events by MIME type regex.
- `--selection-size-limit <bytes>`: Limit clipboard data size.
- `--reconnect-tries <n>`: Number of reconnection attempts.
- `--reconnect-delay <ms>`: Delay between reconnect attempts (default: 100).
- `--disable-timestamps`: Disable log timestamps.

options.title: services.wl-clip-persist.package
options.description: The wl-clip-persist package to use.

options.title: services.wl-clip-persist.systemdTargets
options.description: The systemd targets that will automatically start the wl-clip-persist service.

options.title: services.xcape.enable
options.description: Whether to enable xcape.

options.title: services.xcape.mapExpression
options.description:
The value has the grammar `Key[|OtherKey]`.

The list of key names is found in the header file
{file}`X11/keysymdef.h` (remove the
`XK_` prefix). Note that due to limitations
of X11 shifted keys must be specified as a shift key
followed by the key to be pressed rather than the actual
name of the character. For example to generate "{" the
expression `Shift_L|bracketleft` could be
used (assuming that you have a key with "{" above "[").

You can also specify keys in decimal (prefix #), octal (#0),
or hexadecimal (#0x). They will be interpreted as keycodes
unless no corresponding key name is found.

options.title: services.xcape.package
options.description: The xcape package to use.

options.title: services.xcape.timeout
options.description:
If you hold a key longer than this timeout, xcape will not
generate a key event. Default is 500 ms.

options.title: specialisation
options.description:
A set of named specialized configurations. These can be used to extend
your base configuration with additional settings. For example, you can
have specialisations named "light" and "dark"
that apply light and dark color theme configurations.

::: {.note}
This is an experimental option for now and you therefore have to
activate the specialisation by looking up and running the activation
script yourself. Running the activation script will create a new
Home Manager generation.
:::

For example, to activate the "dark" specialisation, you can
first look up your current Home Manager generation by running

```console
$ home-manager generations | head -1
2022-05-02 22:49 : id 1758 -> /nix/store/jy…ac-home-manager-generation
```

then run

```console
$ /nix/store/jy…ac-home-manager-generation/specialisation/dark/activate
Starting Home Manager activation
…
```

::: {.warning}
Since this option is experimental, the activation process may
change in backwards incompatible ways.
:::

options.title: systemd.user.startServices
options.description:
Whether new or changed services that are wanted by active targets
should be started. Additionally, stop obsolete services from the
previous generation.

The alternatives are

`suggest` (or `false`)
: Use a very simple shell script to print suggested
  {command}`systemctl` commands to run. You will have to
  manually run those commands after the switch.

`sd-switch` (or `true`)
: Use sd-switch, a tool that determines the necessary changes and
  automatically apply them.

options.title: targets.genericLinux.gpu.nvidia.sha256
options.type: null or string matching the pattern sha256-.*=
options.description:
The hash of the downloaded driver file. It can be obtained by
running, for example,

```sh
nix store prefetch-file https://download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86_64/@VERSION@/NVIDIA-Linux-x86_64-@VERSION@.run
```

where `@VERSION@` is replaced with the exact driver version.
If you are on ARM, replace Linux-x86_64 with Linux-aarch64.

options.title: targets.genericLinux.gpu.nvidia.version

options.description:

match the version of the driver used by the host OS.
