options.title: targets.genericLinux.nixGL.defaultWrapper
options.description:
The package wrapper function available for use as `(config.lib.nixGL.wrap
<package>)`. Intended to start programs on the main GPU.

Wrapper functions can be found under `config.lib.nixGL.wrappers`. They
can be used directly, however, setting this option provides a convenient
shorthand.

The following wrappers are available:
- mesa
- mesaPrime
- nvidia
- nvidiaPrime

options.title: targets.genericLinux.nixGL.installScripts
options.description:
For each wrapper `wrp` named in the provided list, a wrapper script
named `nixGLWrp` is installed into the environment. These scripts are
useful for running programs not installed via Home Manager.

The following wrappers are available:
- mesa
- mesaPrime
- nvidia
- nvidiaPrime

options.title: targets.genericLinux.nixGL.offloadWrapper
options.description:
The package wrapper function available for use as
`(config.lib.nixGL.wrapOffload <package>)`. Intended to start programs
on the secondary GPU.

Wrapper functions can be found under `config.lib.nixGL.wrappers`. They
can be used directly, however, setting this option provides a convenient
shorthand.

The following wrappers are available:
- mesa
- mesaPrime
- nvidia
- nvidiaPrime

options.title: targets.genericLinux.nixGL.packages
options.description:
The nixGL package set containing GPU library wrappers. This can be used
to provide OpenGL and Vulkan access to applications on non-NixOS systems
by using `(config.lib.nixGL.wrap <package>)` for the default wrapper, or
`(config.lib.nixGL.wrappers.<wrapper> <package>)` for any available
wrapper.

The wrapper functions are always available. If this option is empty (the
default), they are a no-op. This is useful on NixOS where the wrappers
are unnecessary.

Note that using any Nvidia wrapper requires building the configuration
with the `--impure` option.

options.title: targets.genericLinux.nixGL.prime.card
options.description:
Selects the non-default graphics card used for PRIME render offloading.
The value can be:

- a number, selecting the n-th non-default GPU;
- a PCI bus id in the form `pci-XXX_YY_ZZ_U`;
- a PCI id in the form `vendor_id:device_id`

For more information, consult the Mesa documentation on the `DRI_PRIME`
environment variable.

options.title: targets.genericLinux.nixGL.prime.installScript
options.description:
If this option is set, the wrapper script `prime-offload` is installed
into the environment. It allows starting programs on the secondary GPU
selected by the `nixGL.prime.card` option. This makes sense when the
program is not already using one of nixGL PRIME wrappers, or for
programs not installed from Nixpkgs.

This option can be set to either "mesa" or "nvidia", making the script
use one or the other graphics library.

options.title: targets.genericLinux.nixGL.prime.nvidiaProvider
options.description:
If this option is set, it overrides the offload provider for Nvidia
PRIME offloading. Consult the proprietary Nvidia driver documentation
on the `__NV_PRIME_RENDER_OFFLOAD_PROVIDER` environment variable.

options.title: targets.genericLinux.nixGL.vulkan.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable Vulkan in nixGL wrappers.

This is disabled by default bacause Vulkan brings in several libraries
that can cause symbol version conflicts in wrapped programs. Your
mileage may vary.

options.title: uninstall
options.description:
Whether to set up a minimal configuration that will remove all managed
files and packages.

Use this with extreme care since running the generated activation script
will remove all Home Manager state from your user environment. This
includes removing all your historic Home Manager generations.

options.title: vdirsyncer.auth
options.description: Authentication settings. The default is `basic`.

options.title: vdirsyncer.authCert
options.type: null or string or list of string
options.description:
Either a path to a certificate with a client certificate and
the key or a list of paths to the files with them.

options.title: vdirsyncer.clientIdCommand
options.description:
A command that prints the OAuth credentials to standard
output.

See
<https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/config.html#google>
for more information.

options.title: vdirsyncer.clientSecretCommand
options.description:
A command that prints the OAuth credentials to standard
output.

See
<https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/config.html#google>
for more information.

options.title: vdirsyncer.collections
options.type: null or (list of (string or list of string))
options.description: The collections to synchronize between the storages.

options.title: vdirsyncer.conflictResolution
options.type: null or one of "remote wins", "local wins" or list of string
options.description:
What to do in case of a conflict between the storages. Either
`remote wins` or
`local wins` or
a list that contains a command to run. By default, an error
message is printed.

options.title: vdirsyncer.enable
options.description: Whether to enable synchronization using vdirsyncer.

options.title: vdirsyncer.itemTypes
options.description:
Kinds of items to show. The default is to show everything.
This depends on particular features of the server, the results
are not validated.

options.title: vdirsyncer.metadata
options.description:
Metadata keys that should be synchronized when vdirsyncer
metasync is executed.

options.title: vdirsyncer.partialSync
options.description:
What should happen if synchronization in one direction
is impossible due to one storage being read-only.
Defaults to `revert`.

See
<https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/config.html#pair-section>
for more information.

options.title: vdirsyncer.postHook
options.description:
Command to call for each item creation and modification.
The command will be called with the path of the new/updated
file.

options.title: vdirsyncer.timeRange
options.description:
A time range to synchronize. start and end can be any Python
expression that returns a `datetime.datetime`
object.

options.title: vdirsyncer.timeRange.end
options.description: End of time range to show.

options.title: vdirsyncer.timeRange.start
options.description: Start of time range to show.

options.title: vdirsyncer.tokenFile
options.description: A file path where access tokens are stored.

options.title: vdirsyncer.urlCommand
options.description: A command that prints the URL of the storage.

options.title: vdirsyncer.useVcard4
options.description:
Specifies whether vdirsyncer should request vCards in version 4.0.
If set to `false` then vdirsyncer will default to version 3.0.

options.title: vdirsyncer.userAgent
options.description:
The user agent to report to the server. Defaults to
`vdirsyncer`.

options.title: vdirsyncer.userNameCommand
options.description: A command that prints the user name to standard output.

options.title: vdirsyncer.verify
options.description: Null or path to certificate to verify SSL against

options.title: vdirsyncer.verifyFingerprint
options.description:
Optional. SHA1 or MD5 fingerprint of the expected server certificate.

See
<https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/ssl-tutorial.html#ssl-tutorial>
for more information.

options.title: wayland.systemd.target
options.description:
The systemd target that will automatically start the graphical Wayland services.
This option is a generalization of individual `systemd.target` or `systemdTarget`,
and affect all Wayland services by default.

When setting this value to `"sway-session.target"`,
make sure to also enable {option}`wayland.windowManager.sway.systemd.enable`,
otherwise the service may never be started.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable configuration for Hyprland, a tiling Wayland
compositor that doesn't sacrifice on its looks.

::: {.note}
This module configures Hyprland and adds it to your user's {env}`PATH`,
but does not make certain system-level changes. NixOS users should
enable the NixOS module with {option}`programs.hyprland.enable`, which
makes system-level changes such as adding a desktop session entry.
:::

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.extraConfig
options.description: Extra configuration lines to add to `~/.config/hypr/hyprland.conf`.
options.example:
''
  # window resize
  bind = $mod, S, submap, resize
  
  submap = resize
  binde = , right, resizeactive, 10 0
  binde = , left, resizeactive, -10 0
  binde = , up, resizeactive, 0 -10
  binde = , down, resizeactive, 0 10
  bind = , escape, submap, reset
  submap = reset
''

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.finalPackage
options.description: The Hyprland package after applying configuration.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.finalPortalPackage
options.description: The xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland package after overriding its hyprland input.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.importantPrefixes
options.description: List of prefix of attributes to source at the top of the config.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.package
options.description: The hyprland package to use. Set this to null if you use the NixOS module to install Hyprland.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.plugins
options.description:
List of Hyprland plugins to use. Can either be packages or
absolute plugin paths.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.portalPackage
options.description: The xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland package to use.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.settings
options.description:
Hyprland configuration written in Nix. Entries with the same key
should be written as lists. Variables' and colors' names should be
quoted. See <https://wiki.hypr.land> for more examples.

::: {.note}
Use the [](#opt-wayland.windowManager.hyprland.plugins) option to
declare plugins.
:::

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.sourceFirst
options.description:
Whether to enable putting source entries at the top of the configuration
.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.submaps
options.description:
Attribute set of Hyprland submaps.

See <https://wiki.hypr.land/Configuring/Binds#submaps> to learn about submaps

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.submaps.<name>.settings
options.description: Hyprland binds to be put in the submap

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.systemd.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable {file}`hyprland-session.target` on
hyprland startup. This links to `graphical-session.target`.
Some important environment variables will be imported to systemd
and D-Bus user environment before reaching the target, including
- `DISPLAY`
- `HYPRLAND_INSTANCE_SIGNATURE`
- `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`
- `XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP`

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.systemd.enableXdgAutostart
options.description:
Whether to enable autostart of applications using
{manpage}`systemd-xdg-autostart-generator(8)`.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.systemd.extraCommands
options.description: Extra commands to be run after D-Bus activation.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.systemd.variables
options.description:
Environment variables to be imported in the systemd & D-Bus user
environment.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.hyprland.xwayland.enable
options.description: Whether to enable XWayland.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.autostart
options.description: Command to autostart when labwc start.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Labwc, a wayland window-stacking compositor.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.environment
options.description: Environment variable to add when labwc start.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.extraConfig
options.description: Extra lines appended to {file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/labwc/rc.xml`.
options.example:
''
  <tablet mapToOutput="" rotate="0" mouseEmulation="no">
    <!-- Active area dimensions are in mm -->
    <area top="0.0" left="0.0" width="0.0" height="0.0" />
    <map button="Tip" to="Left" />
    <map button="Stylus" to="Right" />
    <map button="Stylus2" to="Middle" />
  </tablet>
''

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.menu
options.description: Config to configure labwc menu

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.package
options.description: The labwc package to use. Set to `null` to use Nixos labwc package.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.rc
options.description:
Config to configure labwc options.
Use "@attributes" for attributes.
See <https://labwc.github.io/labwc-config.5.html> for configuration.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.systemd.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable {file}`labwc-session.target` on
labwc startup. This links to {file}`graphical-session.target`.
Some important environment variables will be imported to systemd
and D-Bus user environment before reaching the target, including
- `DISPLAY`
- `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`
- `XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP`

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.systemd.extraCommands
options.description: Extra commands to be run after D-Bus activation.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.systemd.variables
options.description:
Environment variables to be imported in the systemd & D-Bus user
environment.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.labwc.xwayland.enable
options.description: Whether to enable XWayland.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.river.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the river window manager.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.river.extraConfig
options.description: Extra lines appended to {file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/river/init`.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.river.extraSessionVariables
options.description: Extra session variables set when running the compositor.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.river.package
options.description:
The river-classic package to use. Set to `null` to not add any river package to your path.
This should be done if you want to use the NixOS river module to install river.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.river.settings
options.description: General settings given to `riverctl`.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.river.systemd.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable {file}`river-session.target` on
river startup. This links to {file}`graphical-session.target`}.
Some important environment variables will be imported to systemd
and D-Bus user environment before reaching the target, including
- `DISPLAY`
- `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`
- `XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP`
- `NIXOS_OZONE_WL`
- `XCURSOR_THEME`
- `XCURSOR_SIZE`

options.title: wayland.windowManager.river.systemd.extraCommands
options.description: Extra commands to be run after D-Bus activation.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.river.systemd.variables
options.description:
Environment variables to be imported in the systemd & D-Bus user
environment.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.river.xwayland.enable
options.description: Whether to enable XWayland.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.checkConfig
options.description: If enabled, validates the generated config file.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config
options.description: Sway configuration options.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.assigns
options.type: attribute set of list of attribute set of (string or boolean)
options.description:
An attribute set that assigns applications to workspaces based
on criteria.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars
options.description: Sway bars settings blocks. Set to empty list to remove bars completely.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors
options.description:
Bar color settings. All color classes can be specified using submodules
with 'border', 'background', 'text', fields and RGB color hex-codes as values.
See default values for the reference.
Note that 'background', 'status', and 'separator' parameters take a single RGB value.

See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#_colors>.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.activeWorkspace
options.description: Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace is active.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.background
options.description: Background color of the bar.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.bindingMode
options.description: Border, background and text color for the binding mode indicator
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.focusedBackground
options.description: Background color of the bar on the currently focused monitor output.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.focusedSeparator
options.description: Text color to be used for the separator on the currently focused monitor output.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.focusedStatusline
options.description: Text color to be used for the statusline on the currently focused monitor output.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.focusedWorkspace
options.description: Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace has focus.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.inactiveWorkspace
options.description:
Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace does not
have focus and is not active.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.separator
options.description: Text color to be used for the separator.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.statusline
options.description: Text color to be used for the statusline.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.colors.urgentWorkspace
options.description:
Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace contains
a window with the urgency hint set.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.command
options.description: Command that will be used to start a bar.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.extraConfig
options.description: Extra configuration lines for this bar.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.fonts
options.description: Font configuration for this bar.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.hiddenState
options.description: The default bar mode when 'bar.mode' == 'hide'.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.id
options.description:
Specifies the bar ID for the configured bar instance.
If this option is missing, the ID is set to bar-x, where x corresponds
to the position of the embedding bar block in the config file.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.mode
options.description: Bar visibility mode.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.position
options.description: The edge of the screen swaybar should show up.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.statusCommand
options.description: Command that will be used to get status lines.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.trayOutput
options.description: Where to output tray.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.trayPadding
options.description:
Sets the pixel padding of the system tray.
This padding will surround the tray on all sides and between each item.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.workspaceButtons
options.description: Whether workspace buttons should be shown or not.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bars.*.workspaceNumbers
options.description: Whether workspace numbers should be displayed within the workspace buttons.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bindkeysToCode
options.description: Whether to make use of {option}`--to-code` in keybindings.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bindswitches
options.description:
Binds <switch> to execute the sway command command on state changes. Supported switches are lid (laptop
lid) and tablet (tablet mode) switches. Valid values for state are on, off and toggle. These switches are
on when the device lid is shut and when tablet mode is active respectively. toggle is also supported to run
a command both when the switch is toggled on or off.
See sway(5).

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bindswitches.<name>.action
options.description: The sway command to execute on state changes

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bindswitches.<name>.locked
options.description:
Unless the flag --locked is set, the command
will not be run when a screen locking program
is active. If there is a matching binding with
and without --locked, the one with will be preferred
when locked and the one without will be
preferred when unlocked.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.bindswitches.<name>.reload
options.description:
If the --reload flag is given, the binding will
also be executed when the config is reloaded.
toggle bindings will not be executed on reload.
The --locked flag will operate as normal so if
the config is reloaded while locked and
--locked is not given, the binding will not be
executed.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.colors
options.description:
Color settings. All color classes can be specified using submodules
with 'border', 'background', 'text', 'indicator' and 'childBorder' fields
and RGB color hex-codes as values. See default values for the reference.
Note that 'sway.config.colors.background' parameter takes a single RGB value.

See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#_changing_colors>.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.colors.background
options.description:
Background color of the window. Only applications which do not cover
the whole area expose the color.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.colors.focused
options.description: A window which currently has the focus.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.colors.focusedInactive
options.description:
A window which is the focused one of its container,
but it does not have the focus at the moment.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.colors.placeholder
options.description:
Background and text color are used to draw placeholder window
contents (when restoring layouts). Border and indicator are ignored.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.colors.unfocused
options.description: A window which is not focused.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.colors.urgent
options.description: A window which has its urgency hint activated.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.defaultWorkspace
options.description:
The default workspace to show when sway is launched.
This must to correspond to the value of the keybinding of the default workspace.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.down
options.description: Home row direction key for moving down.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.floating
options.description: Floating window settings.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.floating.border
options.description: Floating windows border width.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.floating.criteria
options.type: list of attribute set of (string or boolean)
options.description: List of criteria for windows that should be opened in a floating mode.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.floating.modifier
options.description: Modifier key or keys that can be used to drag floating windows.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.floating.titlebar
options.description: Whether to show floating window titlebars.
options.default:
''
  true for state version ≥ 23.05
  false for state version < 23.05
''

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.focus
options.description: Focus related settings.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.focus.followMouse
options.description: Whether focus should follow the mouse.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.focus.forceWrapping
options.description:
Whether to force focus wrapping in tabbed or stacked containers.

This option is deprecated, use {option}`focus.wrapping` instead.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.focus.mouseWarping
options.description:
Whether mouse cursor should be warped to the center of the window when switching focus
to a window on a different output.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.focus.newWindow
options.description:
This option modifies focus behavior on new window activation.

See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#focus_on_window_activation>

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.focus.wrapping
options.description:
Whether the window focus commands automatically wrap around the edge of containers.

See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#_focus_wrapping>

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.fonts
options.description: Font configuration for window titles, nagbar...

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps
options.description: Gaps related settings.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps.bottom
options.description: Bottom gaps value.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps.horizontal
options.description: Horizontal gaps value.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps.inner
options.description: Inner gaps value.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps.left
options.description: Left gaps value.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps.outer
options.description: Outer gaps value.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps.right
options.description: Right gaps value.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps.smartBorders
options.description:
This option controls whether to disable container borders on
workspace with a single container.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps.smartGaps
options.description:
This option controls whether to disable all gaps (outer and inner)
on workspace with a single container.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps.top
options.description: Top gaps value.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.gaps.vertical
options.description: Vertical gaps value.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.input
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of string
options.description:
An attribute set that defines input modules. See
{manpage}`sway-input(5)`
for options.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.keybindings
options.description:
An attribute set that assigns a key press to an action using a key symbol.
See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#keybindings>.

Consider to use `lib.mkOptionDefault` function to extend or override
default keybindings instead of specifying all of them from scratch.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.keycodebindings
options.description:
An attribute set that assigns keypress to an action using key code.
See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#keybindings>.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.left
options.description: Home row direction key for moving left.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.menu
options.description: Default launcher to use.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.modes
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of string
options.description:
An attribute set that defines binding modes and keybindings
inside them

Only basic keybinding is supported (bindsym keycomb action),
for more advanced setup use 'sway.extraConfig'.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.modifier
options.description: Modifier key that is used for all default keybindings.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.output
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of string
options.description:
An attribute set that defines output modules. See
{manpage}`sway-output(5)`
for options.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.right
options.description: Home row direction key for moving right.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.seat
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of string
options.description:
An attribute set that defines seat modules. See
{manpage}`sway-input(5)`
for options.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.startup
options.description:
Commands that should be executed at startup.

See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#_automatically_starting_applications_on_i3_startup>.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.startup.*.always
options.description: Whether to run command on each sway restart.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.startup.*.command
options.description: Command that will be executed on startup.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.terminal
options.description: Default terminal to run.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.up
options.description: Home row direction key for moving up.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.window
options.description: Window titlebar and border settings.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.window.border
options.description: Window border width.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.window.commands
options.description:
List of commands that should be executed on specific windows.
See {option}`for_window` swaywm option documentation.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.window.commands.*.command
options.description: Swaywm command to execute.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.window.commands.*.criteria
options.description:
Criteria of the windows on which command should be executed.

A value of `true` is equivalent to using an empty
criteria (which is different from an empty string criteria).

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.window.hideEdgeBorders
options.type: one of "none", "vertical", "horizontal", "both", "smart", "smart_no_gaps", "--i3 none", "--i3 vertical", "--i3 horizontal", "--i3 both", "--i3 smart", "--i3 smart_no_gaps"
options.description: Hide window borders adjacent to the screen edges.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.window.titlebar
options.description: Whether to show window titlebars.
options.default:
''
  true for state version ≥ 23.05
  false for state version < 23.05
''

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.workspaceAutoBackAndForth
options.description:
Assume you are on workspace "1: www" and switch to "2: IM" using
mod+2 because somebody sent you a message. You don’t need to remember
where you came from now, you can just press $mod+2 again to switch
back to "1: www".

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.workspaceLayout
options.description:
The mode in which new containers on workspace level will
start.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.workspaceOutputAssign
options.description: Assign workspaces to outputs.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.workspaceOutputAssign.*.output
options.description:
Name(s) of the output(s) from {command}`
  swaymsg -t get_outputs
`.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.config.workspaceOutputAssign.*.workspace
options.description: Name of the workspace to assign.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.enable
options.description: Whether to enable sway wayland compositor.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.extraConfig
options.description: Extra configuration lines to add to ~/.config/sway/config.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.extraConfigEarly
options.description: Like extraConfig, except lines are added to ~/.config/sway/config before all other configuration.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.extraOptions
options.description:
Command line arguments passed to launch Sway. Please DO NOT report
issues if you use an unsupported GPU (proprietary drivers).

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.extraSessionCommands
options.description: Shell commands executed just before Sway is started.
options.example:
''
  export SDL_VIDEODRIVER=wayland
  # needs qt5.qtwayland in systemPackages
  export QT_QPA_PLATFORM=wayland
  export QT_WAYLAND_DISABLE_WINDOWDECORATION="1"
  # Fix for some Java AWT applications (e.g. Android Studio),
  # use this if they aren't displayed properly:
  export _JAVA_AWT_WM_NONREPARENTING=1
''

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.package
options.description:
Sway package to use. Will override the options
'wrapperFeatures', 'extraSessionCommands', and 'extraOptions'.
Set to `null` to not add any Sway package to your
path. This should be done if you want to use the NixOS Sway
module to install Sway. Beware setting to `null` will also disable
reloading Sway when new config is activated.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.swaynag.enable
options.description: Whether to enable configuration of swaynag, a lightweight error bar for sway.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.swaynag.settings
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (Swaynag config atom (null, bool, int, float, str))
options.description:
Configuration written to
{file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/swaynag/config`.

See
{manpage}`swaynag(5)`
for a list of available options and an example configuration.
Note, configurations declared under `<config>`
will override the default type values of swaynag.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.systemd.dbusImplementation
options.description:
The D-Bus implementation used on the system.
This affects which tool is used to import environment variables when starting the Sway session.
On NixOS, this should match the value of the option [`services.dbus.implementation` (NixOS)](https://nixos.org/manual/nixos/stable/options#opt-services.dbus.implementation).
When set to `dbus`, `dbus-update-activation-environment --systemd <variables>` is run.
Otherwise, when set to `broker`, `systemctl --user import-environment <variables>` is run.
See <https://github.com/swaywm/sway/wiki#systemd-and-dbus-activation-environments> for more documentation.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.systemd.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable {file}`sway-session.target` on
sway startup. This links to
{file}`graphical-session.target`.
Some important environment variables will be imported to systemd
and dbus user environment before reaching the target, including
* {env}`DISPLAY`
* {env}`WAYLAND_DISPLAY`
* {env}`SWAYSOCK`
* {env}`XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP`
* {env}`XDG_SESSION_TYPE`
* {env}`NIXOS_OZONE_WL`
* {env}`XCURSOR_THEME`
* {env}`XCURSOR_SIZE`
You can extend this list using the `systemd.variables` option.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.systemd.extraCommands
options.description: Extra commands to run after D-Bus activation.
options.default:
[
  "systemctl --user reset-failed"
  "systemctl --user start sway-session.target"
  "swaymsg -mt subscribe '[]' || true"
  "systemctl --user stop sway-session.target"
]

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.systemd.variables
options.description: Environment variables imported into the systemd and D-Bus user environment.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.systemd.xdgAutostart
options.description:
Whether to enable autostart of applications using
{manpage}`systemd-xdg-autostart-generator(8)`
.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.wrapperFeatures
options.description: Attribute set of features to enable in the wrapper.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.wrapperFeatures.base
options.description:
Whether to make use of the base wrapper to execute extra session commands and prepend a
dbus-run-session to the sway command.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.wrapperFeatures.gtk
options.description:
Whether to make use of the wrapGAppsHook wrapper to execute sway with required environment
variables for GTK applications.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.sway.xwayland
options.description: Enable xwayland, which is needed for the default configuration of sway.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Wayfire, a wayland compositor based on wlroots.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.package
options.description:
The wayfire package to use. Set to `null` to not add any wayfire package to your path.
This should be done if you want to use the NixOS wayfire module to install wayfire.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.plugins
options.description: Additional plugins to use with wayfire

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.settings
options.type: open submodule of attribute set of attribute set of (string or absolute path or boolean or signed integer or floating point number)
options.description:
Wayfire configuration written in Nix.

See <https://github.com/WayfireWM/wayfire/wiki/Configuration>

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.settings.core.plugins
options.description: Load the specified plugins

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.systemd.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable {file}`wayfire-session.target` on
wayfire startup. This links to {file}`graphical-session.target`}.
Some important environment variables will be imported to systemd
and D-Bus user environment before reaching the target, including
- `DISPLAY`
- `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`
- `XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP`
- `NIXOS_OZONE_WL`
- `XCURSOR_THEME`
- `XCURSOR_SIZE`

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.systemd.extraCommands
options.description: Extra commands to be run after D-Bus activation.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.systemd.variables
options.description:
Environment variables to be imported in the systemd & D-Bus user
environment.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.wf-shell.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Manage wf-shell Configuration.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.wf-shell.package
options.description: The wf-shell package to use.

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.wf-shell.settings
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (string or absolute path or boolean or signed integer or floating point number)
options.description:
Wf-shell configuration written in Nix.

See <https://github.com/WayfireWM/wf-shell/blob/master/wf-shell.ini.example>

options.title: wayland.windowManager.wayfire.xwayland.enable
options.description: Whether to enable XWayland.

options.title: xdg.autostart.enable
options.description: Whether to enable creation of XDG autostart entries.

options.title: xdg.autostart.entries
options.description: Paths to desktop files that should be linked to `XDG_CONFIG_HOME/autostart`

options.title: xdg.autostart.readOnly
options.description:
Make `XDG_CONFIG_HOME/autostart` a symlink to a readonly directory so that
programs cannot install arbitrary autostart services.

options.title: xdg.cacheFile
options.description:
Attribute set of files to link into the user's XDG
cache home.

options.title: xdg.cacheFile.<name>.enable
options.description:
Whether this file should be generated. This option allows specific
files to be disabled.

options.title: xdg.cacheFile.<name>.executable
options.description:
Set the execute bit. If `null`, defaults to the mode
of the {var}`source` file or to `false`
for files created through the {var}`text` option.

options.title: xdg.cacheFile.<name>.force
options.description:
Whether the target path should be unconditionally replaced
by the managed file source. Warning, this will silently
delete the target regardless of whether it is a file or
link.

options.title: xdg.cacheFile.<name>.ignorelinks
options.description:
When `recursive` is enabled, adds `-ignorelinks` flag to lndir

It causes lndir to not treat symbolic links in the source directory specially.
The link created in the target directory will point back to the corresponding
(symbolic link) file in the source directory. If the link is to a directory

options.title: xdg.cacheFile.<name>.onChange
options.description:
Shell commands to run when file has changed between
generations. The script will be run
*after* the new files have been linked
into place.

Note, this code is always run when `recursive` is
enabled.

options.title: xdg.cacheFile.<name>.recursive
options.description:
If the file source is a directory, then this option
determines whether the directory should be recursively
linked to the target location. This option has no effect
if the source is a file.

If `false` (the default) then the target
will be a symbolic link to the source directory. If
`true` then the target will be a
directory structure matching the source's but whose leafs
are symbolic links to the files of the source directory.

options.title: xdg.cacheFile.<name>.source
options.description:
Path of the source file or directory. If
[](#opt-xdg.cacheFile._name_.text)
is non-null then this option will automatically point to a file
containing that text.

options.title: xdg.cacheFile.<name>.target
options.description: Path to target file relative to {var}`xdg.cacheHome`.

options.title: xdg.cacheFile.<name>.text
options.description:
Text of the file. If this option is null then
[](#opt-xdg.cacheFile._name_.source)
must be set.

options.title: xdg.cacheHome
options.description:
Absolute path to directory holding application caches.

Sets `XDG_CACHE_HOME` for the user if `xdg.enable` is set `true`.

options.title: xdg.dataHome
options.description:
Absolute path to directory holding application data.

Sets `XDG_DATA_HOME` for the user if `xdg.enable` is set `true`.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries
options.description:
Desktop Entries allow applications to be shown in your desktop environment's app launcher.

You can define entries for programs without entries or override existing entries.

See <https://specifications.freedesktop.org/desktop-entry-spec/latest/recognized-keys.html> for more information on options.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.actions
options.description: The set of actions made available to application launchers.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.actions.<name>.exec
options.description: Program to execute, possibly with arguments.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.actions.<name>.icon
options.type: null or string or absolute path
options.description: Icon to display in file manager, menus, etc.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.actions.<name>.name
options.description: Name of the action.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.categories
options.description: Categories in which the entry should be shown in a menu.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.comment
options.description: Tooltip for the entry.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.exec
options.description: Program to execute, possibly with arguments.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.genericName
options.description: Generic name of the application.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.icon
options.type: null or string or absolute path
options.description: Icon to display in file manager, menus, etc.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.mimeType
options.description: The MIME type(s) supported by this application.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.name
options.description: Specific name of the application.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.noDisplay
options.description:
Means "this application exists, but don't display it in the menus".
This can be useful to e.g. associate this application with MIME types.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.prefersNonDefaultGPU
options.description: If true, the application prefers to be run on a more powerful discrete GPU if available.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.settings
options.description:
Extra key-value pairs to add to the `[Desktop Entry]` section.
This may override other values.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.startupNotify
options.description:
If true, it is KNOWN that the application will send a "remove"
message when started with the `DESKTOP_STARTUP_ID`
environment variable set. If false, it is KNOWN that the application
does not work with startup notification at all.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.terminal
options.description: Whether the program runs in a terminal window.

options.title: xdg.desktopEntries.<name>.type
options.description: The type of the desktop entry.

options.title: xdg.enable
options.description: Whether to enable management of XDG base directories.

options.title: xdg.mime.desktopFileUtilsPackage
options.description: The desktop-file-utils package to use. Used when running update-desktop-database.

options.title: xdg.mime.enable
options.description:
Whether to install programs and files to support the
XDG Shared MIME-info specification and XDG MIME Applications
specification at
<https://specifications.freedesktop.org/shared-mime-info-spec/shared-mime-info-spec-latest.html>
and
<https://specifications.freedesktop.org/mime-apps-spec/mime-apps-spec-latest.html>,
respectively.
options.default: true if host platform is Linux, false otherwise

options.title: xdg.mime.sharedMimeInfoPackage
options.description: The shared-mime-info package to use. Used when running update-mime-database.

options.title: xdg.mimeApps.associations.added
options.type: attribute set of ((list of string) or ((list of string) or string) convertible to it)
options.description:
Defines additional associations of applications with
mimetypes, as if the .desktop file was listing this mimetype
in the first place.

options.title: xdg.mimeApps.associations.removed
options.type: attribute set of ((list of string) or ((list of string) or string) convertible to it)
options.description:
Removes associations of applications with mimetypes, as if the
.desktop file was *not* listing this
mimetype in the first place.

options.title: xdg.mimeApps.defaultApplicationPackages
options.description:
Packages whose `.desktop` files will be used to establish default
mimetype associations.

These associations are appended to the associations in
[](#opt-xdg.mimeApps.defaultApplications). If multiple packages
associate with the same mime type, then the priority among them is
determined by their order in the list.

options.title: xdg.mimeApps.defaultApplications
options.type: attribute set of ((list of string) or ((list of string) or string) convertible to it)
options.description:
The default application to be used for a given mimetype. This
is, for instance, the one that will be started when
double-clicking on a file in a file manager. If the
application is no longer installed, the next application in
the list is attempted, and so on.

options.title: xdg.mimeApps.enable
options.description:
Whether to manage {file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mimeapps.list`.

The generated file is read-only.

options.title: xdg.portal.config
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (string or ((list of string) or string) convertible to it)
options.description:
Sets which portal backend should be used to provide the implementation
for the requested interface. For details check {manpage}`portals.conf(5)`.

These will be written with the name `$desktop-portals.conf` for
`xdg.portal.config.$desktop` and `portals.conf` for
`xdg.portal.config.common` as an exception.

These, together with `xdg.portal.configPackages`, will be joined into a
directory and passed to `xdg-desktop-portal.service` through a
`NIXOS_XDG_DESKTOP_PORTAL_CONFIG_DIR` variable.

options.title: xdg.portal.configPackages
options.description:
List of packages that provide XDG desktop portal configuration, usually in
the form of `share/xdg-desktop-portal/$desktop-portals.conf`.

Note that configs in `xdg.portal.config` will be preferred if set.

options.title: xdg.portal.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable [XDG desktop integration](https://github.com/flatpak/xdg-desktop-portal).

Note, if you installed Home Manager via its NixOS module and
'home-manager.useUserPackages' is enabled, make sure to add

``` nix
environment.pathsToLink = [ "/share/xdg-desktop-portal" "/share/applications" ];
```

to your NixOS configuration so that the portal definitions and DE
provided configurations get linked.

options.title: xdg.portal.extraPortals
options.description:
List of additional portals that should be added to the environment.

The directory where the portal definitions have been merged together
(likely `~/.nix-profile/share/xdg-desktop-portal/portals`) will get
passed to `xdg-desktop-portal.service` via the
`NIX_XDG_DESKTOP_PORTAL_DIR` environment variable.

Portals allow interaction with system, like choosing files or taking
screenshots. At minimum, a desktop portal implementation should be
listed.

options.title: xdg.portal.xdgOpenUsePortal
options.description:
Sets environment variable `NIXOS_XDG_OPEN_USE_PORTAL` to `1`
This will make `xdg-open` use the portal to open programs, which resolves bugs involving
programs opening inside FHS envs or with unexpected env vars set from wrappers.
See [#160923](https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/issues/160923) for more info.

options.title: xdg.stateHome

Absolute path to directory holding application states.

Sets `XDG_STATE_HOME` for the user if `xdg.enable` is set `true`.

options.title: xdg.systemDirs.config
options.description:
Directory names to add to {env}`XDG_CONFIG_DIRS`
in the user session.

options.title: xdg.systemDirs.data
options.description:
Directory names to add to {env}`XDG_DATA_DIRS`
in the user session.

options.title: xdg.terminal-exec.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable xdg-terminal-exec, the
[proposed](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/xdg/xdg-specs/-/merge_requests/46)
Default Terminal Execution Specification.

options.title: xdg.terminal-exec.package
options.description: The xdg-terminal-exec package to use.

options.title: xdg.terminal-exec.settings
options.type: attribute set of list of string
options.description:
Configuration options for the Default Terminal Execution Specification.

The keys are the desktop environments that are matched (case-insensitively) against {env}`XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP`,
or `default` which is used when the current desktop environment is not found in the configuration.
The values are a list of terminals' [desktop file IDs](https://specifications.freedesktop.org/desktop-entry-spec/latest/ar01s02.html#desktop-file-id) to try in order of decreasing priority.

options.title: xdg.userDirs.createDirectories
options.description: Whether to enable automatic creation of the XDG user directories.

options.title: xdg.userDirs.documents
options.type: null or (string or absolute path convertible to it)
options.description: The Documents directory.

options.title: xdg.userDirs.download
options.type: null or (string or absolute path convertible to it)
options.description: The Downloads directory.

options.title: xdg.userDirs.enable
options.description:
Whether to manage {file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/user-dirs.dirs`.

The generated file is read-only.

options.title: xdg.userDirs.extraConfig
options.type: attribute set of (string or absolute path convertible to it)

options.title: xdg.userDirs.music
options.type: null or (string or absolute path convertible to it)
options.description: The Music directory.

options.title: xdg.userDirs.pictures
options.type: null or (string or absolute path convertible to it)
options.description: The Pictures directory.

options.title: xdg.userDirs.publicShare

options.title: xdg.userDirs.templates

options.title: xdg.userDirs.videos
options.type: null or (string or absolute path convertible to it)

options.title: xfconf.settings
options.description: Settings to write to the Xfconf configuration system.

options.title: xresources.extraConfig
options.description:
Additional X server resources contents.
If this and all other xresources options are
`null`, then this feature is disabled and no
{file}`~/.Xresources` link is produced.

options.title: xresources.path
options.description: Path where Home Manager should link the {file}`.Xresources` file.

options.title: xresources.properties
options.type: null or (attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or floating point number or string or list of (boolean or signed integer or floating point number or string)))
options.description:
X server resources that should be set.
Booleans are formatted as "true" or "false" respectively.
List elements are recursively formatted as a string and joined by commas.
All other values are directly formatted using builtins.toString.
Note, that 2-dimensional lists are not supported and specifying one will throw an exception.
If this and all other xresources options are
`null`, then this feature is disabled and no
{file}`~/.Xresources` link is produced.

options.title: xsession.enable
options.description: Whether to enable X Session.

options.title: xsession.initExtra
options.description: Extra shell commands to run during initialization.

options.description: Alias of {option}`home.pointerCursor.x11.defaultCursor`.

options.description: Alias of {option}`home.pointerCursor.name`.

options.description: Alias of {option}`home.pointerCursor.package`.

options.title: xsession.pointerCursor.size
options.description: Alias of {option}`home.pointerCursor.size`.

options.title: xsession.preferStatusNotifierItems
options.description:
Whether tray applets should prefer using the Status Notifier
Items (SNI) protocol, commonly called App Indicators. Note,
not all tray applets or status bars support SNI.

options.title: xsession.profileExtra
options.description: Extra shell commands to run before session start.

options.description:
Path, relative to {env}`HOME`, where Home Manager

options.title: xsession.scriptPath
options.description:
Path, relative to {env}`HOME`, where Home Manager
should write the X session script.

options.description:
List of lua packages available for being
used in the Awesome configuration.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.awesome.noArgb
options.description:
Disable client transparency support, which can be greatly
detrimental to performance in some setups

options.title: xsession.windowManager.awesome.package
options.description: The awesome package to use. to use for running the Awesome WM

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.alwaysResetDesktops
options.description:
If set to `true`, desktops configured in {option}`monitors` will be reset
every time the config is run.

If set to `false`, desktops will only be configured the first time the config is run.
This is useful if you want to dynamically add desktops and you don't want them to be destroyed if you
re-run `bspwmrc`.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.enable
options.description: Whether to enable bspwm window manager.

options.description: Additional shell commands to be run at the end of the config file.

options.description: Like extraConfig, except commands are run at the start of the config file.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.monitors
options.type: attribute set of list of string
options.description: Specifies the names of desktops to create on each monitor.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.package
options.description: The bspwm package to use.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules
options.type: attribute set of (open submodule of attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or floating point number or string))
options.description: Rule configuration. The keys of the attribute set are the targets of the rules.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.border
options.description: Whether the node should have border.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.center
options.description: Whether the node will be put in the center, in floating mode.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.desktop
options.description: The desktop where the rule should be applied.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.focus
options.description: Whether the node should gain focus on creation.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.follow
options.description: Whether focus should follow the node when it is moved.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.hidden
options.description: Whether the node should occupy any space.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.layer
options.description: The layer where a new window should spawn.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.locked
options.description:
Whether the node should ignore {command}`node --close`
messages.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.manage
options.description:
Whether the window should be managed by bspwm. If false, the window
will be ignored by bspwm entirely. This is useful for overlay apps,
e.g. screenshot tools.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.marked
options.description: Whether the node will be marked for deferred actions.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.monitor
options.description: The monitor where the rule should be applied.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.node
options.description: The node where the rule should be applied.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.private
options.description: Whether the node should stay in the same tiling position and size.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.rectangle
options.description: The node's geometry, in the format `WxH+X+Y`.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.splitDir
options.description: The direction where the container is going to be split.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.splitRatio
options.description:
The ratio between the new window and the previous existing window in
the desktop.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.state
options.description: The state in which a new window should spawn.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.sticky
options.description: Whether the node should stay on the focused desktop.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.settings
options.type: attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or floating point number or string or list of (boolean or signed integer or floating point number or string))
options.description: General settings given to `bspc config`.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.bspwm.startupPrograms
options.description: Programs to be executed during startup.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.command
options.description:
Command to use to start the window manager.

The default value allows integration with NixOS' generated xserver configuration.

Extra actions and commands can be specified in {option}`xsession.initExtra`.

options.description:

options.description: i3 configuration options.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.assigns
options.type: attribute set of list of attribute set of (string or boolean)
options.description:
An attribute set that assigns applications to workspaces based
on criteria.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars
options.description: i3 bars settings blocks. Set to empty list to remove bars completely.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors
options.description:
Bar color settings. All color classes can be specified using submodules
with 'border', 'background', 'text', fields and RGB color hex-codes as values.
See default values for the reference.
Note that 'background', 'status', and 'separator' parameters take a single RGB value.

See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#_colors>.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.activeWorkspace
options.description: Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace is active.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.background
options.description: Background color of the bar.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.bindingMode
options.description: Border, background and text color for the binding mode indicator
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.focusedBackground
options.description: Background color of the bar on the currently focused monitor output.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.focusedSeparator
options.description: Text color to be used for the separator on the currently focused monitor output.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.focusedStatusline
options.description: Text color to be used for the statusline on the currently focused monitor output.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.focusedWorkspace
options.description: Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace has focus.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.inactiveWorkspace
options.description:
Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace does not
have focus and is not active.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.separator
options.description: Text color to be used for the separator.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.statusline
options.description: Text color to be used for the statusline.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.urgentWorkspace
options.description:
Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace contains
a window with the urgency hint set.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.command
options.description: Command that will be used to start a bar.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.extraConfig
options.description: Extra configuration lines for this bar.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.fonts
options.description: Font configuration for this bar.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.hiddenState
options.description: The default bar mode when 'bar.mode' == 'hide'.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.id
options.description:
Specifies the bar ID for the configured bar instance.
If this option is missing, the ID is set to bar-x, where x corresponds
to the position of the embedding bar block in the config file.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.mode
options.description: Bar visibility mode.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.position
options.description: The edge of the screen i3bar should show up.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.description: Command that will be used to get status lines.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.trayOutput
options.description: Where to output tray.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.trayPadding
options.description:
Sets the pixel padding of the system tray.
This padding will surround the tray on all sides and between each item.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.workspaceButtons
options.description: Whether workspace buttons should be shown or not.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.workspaceNumbers
options.description: Whether workspace numbers should be displayed within the workspace buttons.
options.default: null for state version ≥ 20.09, as example otherwise

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors
options.description:
Color settings. All color classes can be specified using submodules
with 'border', 'background', 'text', 'indicator' and 'childBorder' fields
and RGB color hex-codes as values. See default values for the reference.
Note that 'i3.config.colors.background' parameter takes a single RGB value.

See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#_changing_colors>.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors.background
options.description:
Background color of the window. Only applications which do not cover
the whole area expose the color.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors.focusedInactive
options.description:
A window which is the focused one of its container,
but it does not have the focus at the moment.

options.description:
Background and text color are used to draw placeholder window
contents (when restoring layouts). Border and indicator are ignored.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors.unfocused
options.description: A window which is not focused.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors.urgent
options.description: A window which has its urgency hint activated.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.defaultWorkspace
options.description:

This must to correspond to the value of the keybinding of the default workspace.

options.description: Floating window settings.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.floating.border
options.description: Floating windows border width.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.floating.criteria
options.type: list of attribute set of (string or boolean)
options.description: List of criteria for windows that should be opened in a floating mode.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.i3.config.floating.modifier

options.description: Whether to show floating window titlebars.
options.default:
''
  true for state version ≥ 23.05
  config.gaps == null for state version < 23.05
''

An attribute set that assigns a key press to an action using a key symbol.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.xmonad.config
options.description:
The configuration file to be used for xmonad. This must be
an absolute path or `null` in which case
{file}`~/.xmonad/xmonad.hs` will not be managed
by Home Manager.

If this option is set to a non-`null` value,
recompilation of xmonad outside of Home Manager (e.g. via
{command}`xmonad --recompile`) will fail.
options.example:
pkgs.writeText "xmonad.hs" ''
  import XMonad
  main = xmonad defaultConfig
      { terminal    = "urxvt"
      , modMask     = mod4Mask
      , borderWidth = 3
      }
''

options.title: xsession.windowManager.xmonad.enable
options.description: Whether to enable xmonad window manager.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.xmonad.enableContribAndExtras
options.description: Enable xmonad-{contrib,extras} in xmonad.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.xmonad.extraPackages
options.description:
Extra packages available to GHC when rebuilding xmonad. The
value must be a function which receives the attribute set
defined in {var}`haskellPackages` as the sole
argument.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.xmonad.haskellPackages
options.description:
The {var}`haskellPackages` used to build xmonad
and other packages. This can be used to change the GHC
version used to build xmonad and the packages listed in
{var}`extraPackages`.

options.title: xsession.windowManager.xmonad.libFiles
options.description:
Additional files that will be saved in
{file}`~/.xmonad/lib/` and included in the configuration
build. The keys are the file names while the values are paths to the
contents of the files.
